artigos.titulo: Wat3R: Underwater 3D Geometry Learning without Annotations
artigos.resumo: Estimating 3D geometry in underwater environments presents unique challenges due to light attenuation, scattering, and the absence of large-scale, high-quality 3D annotations. Pioneering methods rely on massive dense annotations that are impractical in underwater settings. In this paper, we propose Wat3R, a cross-domain semi-supervised learning framework designed to adapt feed-forward 3D reconstruction models from air to underwater scenes. Uniquely, our method eliminates the need for any annotated underwater data following a teacher-student architecture, that learns robust geometry representations merely on abundant unlabeled real underwater video footage. We also design a cross-view consistency loss that leverages geometric cues from other views to compensate for the information degradation in the current view caused by water attenuation and scattering. Furthermore, considering the lack of comprehensive evaluation benchmarks, we construct Water3D, a diverse dataset covering various water bodies and underwater scenarios, designed for geometric task evaluation. Experimental results demonstrate that Wat3R outperforms current state-of-the-art methods in underwater multi-view depth estimation and point cloud reconstruction. The dataset and code are available at https://github.com/LSXI7/Wat3R .
artigos.titulo_en: Wat3R: Underwater 3D Geometry Learning without Annotations
artigos.titulo_pt: Wat3R: Aprendizado de Geometria 3D Subaquática sem Anotações
artigos.resumo_executivo_en: Wat3R is a cross-domain semi-supervised learning framework that adapts 3D reconstruction models from air to underwater scenes without requiring annotated data. The method employs a teacher-student architecture trained on abundant unlabeled real underwater video footage and incorporates a cross-view consistency loss to compensate for information degradation caused by light attenuation and scattering. The authors also introduce Water3D, a diverse dataset for evaluating geometric tasks in underwater environments.
artigos.resumo_executivo_pt: Wat3R é um framework de aprendizado semi-supervisionado cross-domain que adapta modelos de reconstrução 3D de ambientes aéreos para cenas subaquáticas, eliminando a necessidade de dados anotados. O método utiliza arquitetura professor-aluno treinada em vídeos reais subaquáticos não-rotulados e incorpora uma perda de consistência cross-view para compensar a degradação causada por atenuação de luz e espalhamento. Os autores também introduzem Water3D, um dataset diverso para avaliação de tarefas geométricas em ambientes aquáticos.
artigos.justificativa: O trabalho aborda um problema prático crítico em aplicações subaquáticas (inspeção de infraestrutura, exploração marinha, robótica submarina) ao eliminar a necessidade de anotações custosas e fornecer um dataset público para benchmarking, reduzindo significativamente barreiras de implementação em ambientes reais.

artigos.titulo: ZipDepth: Bringing Lightweight Zero-Shot Monocular Depth Anywhere, on Any Device
artigos.resumo: Monocular depth estimation has seen remarkable progress through foundation models achieving robust zero-shot generalization, yet their computational demands place them far beyond the reach of embedded and mobile platforms. Lightweight alternatives exist, but have been developed almost exclusively within single-domain, self-supervised paradigms, failing silently under domain shift. We present ZipDepth, a compact monocular depth network that bridges this gap by combining an efficient reparameterizable encoder-decoder with large-scale knowledge distillation from a foundation model over a large multi-domain training set. Comprising just 6.1M parameters, ZipDepth runs at real-time rates from server GPUs to power-constrained devices, achieving the best trade-off between zero-shot accuracy and deployment efficiency among lightweight models across five benchmarks, taking a significant step towards the accuracy of foundation models with 50x more parameters.
artigos.titulo_en: ZipDepth: Bringing Lightweight Zero-Shot Monocular Depth Anywhere, on Any Device
artigos.titulo_pt: ZipDepth: Levando Estimativa de Profundidade Monocular Leve e Zero-Shot para Qualquer Lugar, em Qualquer Dispositivo
artigos.resumo_executivo_en: ZipDepth is a compact monocular depth estimation network with only 6.1M parameters that combines an efficient encoder-decoder with knowledge distillation from foundation models across multiple domains. The model achieves real-time execution from server GPUs to power-constrained devices while maintaining robust zero-shot generalization. ZipDepth delivers the best trade-off between zero-shot accuracy and deployment efficiency among lightweight models, approaching the performance of foundation models with 50x more parameters.
artigos.resumo_executivo_pt: ZipDepth é uma rede compacta de estimativa de profundidade monocular com apenas 6.1M parâmetros que combina um encoder-decoder eficiente com destilação de conhecimento de modelos foundation em múltiplos domínios. O modelo alcança execução em tempo real em GPUs de servidor até dispositivos com restrições de energia, mantendo generalização zero-shot robusta. ZipDepth oferece o melhor equilíbrio entre precisão zero-shot e eficiência de implantação entre modelos leves, aproximando-se da precisão de modelos foundation com 50x mais parâmetros.
artigos.justificativa: ZipDepth viabiliza a implantação de estimativa de profundidade zero-shot em dispositivos móveis e embarcados, abrindo oportunidades comerciais em aplicações de visão computacional em tempo real com restrições de recursos, como robótica, smartphones e IoT, mantendo precisão competitiva com modelos muito maiores.

artigos.titulo: LongE2V: Long-Horizon Event-based Video Reconstruction, Prediction, and Frame Interpolation with Video Diffusion Models
artigos.resumo: Recovering high-quality video from sparse event streams is a challenging task. Regression methods often blur textures, while existing generative models struggle with long-term stability. We propose LongE2V, a novel approach that leverages pre-trained video diffusion priors to jointly handle event-based video reconstruction, prediction, and frame interpolation. By fine-tuning a foundational video model, our approach achieves high data efficiency and superior perceptual quality. We introduce Autoregressive Unrolling and Adaptive Context Switching to mitigate temporal drift in extremely long sequences. We also propose Reencoding Alignment with Cross Residual Correction to ensure precise bidirectional consistency during frame interpolation. Furthermore, Event Voxel Density Augmentation ensures robustness across varying sensor resolutions. Extensive experiments on real-world benchmarks demonstrate that LongE2V outperforms state-of-the-art methods across all three tasks, exhibiting exceptional temporal coherence and zero-shot generalization. Project page: https://cdfan0627.github.io/LongE2V-page/
artigos.titulo_en: LongE2V: Long-Horizon Event-based Video Reconstruction, Prediction, and Frame Interpolation with Video Diffusion Models
artigos.titulo_pt: LongE2V: Reconstrução, Predição e Interpolação de Quadros em Vídeos Baseados em Eventos de Longo Horizonte com Modelos de Difusão de Vídeo
artigos.resumo_executivo_en: LongE2V is a novel approach leveraging pre-trained video diffusion priors to jointly handle event-based video reconstruction, prediction, and frame interpolation from sparse event streams. The method introduces Autoregressive Unrolling, Adaptive Context Switching, and Reencoding Alignment to ensure temporal coherence in extremely long sequences and precise bidirectional consistency. Extensive experiments on real-world benchmarks demonstrate superior performance across all three tasks with exceptional zero-shot generalization.
artigos.resumo_executivo_pt: LongE2V é uma abordagem inovadora que utiliza modelos de difusão de vídeo pré-treinados para reconstrução, predição e interpolação de vídeos a partir de fluxos de eventos esparsos. O método introduz técnicas como Autoregressive Unrolling, Adaptive Context Switching e Reencoding Alignment para garantir coerência temporal em sequências extremamente longas e consistência bidirecional precisa. Experimentos em benchmarks do mundo real demonstram desempenho superior em todas as três tarefas com excelente generalização zero-shot.
artigos.justificativa: A reconstrução de vídeo de alta qualidade a partir de sensores de eventos tem aplicações críticas em visão computacional de baixa latência, vigilância, robótica e sistemas autônomos, oferecendo eficiência energética superior aos métodos tradicionais de câmera.

artigos.titulo: Geometry and Gradient-based Partitioning for Panoramic Outdoor Reconstruction
artigos.resumo: Scaling 3D Gaussian Splatting (3DGS) to large outdoor scenes is costly in both data acquisition and computation. Adopting panoramic images with equirectangular projection (ERP) can reduce capture effort via their full $360^{\circ}$ field of view, yet the resulting omnipresent visibility invalidates existing partitioning strategies that rely on local camera frustums, causing block-wise optimization to degenerate into global training. Thus, we propose PanoLOG, a two-stage coarse-to-fine framework equipped with a Geometry and Gradient-based Partitioning Strategy tailored for large-scale panoramic 3DGS reconstruction. In the global coarse stage, PanoLOG leverages sky-sphere modeling and panoramic monocular depth supervision for reliable geometry, while in the refinement stage, G$^2$PS builds adaptive bounding volumes via parallax-driven uncertainty and assigns cameras via gradient-based importance scoring. Furthermore, we construct Pano360, the first benchmark on large-scale panoramic dataset for outdoor scene reconstruction. Extensive experiments demonstrate that G$^2$PS achieves state-of-the-art rendering quality while maintaining scalable, block-parallel training. Our models, training code, and dataset are publicly available.
artigos.titulo_en: Geometry and Gradient-based Partitioning for Panoramic Outdoor Reconstruction
artigos.titulo_pt: Particionamento Baseado em Geometria e Gradiente para Reconstrução Panorâmica ao Ar Livre
artigos.resumo_executivo_en: The paper introduces PanoLOG, a 3D reconstruction framework for large-scale panoramic scenes using 3D Gaussian Splatting with equirectangular images. It proposes an innovative geometry and gradient-based partitioning strategy (G²PS) enabling efficient parallel optimization while overcoming limitations of conventional methods. The work includes a new benchmark (Pano360) and demonstrates state-of-the-art rendering quality with scalable training.
artigos.resumo_executivo_pt: O artigo apresenta PanoLOG, um framework de reconstrução 3D para cenas panorâmicas em larga escala utilizando 3D Gaussian Splatting com imagens equirretangulares. A solução propõe uma estratégia inovadora de particionamento baseada em geometria e gradientes (G²PS) que permite otimização paralela eficiente, superando limitações de métodos convencionais. O trabalho inclui um novo benchmark (Pano360) e demonstra qualidade de renderização estado-da-arte com treinamento escalável.
artigos.justificativa: A solução aborda desafios críticos de reconstrução 3D em larga escala com aplicações diretas em mapeamento urbano, documentação de patrimônio, produção de conteúdo imersivo e realidade virtual, reduzindo significativamente custos de aquisição de dados e computação.

artigos.titulo: UniClawBench: A Universal Benchmark for Proactive Agents on Real-World Tasks
artigos.resumo: The rapid development of large language models and multimodal large language models has accelerated the emergence of proactive agents capable of operating everyday tools and assisting users in real-world environments. However, existing benchmarks struggle to evaluate such agents effectively, as they often rely on sandboxed environments and single-turn evaluation paradigms. Moreover, their scenario-based task taxonomies mix multiple model capabilities within the same task category, making it difficult to identify the root causes of agent failures. To address these limitations, we introduce UniClawBench, the first capability-driven benchmark designed to evaluate proactive agents in dynamic, real-world settings. UniClawBench is built around five foundational model capabilities: Skill Usage, Exploration, Long-Context Reasoning, Multimodal Understanding, and Cross-Platform Coordination. Based on these capabilities, we design 400 bilingual real-world tasks. Unlike previous benchmarks that rely on static, pre-recorded answers, our benchmark evaluates agents in live Docker containers using fine-grained, step-by-step completion checkpoints. Furthermore, we design a closed-loop evaluation strategy comprising an executor agent, a hidden supervisor agent, and a user agent to simulate realistic multi-turn human feedback without leaking grading criteria. To disentangle base model capabilities from framework-level design choices, we evaluate state-of-the-art models under multiple agent frameworks. Through comprehensive comparisons across both models and frameworks, we show how base model capabilities and agent framework designs jointly shape performance in real-world environments. To facilitate future research, we make our benchmark and code publicly available at https://github.com/HKU-MMLab/UniClawBench.
artigos.titulo_en: UniClawBench: A Universal Benchmark for Proactive Agents on Real-World Tasks
artigos.titulo_pt: UniClawBench: Um Benchmark Universal para Agentes Proativos em Tarefas do Mundo Real
artigos.resumo_executivo_en: UniClawBench is the first capability-driven benchmark for evaluating proactive agents in dynamic real-world environments, built around five foundational model capabilities: Skill Usage, Exploration, Long-Context Reasoning, Multimodal Understanding, and Cross-Platform Coordination. The benchmark comprises 400 bilingual real-world tasks evaluated in live Docker containers with fine-grained step-by-step checkpoints, employing a closed-loop evaluation strategy with executor, hidden supervisor, and user agents to simulate realistic human feedback. Results demonstrate how base model capabilities and agent framework design choices jointly shape performance in real-world settings.
artigos.resumo_executivo_pt: UniClawBench é o primeiro benchmark orientado por capacidades para avaliar agentes proativos em ambientes dinâmicos do mundo real, baseado em cinco capacidades fundamentais: Uso de Habilidades, Exploração, Raciocínio em Contexto Longo, Compreensão Multimodal e Coordenação Multiplataforma. O benchmark inclui 400 tarefas bilíngues avaliadas em contêineres Docker ao vivo com pontos de verificação granulares, e utiliza uma estratégia de avaliação em loop fechado com agentes executor, supervisor oculto e usuário para simular feedback humano realista. Os resultados demonstram como as capacidades do modelo base e as escolhas de design do framework de agentes moldam conjuntamente o desempenho em ambientes reais.
artigos.justificativa: O benchmark aborda a avaliação crítica de agentes proativos em ambientes reais, fornecendo uma metodologia robusta para avaliar modelos de linguagem e multimodais em tarefas práticas. Isso é altamente relevante para empresas que desenvolvem ou implementam sistemas de agentes autônomos, pois oferece métricas padronizadas e confiáveis para medir desempenho em cenários do mundo real.

artigos.titulo: OPSD-V: On-Policy Self-Distillation for Post-Training Few-Step Autoregressive Video Generators
artigos.resumo: We propose OPSD-V, an on-policy self-distillation paradigm for post-training few-step autoregressive (AR) video diffusion models. Existing few-step AR video generators can produce long videos with low latency, but still suffer from error accumulation and weakened motion dynamics during long autoregressive rollout. OPSD-V reduces long-horizon degradation while preserving the original few-step inference path. The key idea is to introduce real long-video data as temporal context during training and use it to provide dense trajectory-level supervision. Specifically, the student follows the exact inference-time rollout, generating each chunk conditioned on its own previously generated KV cache. In parallel, the teacher is evaluated at the same student-visited denoising states, but uses a cleaner AR-consistent temporal cache in which older history can be replaced by real-video context. This provides dense denoising-level corrective targets under on-policy AR cache dynamics, without changing the sampler, number of denoising steps, or inference-time cache mechanism. We apply OPSD-V to representative few-step AR video models, including Self-Forcing and LongLive. Experiments show consistent improvements in visual quality, motion dynamics, and VBenchLong scores. A user study with 10 participants comparing 20 video pairs shows that OPSD-V is preferred over the base models in 66.0% of overall-preference judgments (82.5% excluding ties).
artigos.titulo_en: OPSD-V: On-Policy Self-Distillation for Post-Training Few-Step Autoregressive Video Generators
artigos.titulo_pt: OPSD-V: Auto-Destilação em Política para Pós-Treinamento de Geradores de Vídeo Autorregressivos com Poucos Passos
artigos.resumo_executivo_en: OPSD-V is an on-policy self-distillation paradigm for optimizing few-step autoregressive video diffusion models, reducing long-horizon degradation while preserving inference efficiency. The method introduces long-video data as temporal context during training, using a teacher that accesses student-visited denoising states with cleaner temporal cache to provide dense corrective supervision. Experiments demonstrate consistent improvements in visual quality, motion dynamics, and VBenchLong scores, with 66% user preference over base models.
artigos.resumo_executivo_pt: OPSD-V é um paradigma de auto-destilação em política para otimizar modelos de difusão de vídeo autorregressivos com poucos passos, reduzindo a degradação em horizontes longos enquanto preserva a eficiência de inferência. O método introduz dados de vídeo longo como contexto temporal durante o treinamento, usando um professor que acessa estados de denoising visitados pelo aluno com cache temporal mais limpo para fornecer supervisão corretiva densa. Experimentos demonstram melhorias consistentes em qualidade visual, dinâmica de movimento e scores VBenchLong, com preferência do usuário de 66% em comparação com modelos base.
artigos.justificativa: O trabalho aborda um problema crítico em geração de vídeo em tempo real (poucos passos de inferência) com aplicações diretas em produção de conteúdo, streaming e criação de mídia, oferecendo melhorias mensuráveis em qualidade e dinâmica de movimento sem aumentar latência.

artigos.titulo: Enhancing In-context Panoramic Generation via Geometric-aware Pretraining
artigos.resumo: In this work, we present Canvas360, a two-stage framework for in-context panoramic generation that combines geometry-aware pretraining with downstream task-specific fine-tuning. To address the lack of large-scale, high-quality training data tailored to in-context panoramic tasks, we propose Canvas360Dataset, a collection of 1M high-quality paired panoramic samples for style transfer, inpainting, outpainting, and editing, enabling effective supervision across diverse in-context generation scenarios. On the modeling side, Canvas360 enhances text-to-panorama generation through parallel depth generation, velocity circular padding, and similarity loss regularization, enabling the model to learn geometry-aware representations, capture object distortion details, and improve geometric consistency and global coherence. Furthermore, empowered by strong panoramic priors, Canvas360 enables a unified in-context panoramic generation framework that supports diverse downstream tasks via token-level concatenation, surpassing prior methods in both task coverage and modeling flexibility. Extensive experiments show that Canvas360 improves panoramic image fidelity, achieving particularly strong performance on the panorama-specific FAED metric and competitive or leading results across the reported quantitative evaluations. More information can be found on our project page: https://zry000.github.io/Canvas360/
artigos.titulo_en: Enhancing In-context Panoramic Generation via Geometric-aware Pretraining
artigos.titulo_pt: Aprimorando a Geração Panorâmica em Contexto através de Pré-treinamento Consciente de Geometria
artigos.resumo_executivo_en: Canvas360 is a two-stage framework for in-context panoramic generation combining geometry-aware pretraining with task-specific fine-tuning. The work introduces Canvas360Dataset, a collection of 1M high-quality panoramic samples for style transfer, inpainting, outpainting, and editing. The model achieves superior geometric consistency and global coherence through parallel depth generation, velocity circular padding, and similarity loss regularization.
artigos.resumo_executivo_pt: Canvas360 é um framework de dois estágios para geração panorâmica em contexto que combina pré-treinamento consciente de geometria com ajuste fino específico de tarefas. O trabalho introduz Canvas360Dataset, uma coleção de 1M amostras panorâmicas de alta qualidade para transferência de estilo, inpainting, outpainting e edição. O modelo alcança consistência geométrica superior e coerência global através de geração paralela de profundidade, padding circular de velocidade e regularização de perda de similaridade.
artigos.justificativa: Canvas360 oferece uma solução prática para geração e edição de imagens panorâmicas com aplicações diretas em conteúdo imersivo, realidade virtual, fotografia 360° e criação de conteúdo visual em larga escala, com dataset público de 1M amostras facilitando adoção comercial.

artigos.titulo: OpenCoF: Learning to Reason Through Video Generation
artigos.resumo: Reasoning has become a core capability for large models, especially when reliable decisions require understanding logical consequences. Recent video generation models offer a reasoning path distinct from previous Chain-of-Thought (CoT): reasoning can unfold through temporally connected frames, known as Chain-of-Frame (CoF) reasoning. However, existing video generators are primarily trained on general video corpora, still lacking diverse supervision and dedicated designs for CoF reasoning. To address this gap, we introduce OpenCoF, a framework comprising the OpenCoF-17K dataset, a reasoning video dataset spanning 11 task families, and Wan-CoF, a fine-tuned video model for studying whether diverse temporal supervision improves CoF behavior. Across four video reasoning benchmarks, Wan-CoF achieves considerable gains over the Wan2.2-I2V-A14B baseline. Building on this, we empirically explore more advanced designs for CoF capabilities, i.e., equipping the model with visual and textual reasoning tokens. This mechanism respectively captures low-level visual cues and high-level semantic priors for spatial and temporal reasoning. Through performance comparisons and attention analysis, we examine how these tokens contribute across model depth, denoising steps, space, and time. Our results suggest that stronger video reasoning requires both broad temporal supervision and explicit mechanisms for organizing intermediate reasoning state. We open-source the dataset, model, and code to facilitate future research on reasoning-oriented video generation.
artigos.titulo_en: OpenCoF: Learning to Reason Through Video Generation
artigos.titulo_pt: OpenCoF: Aprendendo a Raciocinar Através da Geração de Vídeo
artigos.resumo_executivo_en: The paper introduces OpenCoF, a framework comprising a 17K reasoning video dataset and the Wan-CoF model to study how diverse temporal supervision improves Chain-of-Frame reasoning through temporally connected frames. The model incorporates visual and textual reasoning tokens to capture low-level visual cues and high-level semantic priors, achieving significant gains across four video reasoning benchmarks. The authors open-source the dataset, model, and code to advance research on reasoning-oriented video generation.
artigos.resumo_executivo_pt: O artigo apresenta OpenCoF, um framework que integra um dataset de 17K vídeos de raciocínio e o modelo Wan-CoF para estudar como supervisão temporal diversa melhora o raciocínio através de frames conectados temporalmente (Chain-of-Frame). O modelo incorpora tokens de raciocínio visual e textual para capturar pistas visuais de baixo nível e priors semânticos de alto nível, demonstrando ganhos significativos em quatro benchmarks de raciocínio em vídeo. Os autores disponibilizam dataset, modelo e código para pesquisas futuras em geração de vídeo orientada ao raciocínio.
artigos.justificativa: O trabalho endereça a capacidade crítica de raciocínio em modelos de geração de vídeo, com aplicações diretas em análise de conteúdo visual, tomada de decisão baseada em vídeo e sistemas de IA explicáveis. A disponibilização de dataset e modelo de código aberto acelera a adoção comercial em indústrias que dependem de análise visual e raciocínio temporal.

artigos.titulo: Ideas Have Genomes: Benchmarking Scientific Lineage Reasoning and Lineage-Grounded Idea Generation
artigos.resumo: Scientific ideas rarely start from a blank page. They inherit mechanisms, repair known limitations, and recombine pieces of earlier work, much like biological genomes. Current benchmarks still say little about whether AI systems can follow this inheritance structure. We present IdeaGene-Bench (IG-Bench), a benchmark for scientific lineage reasoning and lineage-grounded idea generation. IG-Bench is organized around the IdeaGene framework: each paper or proposal is represented as a set of minimal, typed, evidence-grounded Idea Genome objects, and a GenomeDiff aligns these objects to record inheritance, mutation, loss, external import, and novel insertion under six operational evolutionary dynamics. The benchmark contains 1,961 golden lineage traces, 1,085 curated Idea Genome objects, and 920 pairwise GenomeDiff records across 10 scientific domains. It supports two evaluations. IG-Exam (42 task types, 1,029 instances) tests closed-form lineage reasoning across Idea Genome abstraction, inheritance tracing, evolutionary reasoning, and lineage verification. IG-Arena evaluates generation with a lineage-conditioned Population-Evolution Score(PES), asking whether a proposal can be inserted as a coherent descendant of a given lineage population: it should inherit the right Idea Genome objects, vary meaningfully from nearby work, and offer selection value for future research. Experiments on 14 LLM-based scientists expose a compositional bottleneck. The strongest system reaches only 27.3% exact accuracy on lineage reasoning, and structured lineage context reshuffles system rankings rather than helping every participant uniformly.
artigos.titulo_en: Ideas Have Genomes: Benchmarking Scientific Lineage Reasoning and Lineage-Grounded Idea Generation
artigos.titulo_pt: Ideias Têm Genomas: Avaliação de Raciocínio sobre Linhagem Científica e Geração de Ideias Fundamentadas em Linhagem
artigos.resumo_executivo_en: The paper introduces IdeaGene-Bench, a benchmark for evaluating AI systems' ability to reason about scientific lineage and generate ideas grounded in inheritance patterns. The IdeaGene framework represents papers as typed, evidence-grounded Idea Genome objects, with GenomeDiff recording evolutionary dynamics such as inheritance, mutation, and innovation. The benchmark contains 1,961 lineage traces, 1,085 curated objects, and 920 GenomeDiff records, revealing a compositional bottleneck where the strongest LLM system achieves only 27.3% exact accuracy on lineage reasoning.
artigos.resumo_executivo_pt: O artigo apresenta IdeaGene-Bench, um benchmark para avaliar o raciocínio de sistemas de IA sobre linhagem científica e geração de ideias fundamentadas em herança. O framework IdeaGene representa papers como objetos de Genoma de Ideias tipados e rastreáveis, com GenomeDiff registrando dinâmicas evolutivas como herança, mutação e inovação. O benchmark contém 1.961 traços de linhagem, 1.085 objetos curados e 920 registros de GenomeDiff, revelando um gargalo composicional onde o melhor sistema LLM atinge apenas 27,3% de acurácia exata em raciocínio de linhagem.
artigos.justificativa: O benchmark aborda um desafio crítico para sistemas de IA generativa: compreender e gerar ideias científicas respeitando sua linhagem evolutiva. Isso é altamente relevante para aplicações em pesquisa assistida por IA, descoberta científica automatizada e sistemas de recomendação de inovação em ambientes corporativos e acadêmicos.

artigos.titulo: Score Accuracy Along the Forward Diffusion Does Not Certify Numerical Stability in Diffusion Sampling
artigos.resumo: Score matching controls average error under the forward marginals, but a discretized reverse-time sampler evaluates the learned score along its own trajectory. We show that small forward-marginal error does not guarantee numerical stability. We construct a single smooth score field with arbitrarily small forward-marginal $L^2$ error. The learned reverse-time process is nonexplosive, has moments of every order, and can be arbitrarily close to the exact reverse-time process in path-space total variation. Yet its Euler--Maruyama discretizations converge in probability while every positive moment diverges. Thus weak convergence can hold even though every Wasserstein distance $W_p$, $p\ge1$, diverges.   The same failure can occur within one fixed finite neural architecture. We construct a family of bounded, globally Lipschitz denoisers for which both the forward-marginal error and the path-space total variation distance tend to zero, while their Euler--Maruyama endpoints diverge in every $W_p$. For compactly supported data, we also give a simple positive result. Projecting the learned denoiser onto a known bounded closed convex set containing the support preserves pointwise accuracy, gives grid-uniform moment bounds, and yields Wasserstein convergence under mild local regularity. Experiments with a small fixed DiT-style network show large growth along rare numerical trajectories and its suppression by denoiser projection, while overall trajectory errors remain small.
artigos.titulo_en: Score Accuracy Along the Forward Diffusion Does Not Certify Numerical Stability in Diffusion Sampling
artigos.titulo_pt: Precisão da Pontuação ao Longo da Difusão Direta Não Certifica Estabilidade Numérica na Amostragem por Difusão
artigos.resumo_executivo_en: The work demonstrates that small forward-marginal errors in diffusion models do not guarantee numerical stability in discretized samplers, constructing examples where weak convergence occurs despite divergence in all Wasserstein distances. A positive solution is proposed through denoiser projection onto closed convex sets, preserving pointwise accuracy and Wasserstein convergence. Experiments with DiT networks confirm growth along rare numerical trajectories and its suppression by denoiser projection.
artigos.resumo_executivo_pt: O trabalho demonstra que pequenos erros de marginal direto em modelos de difusão não garantem estabilidade numérica em amostradores discretizados, construindo exemplos onde convergência fraca ocorre apesar de divergência em todas as distâncias de Wasserstein. Propõe uma solução positiva através da projeção do denoiser em conjuntos convexos fechados, preservando precisão pontual e convergência de Wasserstein. Experimentos com redes DiT confirmam o crescimento em trajetórias numéricas raras e sua supressão pela projeção.
artigos.justificativa: O trabalho aborda questões fundamentais de estabilidade numérica em modelos de difusão, tecnologia crítica para aplicações generativas em produção. Os resultados teóricos e soluções propostas são relevantes para garantir confiabilidade em sistemas de amostragem generativa em escala.

artigos.titulo: MulTTiPop: A Multitrack Transcription Dataset for Pop Music
artigos.resumo: We present MulTTiPop, a dataset of pop music segments and their associated multitrack MIDI recordings for the evaluation of automatic music transcription models. MulTTiPop contains 572 segments of popular music totaling 3.5 hours of audio, and contains songs from diverse genres and decades from the 1930s to 2000s. To collect this dataset, we perform metadata-based matching on song segments from the Lakh MIDI and TheoryTab datasets, manually identify an anchor beat between the audio and MIDI, then use beat tracking on the audio and warp the MIDI to match its tempo and timing. We evaluate state-of-the-art automatic music transcription models on MulTTiPop and find substantial room for improvement, with the best model achieving 38% Onset F1. More details and sound examples of MulTTiPop are available at https://gclef-cmu.org/multtipop.
artigos.titulo_en: MulTTiPop: A Multitrack Transcription Dataset for Pop Music
artigos.titulo_pt: MulTTiPop: Um Dataset de Transcrição Multifaixa para Música Pop
artigos.resumo_executivo_en: MulTTiPop is a dataset of 572 pop music segments (3.5 hours of audio) with associated multitrack MIDI recordings for evaluating automatic music transcription models. The dataset spans diverse genres and decades (1930s-2000s), constructed through metadata-based matching between Lakh MIDI and TheoryTab datasets, with manual beat alignment and tempo adjustment. Evaluation of state-of-the-art models reveals substantial room for improvement, with the best model achieving 38% Onset F1.
artigos.resumo_executivo_pt: MulTTiPop é um dataset de 572 segmentos de música pop (3,5 horas de áudio) com gravações MIDI multifaixa associadas para avaliação de modelos de transcrição automática de música. O dataset abrange diversos gêneros e décadas (1930s-2000s), construído através de correspondência baseada em metadados entre os datasets Lakh MIDI e TheoryTab, com alinhamento manual de batida e ajuste de tempo. A avaliação de modelos estado-da-arte revela espaço substancial para melhoria, com o melhor modelo alcançando 38% de F1 em Onset.
artigos.justificativa: O dataset contribui para o avanço de modelos de transcrição automática de música, com aplicações potenciais em produção musical, análise de áudio e ferramentas criativas para a indústria de música. A identificação de limitações dos modelos atuais (38% F1) abre oportunidades para desenvolvimento de soluções comerciais mais robustas.

artigos.titulo: SLORR: Simple and Efficient In-Training Low-Rank Regularization
artigos.resumo: Low-rank factorization is widely used to compress neural networks, but modern models are often not naturally amenable to aggressive factorization without significant accuracy loss. Existing training-time low-rank regularizers can improve compressibility, but they often require SVDs of large weight matrices, modify the model architecture (introducing additional trainable parameters), or rely on stateful cached quantities. To address these limitations, we introduce SLORR, a simple, stateless, and architecture-preserving framework for in-training low-rank regularization, instantiated with two main variants based on the Hoyer sparsity metric and the nuclear norm. SLORR directly regularizes the original weight matrices using GPU-friendly approximations for the forward and backward passes of the regularizers, for which we provide approximation guarantees. We first evaluate SLORR on ImageNet-1K across short-horizon continued training of ResNet-50, ViT-B/16, and ViT-L/16, and pretraining of ResNet-18, where SLORR induces compressibility while introducing less than 8% training overhead. We further evaluate SLORR-Hoyer in LLM pretraining at 135M and 560M scales: SLORR-trained compressed models preserve performance substantially better than unregularized models while adding less than 1% average training overhead.
artigos.titulo_en: SLORR: Simple and Efficient In-Training Low-Rank Regularization
artigos.titulo_pt: SLORR: Regularização de Baixo Rank Simples e Eficiente Durante o Treinamento
artigos.resumo_executivo_en: SLORR is a stateless and efficient framework for low-rank regularization during neural network training that improves compressibility without modifying model architecture. The method uses GPU-friendly approximations based on Hoyer sparsity and nuclear norm metrics, with training overhead below 8% for vision models and less than 1% for language models. The approach preserves performance of compressed models substantially better than unregularized methods, applicable to both continued training and pretraining scenarios.
artigos.resumo_executivo_pt: SLORR é um framework stateless e eficiente para regularização de baixo rank durante o treinamento de redes neurais, que melhora a compressibilidade sem modificar a arquitetura do modelo. O método utiliza aproximações GPU-friendly baseadas em métricas de esparsidade de Hoyer e norma nuclear, com overhead de treinamento inferior a 8% em visão computacional e menos de 1% em modelos de linguagem. A abordagem preserva o desempenho de modelos comprimidos significativamente melhor que métodos não regularizados, sendo aplicável tanto em treinamento contínuo quanto em pré-treinamento.
artigos.justificativa: SLORR oferece uma solução prática e eficiente para compressão de modelos neurais com overhead mínimo, reduzindo custos computacionais e de armazenamento em produção, o que é crítico para deployment de modelos em larga escala, especialmente LLMs.

artigos.titulo: Using AI-based Learning Assistants in Higher Education: A Large-Scale Descriptive Analysis
artigos.resumo: In this study, we present a large-scale descriptive analysis of the use of an AI-based learning assistant (Syntea) in higher education. Based on objective log data from 77,543 students enrolled in distance studies, we examine usage patterns across gender, age group, study cluster, degree, and study mode. To date, existing research on educational chatbots has largely relied on comparatively small samples and self-reported survey data, while large-scale evidence on actual usage behavior remains limited. Our findings show that Syntea is already embedded in the study routines of many learners, but that usage differs across demographic and structural contexts. By identifying these patterns, our study provides an empirical basis for the further development of AI-based learning support and contributes a large-scale analysis of educational chatbot usage in higher education.
artigos.titulo_en: Using AI-based Learning Assistants in Higher Education: A Large-Scale Descriptive Analysis
artigos.titulo_pt: Uso de Assistentes de Aprendizagem Baseados em IA no Ensino Superior: Uma Análise Descritiva em Larga Escala
artigos.resumo_executivo_en: Large-scale descriptive analysis of an AI-based learning assistant (Syntea) usage in higher education using objective log data from 77,543 distance learning students. The study examines usage patterns across demographic and structural contexts, revealing that the assistant is embedded in many learners' study routines but with significant variations. Findings provide empirical evidence for further development of AI-based educational support systems.
artigos.resumo_executivo_pt: Análise descritiva em larga escala do uso de um assistente de aprendizagem baseado em IA (Syntea) em educação superior, com dados objetivos de 77.543 estudantes em educação a distância. O estudo examina padrões de uso por gênero, faixa etária, cluster de estudo, grau e modo de estudo, revelando que o assistente já está integrado nas rotinas de aprendizagem de muitos alunos, mas com variações significativas entre contextos demográficos e estruturais. Os achados fornecem uma base empírica para o desenvolvimento futuro de suporte de aprendizagem baseado em IA em educação superior.
artigos.justificativa: O estudo fornece evidências empíricas em larga escala sobre o uso real de assistentes educacionais baseados em IA, informando decisões estratégicas sobre implementação e otimização de tecnologias de aprendizagem em instituições de ensino superior. Os insights sobre padrões de uso diferenciados por demografia e contexto estrutural são críticos para personalização e adoção efetiva de soluções educacionais.

artigos.titulo: Dimensionality Reduction Meets Network Science: Sensemaking on UMAP's kNN Graph
artigos.resumo: While UMAP is widely used for exploring high-dimensional data, typical workflows focus on its lower-dimensional embedding, largely overlooking the rich k-nearest-neighbor (kNN) graph that UMAP constructs internally. This graph encodes the data manifold in its original high-dimensional space, before the distortion that UMAP's 2D projection introduces. We demonstrate the untapped potential of this internal representation, showing how standard graph algorithms applied to this graph enhance data sensemaking: (1) PageRank identifies representative data points, (2) k-core decomposition reveals dense core regions versus sparse periphery, and (3) clustering coefficient detects tight-knit neighborhoods with highly-similar data points. Through quantitative and qualitative evaluation on MNIST and Fashion MNIST, we show that these graph-based analyses are not only practical but also competitive with or complementary to purpose-built methods (e.g., k-medoids for exemplar selection, HDBSCAN for density-based clustering).
artigos.titulo_en: Dimensionality Reduction Meets Network Science: Sensemaking on UMAP's kNN Graph
artigos.titulo_pt: Redução de Dimensionalidade Encontra Ciência de Redes: Interpretação do Grafo kNN do UMAP
artigos.resumo_executivo_en: This work explores the potential of the k-nearest-neighbor (kNN) graph constructed internally by UMAP, which encodes the data manifold in the original high-dimensional space. It demonstrates how standard graph algorithms (PageRank, k-core decomposition, and clustering coefficient) applied to this graph enhance data sensemaking and are competitive or complementary to specialized methods like k-medoids and HDBSCAN. Quantitative and qualitative evaluation on MNIST and Fashion MNIST validates the practicality and effectiveness of this approach.
artigos.resumo_executivo_pt: O trabalho explora o potencial do grafo k-vizinhos-mais-próximos (kNN) construído internamente pelo UMAP, que codifica a variedade dos dados no espaço original de alta dimensionalidade. Demonstra como algoritmos de grafo padrão (PageRank, k-core decomposition e clustering coefficient) aplicados a este grafo melhoram a interpretação de dados, sendo competitivos ou complementares a métodos especializados como k-medoids e HDBSCAN. A avaliação quantitativa e qualitativa em MNIST e Fashion MNIST valida a praticidade e efetividade dessa abordagem.
artigos.justificativa: A abordagem oferece métodos práticos e competitivos para análise exploratória de dados de alta dimensionalidade, com aplicações em visualização de dados e descoberta de padrões em datasets reais, complementando ferramentas amplamente utilizadas como UMAP.

artigos.titulo: AUTOPILOT VQA: Benchmarking Vision-Language Models for Incident-Centric Dashcam Understanding
artigos.resumo: Recent advances in Vision-Language Models, Large Language Models, and Multimodal Large Language Models have improved autonomous driving tasks such as scene understanding, decision making, trajectory prediction, and visual question answering. However, evaluating whether these models can reliably reason about safety-critical incidents remains challenging. To address this gap, we present AUTOPILOT-VQA, an incident-centric visual question answering benchmark for dashcam video understanding. The dataset evaluates different systems through structured questions designed around real-world driving incidents and near-incidents. The benchmark covers diverse safety-relevant categories, including weather and lighting conditions, traffic environment, road layout, road surface state, signage, involved entities, accident occurrence, impact location, and avoidability-related reasoning. By requiring models to answer grounded questions about both contextual scene properties and event-level incident details, AUTOPILOT-VQA moves beyond object recognition toward temporally grounded, safety-aware reasoning. The dataset is released as part of the AUTOPILOT CVPR 2026 competition and provides a standardized benchmark for assessing the reliability of autonomous driving systems in different scenarios. Our benchmark support developments for more interpretable, robust, and safety-conscious vision-language systems for real-world autonomous driving.
artigos.titulo_en: AUTOPILOT VQA: Benchmarking Vision-Language Models for Incident-Centric Dashcam Understanding
artigos.titulo_pt: AUTOPILOT VQA: Avaliação de Modelos Visão-Linguagem para Compreensão de Dashcam Centrada em Incidentes
artigos.resumo_executivo_en: The paper introduces AUTOPILOT-VQA, an incident-centric visual question answering benchmark for dashcam video understanding in autonomous driving. The dataset evaluates vision-language models through structured questions about real-world driving incidents, covering safety-critical categories including weather conditions, traffic environment, road layout, and avoidability reasoning. The benchmark advances the development of more interpretable, robust, and safety-conscious vision-language systems for real-world autonomous driving scenarios.
artigos.resumo_executivo_pt: O artigo apresenta AUTOPILOT-VQA, um benchmark de visual question answering centrado em incidentes para compreensão de vídeos de dashcam em direção autônoma. O dataset avalia modelos visão-linguagem através de questões estruturadas sobre incidentes reais de direção, cobrindo categorias críticas de segurança como condições climáticas, ambiente de tráfego, layout de estrada e raciocínio sobre evitabilidade. O benchmark promove o desenvolvimento de sistemas visão-linguagem mais interpretáveis, robustos e conscientes de segurança para direção autônoma em cenários do mundo real.
artigos.justificativa: O benchmark aborda avaliação de segurança crítica em sistemas de direção autônoma, fornecendo uma ferramenta padronizada para avaliar a confiabilidade de modelos visão-linguagem em cenários de incidentes reais, essencial para validação de sistemas autônomos antes do deployment em produção.

artigos.titulo: ARDY: Autoregressive Diffusion with Hybrid Representation for Interactive Human Motion Generation
artigos.resumo: Generating realistic 3D human motions in real-time within interactive applications is key for animation, simulation, and humanoid robotics. While recent offline motion generation approaches offer precise control via text and kinematic constraints, they lack the inference speed required for interactive settings. Conversely, existing online methods enable real-time synthesis but often sacrifice controllability or struggle with complex text semantics and long-horizon goals due to limited context windows. In this work, we introduce ARDY, a streaming generation framework that bridges this gap by enabling high-fidelity motion generation controllable via online text prompts and flexible kinematic constraints. ARDY employs a hybrid representation that combines explicit root features with a latent body embedding, balancing precise trajectory control with efficient generative learning. We propose a two-stage autoregressive transformer denoiser that features variable history context and supports conditioning on flexible, long-horizon kinematic constraints. By training on a large-scale motion capture dataset and being directly conditioned on text labels and kinematic constraints sampled from ground truth poses, ARDY natively learns controllable generation that supports online prompting and flexible long-horizon goals. Extensive evaluations on the HumanML3D benchmark and the large-scale, high-fidelity Bones Rigplay dataset demonstrate ARDY's high motion quality and constraint adherence, validating the efficacy of our key architectural decisions. Finally, we demonstrate the method's practical versatility through an interactive demo featuring dynamic text control, diverse keyframe pose constraints, path following, and interactive locomotion control via mouse and keyboard. Supplementary video results, code, and model releases can be found at https://research.nvidia.com/labs/sil/projects/ardy/.
artigos.titulo_en: ARDY: Autoregressive Diffusion with Hybrid Representation for Interactive Human Motion Generation
artigos.titulo_pt: ARDY: Difusão Autorregressiva com Representação Híbrida para Geração Interativa de Movimento Humano
artigos.resumo_executivo_en: ARDY is a real-time 3D human motion generation framework combining autoregressive diffusion with hybrid representation for interactive applications. The system enables precise control via online text prompts and flexible kinematic constraints, balancing motion quality with computational efficiency. Validated on high-fidelity benchmarks, the method demonstrates practical applicability in animation, simulation, and humanoid robotics.
artigos.resumo_executivo_pt: ARDY é um framework de geração de movimento 3D humano em tempo real que combina difusão autorregressiva com representação híbrida para aplicações interativas. O sistema permite controle preciso via prompts de texto online e restrições cinemáticas flexíveis, equilibrando qualidade de movimento com eficiência computacional. Validado em benchmarks de alta fidelidade, o método demonstra aplicabilidade prática em animação, simulação e robótica humanóide.
artigos.justificativa: O trabalho endereça demandas críticas de indústrias de animação, games e robótica ao viabilizar geração de movimento humano 3D em tempo real com controle interativo, combinando qualidade visual com responsividade necessária para aplicações comerciais. A disponibilização de código e modelos amplifica seu potencial de adoção em produtos e serviços.

artigos.titulo: Workflow as Knowledge: Semantic Persistence for LLM-Mediated Workflows
artigos.resumo: Large language model (LLM) applications increasingly use explicit workflows for tool use, retrieval, branching, checkpointing, and human approval. Existing workflow systems already address many execution concerns. This paper proposes a Lisp-inspired but language-independent conceptual model: symbolic forms, object identity, and live-image thinking are used as explanatory lenses, not implementation commitments. In this model, workflow definitions, workflow instances, inference records, context snapshots, and dependency relations are represented as persistent knowledge objects in a shared knowledge substrate. Its central semantic distinction is between derive and infer: derive is deterministic computation over available state; infer is mediated LLM judgment under declared context and executor-controlled capability policy.   The result is a preliminary conceptual account of semantic persistence: workflows do not merely produce knowledge and leave traces, but can themselves be represented as inspectable, resumable, and reviewable knowledge objects, while formal transition semantics remain future work.
artigos.titulo_en: Workflow as Knowledge: Semantic Persistence for LLM-Mediated Workflows
artigos.titulo_pt: Fluxo de Trabalho como Conhecimento: Persistência Semântica para Fluxos de Trabalho Mediados por LLM
artigos.resumo_executivo_en: The paper proposes a Lisp-inspired conceptual model for representing LLM-mediated workflows as persistent knowledge objects in a shared substrate. The model distinguishes between "derive" (deterministic computation) and "infer" (LLM-mediated judgment), enabling workflow definitions, instances, inference records, and dependencies to be inspectable, resumable, and reviewable. This approach provides a preliminary account of semantic persistence for LLM applications utilizing tools, retrieval, branching, and human approval mechanisms.
artigos.resumo_executivo_pt: O artigo propõe um modelo conceitual inspirado em Lisp para representar fluxos de trabalho mediados por LLM como objetos de conhecimento persistentes em um substrato compartilhado. O modelo distingue entre "derive" (computação determinística) e "infer" (julgamento mediado por LLM), permitindo que definições de fluxo, instâncias, registros de inferência e dependências sejam inspecionáveis, retomáveis e revisáveis. A abordagem oferece uma conta preliminar de persistência semântica para sistemas de aplicações LLM que utilizam ferramentas, recuperação, ramificação e aprovação humana.
artigos.justificativa: A persistência semântica de fluxos de trabalho LLM é crítica para aplicações empresariais que exigem auditoria, rastreabilidade e retomabilidade de processos complexos mediados por IA. O modelo proposto endereça desafios práticos de governança e confiabilidade em sistemas LLM em produção.

artigos.titulo: The Illusion of Equivalency: Statistical Characterization of Quantization Effects in LLMs
artigos.resumo: Post-training quantization is widely used to deploy large language models in resource-constrained settings, yet its evaluation relies almost exclusively on accuracy and perplexity. We show that these metrics fail to capture behavioral changes induced by quantization. We introduce correctness agreement, a decision-level metric that measures overlap in correct predictions between a base model and its quantized variants, independent of absolute accuracy. Across multiple models and quantization schemes from 8-bit to 2-bit, we find that behavioral divergence emerges under moderate quantization even when task performance appears preserved. To explain this effect, we analyze quantization as a structural operator on attention weights and quantify layer-wise distortions using statistical and distributional measures. Our results reveal non-linear breakpoints at low bit-widths and show that query and key projections are consistently more sensitive than value and output projections. These findings expose an illusion of equivalence between base and quantized models and motivate behavioral evaluation beyond conventional performance metrics.
artigos.titulo_en: The Illusion of Equivalency: Statistical Characterization of Quantization Effects in LLMs
artigos.titulo_pt: A Ilusão da Equivalência: Caracterização Estatística dos Efeitos da Quantização em LLMs
artigos.resumo_executivo_en: The paper demonstrates that conventional metrics such as accuracy and perplexity fail to capture behavioral changes induced by post-training quantization of language models. It introduces the "correctness agreement" metric to measure behavioral divergences between base and quantized models, revealing that significant divergences emerge even when task performance appears preserved. The findings expose an illusion of equivalence between models and motivate behavioral evaluation beyond conventional performance metrics.
artigos.resumo_executivo_pt: O artigo demonstra que métricas convencionais como acurácia e perplexidade falham em capturar mudanças comportamentais induzidas pela quantização pós-treinamento de modelos de linguagem. Introduz a métrica "correctness agreement" para medir divergências comportamentais entre modelos base e quantizados, revelando que divergências significativas emergem mesmo quando o desempenho em tarefas aparenta ser preservado. Os resultados expõem uma ilusão de equivalência entre modelos e motivam avaliações comportamentais além de métricas de desempenho convencionais.
artigos.justificativa: A quantização é crítica para deployment de LLMs em ambientes com recursos limitados, e este trabalho fornece insights fundamentais sobre limitações das métricas de avaliação atuais, impactando diretamente decisões de produção e confiabilidade de modelos quantizados em aplicações comerciais.

artigos.titulo: Super Weights in LLMs and the Failure of Selective Training
artigos.resumo: Recent work identified Super Weights, individual parameters whose removal degrades model performance by orders of magnitude. We show that this degradation due to pruning Super Weights does not universally apply to all LLMs. Furthermore, if these parameters are so important, Super Weight-aware training should be effective. We show the opposite. Training Super Weights in isolation (100 to 8,192 parameters) drops accuracy to random-guessing levels on both OLMo-1B and OLMo-7B, and expanding to local neighborhoods of up to 36K parameters provides no improvement. The failure is specific to Super Weight coordinates: training an equal number of randomly chosen positions in the same down_proj layers instead improves over the baseline, so the collapse comes from targeting Super Weights, not from sparsity itself. Vanilla LoRA, updating every position in attention weight matrices through low-rank structure, succeeds with only 0.16% of parameters, and applying the same low-rank update to down_proj succeeds as well. A 10-seed ablation confirms that constraining LoRA updates at positions corresponding to Super Weight coordinates yields statistically indistinguishable results. These findings establish that parameter importance does not imply parameter trainability in isolation, and that effective fine-tuning relies on structured decompositions over entire layers rather than targeting individually important weights.
artigos.titulo_en: Super Weights in LLMs and the Failure of Selective Training
artigos.titulo_pt: Super Pesos em LLMs e o Fracasso do Treinamento Seletivo
artigos.resumo_executivo_en: This work investigates Super Weights in LLMs (parameters whose removal significantly degrades performance) and demonstrates that selective training of these isolated weights fails, reducing accuracy to random-guessing levels. The authors show that parameter importance does not imply parameter trainability in isolation, and that effective fine-tuning requires structured decompositions across entire layers, such as LoRA, rather than optimization of individual weights.
artigos.resumo_executivo_pt: O trabalho investiga Super Pesos em LLMs (parâmetros cuja remoção degrada significativamente o desempenho) e demonstra que treinamento seletivo desses pesos isolados falha, reduzindo a acurácia a níveis aleatórios. Os autores mostram que a importância de um parâmetro não implica sua treinabilidade em isolamento, e que fine-tuning efetivo requer decomposições estruturadas em camadas inteiras, como LoRA, em vez de otimização de pesos individuais.
artigos.justificativa: O trabalho fornece insights críticos para otimização de LLMs em produção, demonstrando que estratégias de treinamento seletivo baseadas em importância de parâmetros são ineficazes, enquanto LoRA com apenas 0,16% dos parâmetros é bem-sucedido. Esses achados são essenciais para empresas que buscam fine-tuning eficiente e redução de custos computacionais em modelos de linguagem.

artigos.titulo: Validity of LLMs as data annotators: AMALIA on authority
artigos.resumo: A national language model offers a linguistic community its own instrument for measuring what its citizens say and value. Portugal's AMALIA, a publicly funded 9B-parameter model for European Portuguese, appears competitive on agreement alone: asked to code the moral foundation of authority, it agrees with trained human coders to within six F1 points of open models eight to thirteen times its size. Yet agreement is reliability, not validity. For theoretical constructs that must be inferred rather than read from surface features, the question is whether the model follows the construct's theory or reaches the right code by correlated shortcuts. We test this with the recovery gap: the loss in performance when a holistic prompt is decomposed into the codebook's atomic clauses and recombined by the theory's explicit rule. If calibration closes that gap, some portability should survive across models and languages; where it does not, the construct-model instrument is the likely locus of failure. We ask whether a calibrated English instrument transfers to AMALIA-9B and to European Portuguese. For one construct and one corpus, it does not. Decomposition recovers only about half of AMALIA's holistic performance, and error analysis suggests reliance on surface correlates, especially moral outrage near authority figures. An open multilingual LLM closes the gap on the same Portuguese corpus under the same instructions, pointing away from the corpus as the main explanation. AMALIA can still screen and pre-code at scale, but it cannot yet measure this construct well enough to stand alone. The study is a single counterexample, not a verdict on national models; it argues that sovereign-LLM benchmark batteries should test not only agreement with human coders, but the evidential route by which that agreement is warranted.
artigos.titulo_en: Validity of LLMs as data annotators: AMALIA on authority
artigos.titulo_pt: Validade de LLMs como anotadores de dados: AMALIA sobre autoridade
artigos.resumo_executivo_en: The study evaluates the validity of the Portuguese language model AMALIA-9B as a data annotator for complex theoretical constructs, specifically moral foundations of authority. While the model achieves high agreement with trained human coders, decomposition analyses reveal that the model relies on surface correlates rather than following the construct's underlying theory. Results indicate that national language models require rigorous validity testing beyond agreement metrics for reliable measurement applications.
artigos.resumo_executivo_pt: O estudo avalia a validade do modelo de linguagem português AMALIA-9B como anotador de dados para construtos teóricos complexos, especificamente fundações morais de autoridade. Embora o modelo apresente alta concordância com codificadores humanos treinados, análises de decomposição revelam que o modelo depende de correlatos superficiais em vez de seguir a teoria subjacente do construto. Os resultados indicam que modelos de linguagem nacionais requerem testes rigorosos de validade além de métricas de concordância para aplicações de medição confiável.
artigos.justificativa: A pesquisa aborda um desafio crítico para organizações que utilizam LLMs em tarefas de anotação e codificação de dados: a distinção entre concordância superficial e validade teórica. Os achados são relevantes para empresas que dependem de modelos de linguagem para análise de conteúdo, pesquisa de mercado e medição de construtos abstratos, destacando a necessidade de validação rigorosa antes da implementação em produção.

artigos.titulo: WaspMOT: A Benchmark for Long-Term Multi-Object Tracking of Trichogramma Wasps
artigos.resumo: Multi-object tracking (MOT) has achieved strong performance on benchmarks dominated by short video sequences. However, such datasets do not adequately evaluate long-term identity preservation, where objects must be tracked consistently over extended durations. We introduce WaspMOT, a benchmark designed to address this gap through long-duration tracking of Trichogramma wasps in controlled ecological experiments. The dataset contains 10 sequences of approximately 12,000 frames each (over 8 minutes at 25 FPS), with dense MOTChallenge annotations and oracle detections to isolate association performance.   Unlike existing benchmarks, WaspMOT forms a closed-set tracking scenario where all individuals remain present throughout the sequence, requiring consistent identity assignment across thousands of frames despite abrupt jumps, occlusions, and highly similar appearance. We establish a benchmark by evaluating five tracking-by-detection methods, including ByteTrack, BoT-SORT, C-BIoU, OC-SORT, and McByte, under a unified protocol. Results show that all methods suffer from significant trajectory fragmentation, highlighting the difficulty of long-term identity preservation even with perfect detections. A simple spatial tracklet stitching baseline consistently improves performance, indicating that substantial gains remain possible.   WaspMOT provides a new benchmark for studying long-term association and reveals limitations of current tracking approaches that are not observable on conventional datasets. The benchmark will be made publicly available at the project repository: https://github.com/tstanczyk95/WaspMOT/ .
artigos.titulo_en: WaspMOT: A Benchmark for Long-Term Multi-Object Tracking of Trichogramma Wasps
artigos.titulo_pt: WaspMOT: Um Benchmark para Rastreamento Multi-Objeto de Longo Prazo de Vespas Trichogramma
artigos.resumo_executivo_en: WaspMOT is a new benchmark for long-term multi-object tracking containing 10 sequences of approximately 12,000 frames each (over 8 minutes) of Trichogramma wasps in controlled ecological experiments. The dataset addresses the gap in existing benchmarks by evaluating consistent identity preservation over extended durations, with occlusion and highly similar appearance scenarios. Evaluation of five tracking methods reveals significant limitations in current approaches, indicating substantial improvement opportunities through tracklet stitching techniques.
artigos.resumo_executivo_pt: WaspMOT é um novo benchmark para rastreamento multi-objeto de longo prazo, contendo 10 sequências de aproximadamente 12.000 frames cada (mais de 8 minutos) de vespas Trichogramma em experimentos ecológicos controlados. O dataset aborda a lacuna em benchmarks existentes ao avaliar a preservação de identidade consistente ao longo de durações estendidas, com cenários de oclusão e aparência altamente similar. A avaliação de cinco métodos de rastreamento revela limitações significativas em abordagens atuais, indicando oportunidades substanciais de melhoria através de técnicas de stitching de tracklets.
artigos.justificativa: O benchmark WaspMOT fornece uma ferramenta valiosa para avaliar e melhorar algoritmos de rastreamento multi-objeto em cenários de longo prazo, com aplicações potenciais em monitoramento ecológico, agricultura de precisão e sistemas de vigilância que requerem preservação consistente de identidade.

artigos.titulo: Pose-to-Biomechanics: Bridging 3D Human Pose Estimation and Biomechanical Attribute Prediction
artigos.resumo: Recent progress in 3D human pose estimation has made markerless recovery of skeletal motion increasingly accurate and scalable. However, most pose estimators remain optimized for geometric keypoint accuracy, while many real-world applications in rehabilitation, sports science, ergonomics, and clinical movement analysis require biomechanical quantities that describe how the body moves, loads, and activates. In this work, we propose BioModule, a lightweight plug-in temporal transformer that attaches downstream of any 3D pose estimator and predicts biomechanical attributes from standard 17-joint 3D skeletons. BioModule is estimator-agnostic and requires no modification of the upstream pose model, enabling existing pose estimators to be extended toward physically interpretable motion analysis.   To train and evaluate BioModule, we construct a large-scale aligned dataset pairing Human3.6M video and 3D keypoints with the biomechanical label space of Human3.6Mplus. We establish and verify anatomical correspondence between coordinate systems of the two datasets, enabling frame-accurate cross-modal supervision. Using this aligned supervision, BioModule predicts biomechanical quantities. We further benchmark BioModule across seven state-of-the-art 3D pose estimators, providing the first systematic analysis of how upstream pose estimation quality propagates to downstream biomechanical prediction fidelity. The results position BioModule as a compact, modular bridge between vision-based pose estimation and biomechanically meaningful human motion analysis.
artigos.titulo_en: Pose-to-Biomechanics: Bridging 3D Human Pose Estimation and Biomechanical Attribute Prediction
artigos.titulo_pt: Pose-para-Biomecânica: Conectando Estimação de Pose Humana 3D e Predição de Atributos Biomecânicos
artigos.resumo_executivo_en: The work introduces BioModule, a lightweight temporal transformer module that converts 3D pose estimates into interpretable biomechanical attributes without modifying existing pose models. Using an aligned dataset between Human3.6M and Human3.6Mplus, the system establishes precise anatomical correspondence between coordinate systems for frame-accurate supervision. The solution is evaluated across seven state-of-the-art pose estimators, demonstrating how upstream pose estimation quality propagates to downstream biomechanical prediction fidelity.
artigos.resumo_executivo_pt: O trabalho apresenta BioModule, um módulo transformer temporal leve que converte estimativas de pose 3D em atributos biomecânicos interpretáveis, sem modificar modelos de pose existentes. Utilizando um dataset alinhado entre Human3.6M e Human3.6Mplus, o sistema estabelece correspondência anatômica precisa entre coordenadas para supervisão frame-a-frame. A solução é avaliada em sete estimadores de pose estado-da-arte, demonstrando como a qualidade da estimação de pose propaga para a fidelidade da predição biomecânica.
artigos.justificativa: A solução tem aplicação direta em reabilitação, análise de movimento clínico, ciência do esporte e ergonomia, setores com demanda crescente por análise de movimento quantitativa e não-invasiva. O design modular e agnóstico ao estimador permite integração prática em sistemas existentes de captura de movimento.

artigos.titulo: Latent Memory Palace: Reasoning for Control as Autoregressive Variational Inference
artigos.resumo: Human decision-making is highly flexible -- some actions are taken immediately; others require longer deliberation. Language models have exhibited a similar capacity for adaptive "reasoning." However, transferring this capability to continuous control policies has been challenging, as directly reasoning in language space may lack the granularity for spatial understanding and precise motions. In this work, we show that reasoning for control policies can emerge by organizing information in an autoregressive latent space reminiscent of a memory palace, where retrieval is iterative and adaptive. Our method, Latent Memory Palace (LMP), formulates reasoning as variational inference with an autoregressive latent distribution. We derive a latent-space reinforcement learning technique to tractably optimize its variational lower bound. The resulting policy, LMP-$π$, achieves strong empirical performance in simulation and real-world domains while exhibiting interpretable, adaptive allocation of test-time compute. We further show that the same framework yields a variable-length action tokenizer, LMP-$\texttt{tok}$, which significantly improves the performance of downstream autoregressive policies. Together, these results present a new perspective on latent reasoning for control through the lens of variational inference.
artigos.titulo_en: Latent Memory Palace: Reasoning for Control as Autoregressive Variational Inference
artigos.titulo_pt: Palácio da Memória Latente: Raciocínio para Controle como Inferência Variacional Autorregressiva
artigos.resumo_executivo_en: The work introduces Latent Memory Palace (LMP), a method enabling continuous control policies to perform adaptive reasoning through variational inference in autoregressive latent space. The approach achieves strong empirical performance in simulation and real-world domains with interpretable, adaptive allocation of test-time compute. The framework also produces a variable-length action tokenizer that significantly improves downstream autoregressive policy performance.
artigos.resumo_executivo_pt: O trabalho apresenta Latent Memory Palace (LMP), um método que habilita políticas de controle contínuo a realizar raciocínio adaptativo através de inferência variacional em espaço latente autorregressivo. A abordagem alcança forte desempenho em simulação e domínios do mundo real, com alocação interpretável e adaptativa de computação em tempo de teste. O framework também produz um tokenizador de ações de comprimento variável que melhora significativamente o desempenho de políticas autorregressivas subsequentes.
artigos.justificativa: O método demonstra aplicabilidade prática em domínios do mundo real com melhorias significativas em políticas de controle, sendo relevante para robótica, automação e sistemas de controle que requerem raciocínio adaptativo e decisões flexíveis em tempo real.

artigos.titulo: Deep Learning for Joint Narrowband Interference Cancellation and Soft Demodulation in OFDM Systems
artigos.resumo: Narrowband interference (NBI) severely degrades orthogonal frequency-division multiplexing (OFDM) systems by corrupting subcarriers and rendering classical soft demodulation ineffective. Conventional compressed-sensing (CS) mitigation exhibits high sequential latency and leaves structured, non-Gaussian residuals that cause log-likelihood ratio (LLR) unreliability, decoder saturation, and severe error floors when employing classical Gaussian demappers. We resolve this pipeline mismatch using a unified deep learning framework for joint NBI cancellation and robust soft demodulation. First, NBI-CNet employs a physics-informed convolutional architecture to estimate NBI parameters and remove multi-tone interference in a single forward pass. Without requiring prior knowledge of the active interferer count, NBI-CNet reduces computational complexity by up to 60% ($N{=}2048, Q{=}64$) compared to the state-of-the-art EOMP-IDS algorithm. Second, LLR-CNet acts as a structural whitener by mapping non-Gaussian post-mitigation residuals onto well-calibrated soft metrics. Simulations demonstrate that this joint framework eliminates the error floors inherent to traditional baselines across dense grids. Under severe interference ($\text{SIR}{=}{-}10$ dB), the pipeline operates within a $0.2$ to $0.5$ dB SNR margin of the optimal iterative baseline at a target block error rate (BLER) of $10^{-4}$. Under mild interference ($\text{SIR}{=}10$ dB) with heavy spectral overlap ($Q{=}12$), where classical greedy algorithms erroneously subtract valid data components and corrupt the payload, NBI-CNet avoids signal-peak confusion to deliver a coding gain exceeding $3$ dB. Finally, the architecture circumvents the $2{\times}10^{-4}$ error floor triggered by interferer-estimation errors, while its scale-invariant design enables robust generalization across arbitrary FFT sizes without retraining.
artigos.titulo_en: Deep Learning for Joint Narrowband Interference Cancellation and Soft Demodulation in OFDM Systems
artigos.titulo_pt: Aprendizado Profundo para Cancelamento Conjunto de Interferência em Banda Estreita e Demodulação Suave em Sistemas OFDM
artigos.resumo_executivo_en: The paper presents a unified deep learning framework addressing narrowband interference in OFDM systems through two convolutional neural networks: NBI-CNet for interference estimation and cancellation, and LLR-CNet for robust soft demodulation. The solution reduces computational complexity by up to 60% compared to state-of-the-art methods, eliminates error floors inherent to traditional approaches, and maintains performance within 0.2-0.5 dB of the optimal baseline under severe interference. The scale-invariant design enables robust generalization across different FFT sizes without retraining.
artigos.resumo_executivo_pt: O artigo apresenta um framework de aprendizado profundo unificado que resolve o problema de interferência em banda estreita em sistemas OFDM através de duas redes neurais convolucionais: NBI-CNet para estimação e cancelamento de interferência, e LLR-CNet para demodulação robusta. A solução reduz complexidade computacional em até 60% comparada ao estado-da-arte, eliminando erros de piso inerentes aos métodos tradicionais e mantendo desempenho dentro de 0,2-0,5 dB do baseline ótimo sob interferência severa. O design scale-invariant permite generalização robusta entre diferentes tamanhos de FFT sem retreinamento.
artigos.justificativa: A solução aborda um problema crítico em sistemas de comunicação OFDM (amplamente utilizados em 4G/5G e WiFi), oferecendo ganhos significativos em eficiência computacional (60%) e desempenho de decodificação, com potencial de aplicação direta em infraestrutura de telecomunicações e sistemas de comunicação robustos.

artigos.titulo: Remember When It Matters: Proactive Memory Agent for Long-Horizon Agents
artigos.resumo: In long-horizon tasks, decision-relevant state is often scattered across an expanding trajectory, while the action agent must surface it and act. As trajectories grow, task requirements, environment facts, prior attempts, diagnoses, and open subgoals can be buried in the context window or pushed beyond it, failing to influence decisions when needed. We call this failure mode "behavioral state decay". We study memory as an active intervention mechanism rather than passive retrieval. A separate memory agent runs alongside an unmodified action agent, updating a structured memory bank from the recent trajectory and deciding whether to inject a memory-grounded reminder or remain silent. The module is plug-and-play with frontier action agents and existing agent harnesses. Across Terminal-Bench 2.0 and $τ^2$-Bench, it improves pass@1 for both weaker and stronger action agents, with gains of +8.3 pp on Terminal-Bench and +6.8 pp on $τ^2$-Bench. Ablations show that selective intervention outperforms passive bank exposure, always-on injection, advisor-only guidance, and general retrieval. As an early step toward open-weight memory policies, we train Qwen3.5-27B on SETA using SFT and GRPO, improving validation reward and achieving partial transfer to Terminal-Bench.
artigos.titulo_en: Remember When It Matters: Proactive Memory Agent for Long-Horizon Agents
artigos.titulo_pt: Lembre-se Quando Importa: Agente de Memória Proativa para Agentes de Longo Horizonte
artigos.resumo_executivo_en: The paper introduces a proactive memory agent that operates alongside action agents to address "behavioral state decay" in long-horizon tasks, where critical information can be lost within the context window. The plug-and-play module maintains a structured memory bank and selectively injects relevant reminders, achieving improvements of +8.3 pp on Terminal-Bench and +6.8 pp on τ²-Bench. The work also trains open-weight models (Qwen3.5-27B) using SFT and GRPO, demonstrating partial transfer to additional benchmarks.
artigos.resumo_executivo_pt: O artigo propõe um agente de memória proativa que funciona em paralelo com agentes de ação para mitigar o "behavioral state decay" em tarefas de longo horizonte, onde informações críticas podem ser perdidas no contexto. O módulo plug-and-play utiliza um banco de memória estruturado e decide seletivamente quando injetar lembretes relevantes, melhorando o desempenho em +8.3 pp no Terminal-Bench e +6.8 pp no τ²-Bench. O trabalho também treina modelos de peso aberto (Qwen3.5-27B) usando SFT e GRPO, demonstrando transferência parcial para benchmarks adicionais.
artigos.justificativa: A solução aborda um problema crítico em sistemas de IA autônomos de longo horizonte, com aplicações diretas em automação de tarefas complexas, assistentes inteligentes e sistemas de planejamento. A natureza plug-and-play do módulo facilita integração em sistemas existentes, aumentando sua viabilidade comercial.

artigos.titulo: LTM: Large-scale Terrain Model for Wildfire-prone Landscapes
artigos.resumo: Accurate 3D terrain maps are essential for emergency response when assessing wildfire hazards. However, wildfire-prone regions often span vast areas where conventional reconstruction methods underperform. Airborne LiDAR systems provide high-resolution terrain data, but they are expensive and infrequently updated.
