Image-based methods offer a lower-cost alternative, but struggle due to sparse visual features and limited image overlap. We propose a multi-modal reconstruction framework leveraging outdated Digital Elevation Models (DEMs) as geometric priors for image-based 3D reconstruction. Our key innovation is physics-based pixel-pixel alignment between images and DEM data, dramatically reducing computational complexity by eliminating expensive feature matching procedures. To validate our approach, we developed a large-terrain simulator based on a real wildfire-prone area, generating realistic images enabling a comprehensive evaluation. Given posed images and legacy DEMs, our method produces high-fidelity depth maps while maintaining real-time performance. We find significant improvements in reconstruction accuracy and computational efficiency over existing techniques, offering a scalable solution for wildfire response.
artigos.titulo_en: LTM: Large-scale Terrain Model for Wildfire-prone Landscapes
artigos.titulo_pt: LTM: Modelo de Terreno em Larga Escala para Paisagens Propensas a Incêndios Florestais
artigos.resumo_executivo_en: The paper proposes a multi-modal framework for large-scale 3D terrain reconstruction by combining aerial images with legacy Digital Elevation Models (DEMs) as geometric priors. The key innovation is physics-based pixel-pixel alignment that eliminates expensive feature matching procedures, significantly reducing computational complexity. The method produces high-fidelity depth maps in real-time, offering a scalable solution for wildfire emergency response.
artigos.resumo_executivo_pt: O artigo propõe um framework multi-modal para reconstrução 3D de terrenos em larga escala, combinando imagens aéreas com Modelos de Elevação Digital (DEMs) legados como priors geométricos. A inovação principal é um alinhamento pixel-pixel baseado em física que elimina procedimentos custosos de correspondência de features, reduzindo significativamente a complexidade computacional. O método produz mapas de profundidade de alta fidelidade em tempo real, oferecendo uma solução escalável para resposta a incêndios florestais.
artigos.justificativa: A solução endereça um problema crítico em resposta a emergências de incêndios florestais, oferecendo mapas de terreno precisos e atualizados a um custo significativamente menor que sistemas LiDAR convencionais, com potencial de aplicação imediata em agências de proteção ambiental e gestão de desastres.

artigos.titulo: HumanForge: A Human-Centric Deepfake Video Benchmark with Multi-Agent Forgery Rationales
artigos.resumo: Rapid advancements in video diffusion models and temporal editing tools have enabled the generation of highly realistic human-centric videos, posing unprecedented challenges to digital content forensics. Existing benchmarks primarily focus on either face-swapping or global text-to-video synthesis, overlooking the crucial dimensions of human-object or human-human interactions and multi-modal alignment. To address these limitations, we introduce HumanForge, a unified, large-scale, and multi-paradigm human-centric video forgery dataset. To construct and annotate this dataset without labor-intensive manual labeling or hallucinated monolithic prompts, we propose Gen2Anno, a modular active multi-agent pipeline built on LangGraph. Gen2Anno coordinates six specialized agents-ranging from source profiling to MoE-based reference analysis and closed-loop forensic verification-to generate over 18K high-fidelity video segments and produce structured, contrastive omni-annotations containing binary decisions, fine-grained artifact categories, and spatio-temporal localization. Extensive benchmarks using state-of-the-art traditional detectors and Large Multimodal Models (LMMs) demonstrate the significant challenges of zero-shot generalization and fine-grained reasoning on HumanForge. Code and dataset will be publicly released.
artigos.titulo_en: HumanForge: A Human-Centric Deepfake Video Benchmark with Multi-Agent Forgery Rationales
artigos.titulo_pt: HumanForge: Um Benchmark de Vídeo Deepfake Centrado em Humanos com Justificativas de Falsificação Multi-Agente
artigos.resumo_executivo_en: HumanForge is a large-scale, multi-paradigm dataset for detecting human-centric video forgeries, addressing limitations of existing benchmarks by including human-object and human-human interactions with multi-modal alignment. The work proposes Gen2Anno, a modular multi-agent pipeline based on LangGraph that coordinates six specialized agents to generate over 18K high-fidelity video segments with structured and contrastive annotations. Extensive benchmarks with traditional detectors and Large Multimodal Models demonstrate significant challenges in zero-shot generalization and fine-grained reasoning.
artigos.resumo_executivo_pt: HumanForge é um dataset de grande escala e multi-paradigma para detecção de falsificação de vídeos centrados em humanos, abordando limitações de benchmarks existentes ao incluir interações humano-objeto e humano-humano com alinhamento multi-modal. O trabalho propõe Gen2Anno, um pipeline modular de múltiplos agentes baseado em LangGraph que coordena seis agentes especializados para gerar mais de 18K segmentos de vídeo de alta fidelidade com anotações estruturadas e contrastivas. Benchmarks extensivos com detectores tradicionais e Large Multimodal Models demonstram desafios significativos em generalização zero-shot e raciocínio fino-granular.
artigos.justificativa: A detecção de deepfakes é crítica para segurança digital, conformidade regulatória e proteção contra desinformação em plataformas de mídia. O dataset e metodologia propostos endereçam desafios reais de forensics de conteúdo em cenários complexos com interações humanas, com aplicação direta em sistemas de verificação de autenticidade de vídeo.

artigos.titulo: MPFlow: Learning Budgeted Max-Flow Optimization on the Lightning Network with Deep Graph Reinforcement Learning
artigos.resumo: We address liquidity placement in the Bitcoin Lightning Network (LN): given a fixed budget, which channels should a node open to maximize its routing capacity? We cast this as a budget-constrained combinatorial optimization problem on graphs, selecting $k$ edge additions that maximize $s$--$t$ max-flow, a theory-grounded measure of routing capacity, and solve it with graph reinforcement learning. Our lightweight agent combines a message-passing policy network with proximal policy optimization (PPO) and action masking, and is trained under a hub-exclusion curriculum: the network's top hubs are removed from training subgraphs, forcing the policy to learn capacity-aware placement rather than hub attachment. In extensive experiments on real Lightning Network snapshots, our method consistently outperforms strong heuristic baselines on the max-flow objective across multiple seeds and unseen graphs. The agent has been deployed in production for peer recommendations, executing 4640 channel-open decisions that cumulatively allocate 267.3 BTC over $16 million across 30 managed nodes.
artigos.titulo_en: MPFlow: Learning Budgeted Max-Flow Optimization on the Lightning Network with Deep Graph Reinforcement Learning
artigos.titulo_pt: MPFlow: Aprendizado de Otimização de Fluxo Máximo com Orçamento na Rede Lightning com Aprendizado por Reforço em Grafos Profundos
artigos.resumo_executivo_en: The work addresses liquidity placement optimization in the Bitcoin Lightning Network through a budget-constrained combinatorial optimization problem on graphs. A reinforcement learning agent combining message-passing policy networks with proximal policy optimization is trained using hub-exclusion curriculum to learn capacity-aware channel placement. The method has been deployed in production, executing 4,640 channel-open decisions that cumulatively allocated 267.3 BTC across 30 managed nodes.
artigos.resumo_executivo_pt: O trabalho aborda a otimização de posicionamento de liquidez na Rede Lightning do Bitcoin através de um problema de otimização combinatória com restrição orçamentária em grafos. Um agente de aprendizado por reforço baseado em redes de políticas com passagem de mensagens e otimização de políticas proximais é treinado com currículo de exclusão de hubs para aprender posicionamento consciente de capacidade. O método foi implantado em produção, executando 4.640 decisões de abertura de canais que alocaram cumulativamente 267,3 BTC em 30 nós gerenciados.
artigos.justificativa: O sistema foi implantado em produção na Rede Lightning, gerenciando alocação real de capital (267,3 BTC) e otimizando decisões críticas de infraestrutura de rede, demonstrando impacto comercial direto e mensurável em operações de criptomoedas.

artigos.titulo: Do You Need a Frontier Model as a Citation Verifier? Benchmarking Rubric LLMs for Deep-Research Source Attribution
artigos.resumo: Reinforcement learning increasingly relies on an LLM judge to score each rubric criterion, and that judge acts as the reward model during training. Before such a signal can be trusted, we need to know how capable the judge must be and how biased it is. We study this calibration question for citation quality in deep-research systems, where a search-grounded LLM must support each claim it writes with a cited source. Citation quality is a structured rubric task in which each attribution-citation pair is judged along two dimensions that require an LLM, source relevance and factual support. On an adversarial long-form benchmark, we score 8 off-the-shelf LLM judges from 3 model families against gold labels over 1,248 rubric decisions, all of which were human-reviewed and 378 of which were hard cases adjudicated from judge disagreements. Cheaper judges remain competitive across both dimensions, with GPT-5-mini attaining the strongest source-relevance pass-class F1 at 0.908 ($κ$=0.636), while on factual support the judges are statistically indistinguishable (overlapping confidence intervals), so no single model dominates. At comparable F1, the judges still differ substantially in pass-rate drift, false positive rate, and false negative rate. Scalar F1 obscures this directional bias, yet it is exactly what a downstream reinforcement learning loop would reinforce. Calibrating the judge is therefore a prerequisite for using citation rubrics as reward signals, and our results show that this calibration does not require the most expensive available model.
artigos.titulo_en: Do You Need a Frontier Model as a Citation Verifier? Benchmarking Rubric LLMs for Deep-Research Source Attribution
artigos.titulo_pt: Você Precisa de um Modelo de Fronteira como Verificador de Citações? Benchmarking de LLMs com Rubrica para Atribuição de Fontes em Pesquisa Profunda
artigos.resumo_executivo_en: The paper investigates the calibration of LLM judges for evaluating citation quality in deep-research systems, where smaller models like GPT-4-mini demonstrate competitiveness with more expensive models. Results show that cheaper models maintain comparable performance on source relevance and factual support dimensions, but differ substantially in pass-rate drift and false positive/negative rates. Proper judge calibration is essential before using citation rubrics as reward signals in reinforcement learning loops.
artigos.resumo_executivo_pt: O artigo investiga a calibração de juízes LLM para avaliar a qualidade de citações em sistemas de pesquisa profunda, onde modelos menores como GPT-4-mini demonstram competitividade com modelos mais caros. Os resultados mostram que modelos mais baratos mantêm desempenho comparável em dimensões de relevância de fonte e suporte factual, mas diferem substancialmente em viés de taxa de acerto e taxas de falsos positivos/negativos. A calibração adequada do juiz é essencial antes de usar rubricas de citação como sinais de recompensa em loops de aprendizado por reforço.
artigos.justificativa: O trabalho fornece insights críticos para otimizar custos em sistemas de IA generativa que usam LLMs como juízes de recompensa, demonstrando que modelos menores podem ser suficientes para tarefas de verificação de citações, reduzindo significativamente despesas operacionais em produção.

artigos.titulo: ProjAgent: Procedural Similarity Retrieval for Repository-Level Code Generation
artigos.resumo: Repository-level code generation requires implementing target functions while accounting for complex cross-file dependencies and project-specific conventions. Existing retrieval methods predominantly rely on lexical, structural, or semantic similarity, often overlooking repository functions that implement similar procedural logic despite differing in identifiers or application domains. We propose ProjAgent, a repository-level code generation system that introduces procedural similarity as an explicit retrieval signal. ProjAgent decomposes the target function into intermediate reasoning steps and employs an agentic workflow to retrieve repository functions that exhibit similar procedural behavior at each step. The retrieved procedural context is integrated with conventional semantic retrieval to construct a richer repository context for code generation. ProjAgent further incorporates a conservative static-analysis feedback loop that iteratively repairs generated code using compiler and static-analysis feedback. Evaluated on REPOCOD, ProjAgent achieves 41.14% Pass@1, outperforming existing retrieval-based baselines. These results demonstrate that procedural similarity is an effective and previously unexplored retrieval dimension for repository-level code generation.
artigos.titulo_en: ProjAgent: Procedural Similarity Retrieval for Repository-Level Code Generation
artigos.titulo_pt: ProjAgent: Recuperação de Similaridade Procedural para Geração de Código em Nível de Repositório
artigos.resumo_executivo_en: ProjAgent is a repository-level code generation system that introduces procedural similarity as an explicit retrieval signal by decomposing target functions into intermediate reasoning steps. The system employs an agentic workflow to retrieve repository functions with similar procedural behavior at each step, integrating this context with conventional semantic retrieval. It incorporates a conservative static-analysis feedback loop for iterative code repair, achieving 41.14% Pass@1 on the REPOCOD benchmark.
artigos.resumo_executivo_pt: ProjAgent é um sistema de geração de código em nível de repositório que introduz similaridade procedural como sinal de recuperação explícito, decompondo funções alvo em etapas de raciocínio intermediárias. O sistema utiliza um fluxo de trabalho baseado em agentes para recuperar funções do repositório com comportamento procedural similar, integrando esse contexto com recuperação semântica convencional. Incorpora um loop de feedback de análise estática conservadora que repara iterativamente o código gerado, alcançando 41.14% Pass@1 no benchmark REPOCOD.
artigos.justificativa: A geração de código em nível de repositório é crítica para automação de desenvolvimento de software em projetos complexos. O método de similaridade procedural oferece uma abordagem inovadora que melhora significativamente a qualidade do código gerado, com potencial de impacto direto na produtividade de desenvolvimento e redução de custos de manutenção.

artigos.titulo: A Practical Investigation of Training-free Relaxed Speculative Decoding
artigos.resumo: Speculative decoding accelerates sampling from an autoregressive LLM by using a faster auxiliary model to draft tokens which are then verified in parallel by the LLM. Standard speculative decoding is lossless: its rejection and resampling steps exactly preserve the LLM's sampling distribution. Recent work argues that relaxing this strict guarantee can yield further speed-ups, controlled capability-speed trade-offs, or even capability gains. We practically investigate training-free relaxed speculative decoding techniques, unify existing approaches within a shared framework, benchmark them on contemporary settings, and distil takeaways and empirical findings for practitioners. Important takeaways include: relaxation can require considerable capability evaluation unlike lossless speculative decoding, and many relaxed approaches rely on a drafter that is a good language model, making them unsuited for lightweight dedicated multi-token-prediction drafters.
artigos.titulo_en: A Practical Investigation of Training-free Relaxed Speculative Decoding
artigos.titulo_pt: Uma Investigação Prática de Decodificação Especulativa Relaxada Sem Treinamento
artigos.resumo_executivo_en: This paper investigates training-free relaxed speculative decoding techniques that accelerate LLM sampling through faster auxiliary models, unifying existing approaches within a shared framework. Key findings show that relaxation requires substantial capability evaluation unlike lossless speculative decoding, and many relaxed approaches depend on drafters that are good language models. The work provides practical recommendations for practitioners on speed-capability trade-offs.
artigos.resumo_executivo_pt: O artigo investiga técnicas de decodificação especulativa relaxada sem treinamento que aceleram a amostragem de LLMs através de modelos auxiliares mais rápidos, unificando abordagens existentes em um framework compartilhado. Os achados principais indicam que a relaxação pode exigir avaliação considerável de capacidades, diferentemente da decodificação especulativa lossless, e muitas abordagens relaxadas dependem de drafters que sejam bons modelos de linguagem. O trabalho fornece recomendações práticas para implementadores sobre trade-offs entre velocidade e capacidade.
artigos.justificativa: A otimização de velocidade de inferência em LLMs é crítica para aplicações em produção, reduzindo custos computacionais e latência. O trabalho fornece insights práticos sobre trade-offs entre velocidade e qualidade, essencial para implementações comerciais de sistemas de IA generativa.

artigos.titulo: SAM-MT: Real-Time Interactive Multi-Target Video Segmentation
artigos.resumo: Modern Video Object Segmentation (VOS) involves tracking and segmenting user-specified targets. While recent approaches have achieved remarkable performance in single-target scenarios, extending them to multi-target settings typically involves replicating the single-target processing for each individual object, resulting in reduced frame rates (FPS) with unbounded latency as target count increases. Built upon Segment Anything 2 (SAM2), we propose SAM-MT, which addresses this by transforming the model into an interactive framework for real-time Multi-Target video segmentation. SAM-MT uses explicit queries to represent different individual targets, in parallel with a shared representation for global context. It employs decoupled masked attention to keep individual identities distinct from cross-target interference, and sparse memory for stable temporal evolution, along with specialized strategies for occlusion handling and overlap prevention. SAM-MT successfully decouples latency from the number of targets, achieving real-time speed on par with single-target baselines (>36 FPS for 10 targets) while maintaining SAM2's robust video segmentation performance.
artigos.titulo_en: SAM-MT: Real-Time Interactive Multi-Target Video Segmentation
artigos.titulo_pt: SAM-MT: Segmentação de Vídeo Multi-Alvo Interativa em Tempo Real
artigos.resumo_executivo_en: SAM-MT extends Segment Anything 2 for real-time multi-target video segmentation using explicit queries and decoupled masked attention to maintain distinct object identities. The system achieves over 36 FPS for 10 simultaneous targets, decoupling latency from the number of tracked objects. It incorporates specialized strategies for occlusion handling and overlap prevention while maintaining SAM2's robust video segmentation performance.
artigos.resumo_executivo_pt: SAM-MT estende o Segment Anything 2 para segmentação de vídeo multi-alvo em tempo real, utilizando consultas explícitas e atenção mascarada desacoplada para manter identidades distintas entre objetos. O sistema alcança mais de 36 FPS para 10 alvos simultâneos, desacoplando a latência do número de objetos rastreados. A solução incorpora estratégias especializadas para tratamento de oclusão e prevenção de sobreposição, mantendo o desempenho robusto do SAM2.
artigos.justificativa: SAM-MT resolve um desafio crítico em aplicações comerciais de análise de vídeo ao permitir rastreamento e segmentação eficiente de múltiplos objetos em tempo real, viabilizando sistemas de vigilância, análise de multidões e automação visual escaláveis sem degradação de performance.

artigos.titulo: SolarChain-Eval: A Physics-Constrained Benchmark for Trustworthy Economic Agents in Decentralized Energy Markets
artigos.resumo: As agentic AI systems are increasingly applied to cyber-physical environments, their evaluation requires assessment of both task performance and trustworthiness. In decentralized energy markets, autonomous agents may improve market utility, but may also exploit invalid physical data, create artificial liquidity, and produce unstable governance decisions. Therefore, we propose SolarChain-Eval, a physics-constrained benchmark for evaluating trustworthy economic agents. It formulates market governance as a Gymnasium-compatible Markov Decision Process, where agents make hourly decisions. SolarChain-Eval evaluates each policy across multiple dimensions, including market utility, physical safety, slippage, action smoothness, spatial fairness, and auditability. To support agentic evaluation, SolarChain-Eval incorporates an LLM-based Planner/Auditor layer. The Planner defines episode-level action bounds and audit rules, while the Auditor reviews and revises high-risk actions. All interventions are recorded through structured logs, including trigger signals, proposed actions, revised actions, and audit rationales. Experiments with static, random, myopic, RL, and RL+LLM policies reveal a clear utility-safety trade-off. RL agents improve market utility but can still produce unsafe behavior. When the physics penalty is removed, reward-maximizing agents exploit invalid generation and increase artificial liquidity. The LLM Planner/Auditor improves auditability and mitigates selected risks, but it cannot fully compensate for a misspecified reward function. These results indicate that trustworthy agentic AI evaluation requires both physical constraints and transparent intervention traces. We release data and code as open access on GitHub for replicability.
artigos.titulo_en: SolarChain-Eval: A Physics-Constrained Benchmark for Trustworthy Economic Agents in Decentralized Energy Markets
artigos.titulo_pt: SolarChain-Eval: Um Benchmark Restrito por Física para Agentes Econômicos Confiáveis em Mercados de Energia Descentralizados
artigos.resumo_executivo_en: SolarChain-Eval is a benchmark for evaluating autonomous economic agents in decentralized energy markets, incorporating physics constraints and LLM-based audit layers. The work demonstrates a utility-safety trade-off, showing that RL agents improve market utility but can produce unsafe behavior when physical penalties are removed. Results indicate that trustworthy agentic AI evaluation requires both physical constraints and transparent intervention tracing.
artigos.resumo_executivo_pt: SolarChain-Eval é um benchmark para avaliar agentes econômicos autônomos em mercados de energia descentralizados, incorporando restrições físicas e camadas de auditoria baseadas em LLM. O trabalho demonstra um trade-off entre utilidade de mercado e segurança física, revelando que agentes RL melhoram a utilidade mas podem produzir comportamentos inseguros quando penalidades físicas são removidas. Os resultados indicam que avaliação confiável de IA agêntica requer tanto restrições físicas quanto rastreamento transparente de intervenções.
artigos.justificativa: O trabalho aborda avaliação de confiabilidade em sistemas de IA agêntica aplicados a ambientes críticos (mercados de energia), fornecendo um framework prático para mitigar riscos de exploração de dados inválidos e comportamentos instáveis em sistemas autônomos de governança de mercado.

artigos.titulo: Multi-Resolution Feature Stem for Diabetic Retinopathy lesion segmentation
artigos.resumo: Diabetic Retinopathy (DR) is a leading cause of preventable blindness worldwide, requiring automated lesion segmentation using deep learning models for early detection and monitoring. However, DR lesions vary dramatically in size from tiny microaneurysms to large hemorrhages and exudates. This variability creates conflicting demands on the model architecture and input resolution, posing a challenge for effective design. This work investigates the impact of input resolution on different lesion types. Through systematic experimentation with multiple architectures (U-Net, UNet++, Vision Transformers, DeepLabV3+) at $512 \times 512$ and $1024 \times 1024$ resolutions, we identify a critical, counter-intuitive phenomenon where increasing input resolution has opposing effects on different lesion types. We demonstrate that while higher resolution is essential for resolving fine-grained microaneurysms, it can unexpectedly degrade performance on larger hemorrhages. This finding challenges the common assumption that higher resolution is uniformly beneficial. To address this, we propose a novel Multi-Resolution Feature Stem, an input-level pyramid integrated with a UNet++ backbone. This architecture processes multiple scales in parallel, capturing fine-grained details without sacrificing contextual information. This work contributes crucial empirical evidence of this complex, resolution-dependent behavior and a practical, parameter-efficient architecture that successfully resolves this trade-off.
artigos.titulo_en: Multi-Resolution Feature Stem for Diabetic Retinopathy lesion segmentation
artigos.titulo_pt: Stem de Características Multi-Resolução para Segmentação de Lesões em Retinopatia Diabética
artigos.resumo_executivo_en: This work investigates the counter-intuitive impact of input resolution on diabetic retinopathy lesion segmentation, demonstrating that higher resolution benefits small microaneurysms but degrades performance on larger hemorrhages. A novel Multi-Resolution Feature Stem architecture is proposed that processes multiple scales in parallel, successfully resolving this trade-off while maintaining parameter efficiency. The research provides empirical evidence of resolution-dependent behavior and a practical solution for automated early detection and monitoring of diabetic retinopathy.
artigos.resumo_executivo_pt: O trabalho aborda a segmentação de lesões em Retinopatia Diabética através de uma investigação sistemática do impacto da resolução de entrada em diferentes tipos de lesões, revelando que aumentar a resolução tem efeitos opostos em lesões de diferentes tamanhos. Propõe uma arquitetura inovadora chamada Multi-Resolution Feature Stem que processa múltiplas escalas em paralelo, resolvendo o trade-off entre capturar detalhes finos de microaneurismas e manter informações contextuais para hemorragias maiores. A solução é parameter-efficient e demonstra empiricamente como resolver esse comportamento complexo dependente de resolução.
artigos.justificativa: A Retinopatia Diabética é uma das principais causas de cegueira evitável globalmente, e a segmentação automatizada de lesões é crítica para detecção precoce e monitoramento. A solução proposta oferece uma arquitetura prática e eficiente que melhora significativamente a precisão diagnóstica em aplicações clínicas reais.

artigos.titulo: Do Transformations Reveal the Truth? Generative Residual Learning for Generalized AI-Generated Image Detection
artigos.resumo: The rapid advancement of generative AI has enabled the creation of highly realistic deepfake media, posing significant threats, including misinformation, digital identity theft, fraud, and manipulation of public opinion. AI-generated image (AIGI) detection is reliably challenging due to the diversity of generative methods and the subtle artifacts they leave behind. In this work, we propose GenRes, a novel framework for generative residual learning via a neural tensor network, which models fine-grained relational features between original and transformed samples to enhance generalization. To address scenarios involving multiple generative transformations, we introduce GenRes++, which employs a learnable attention mechanism to aggregate relational features across multiple transformed samples and enables the model to focus on the most informative cues. Both models leverage PE-Core as a feature extractor, providing generalized and semantically rich embeddings that improve cross-domain performance and enable the detection of AIGI generated by unseen methods. Comprehensive experiments on multiple benchmark datasets demonstrate that the proposed GenRes++ approach outperforms existing methods.
artigos.titulo_en: Do Transformations Reveal the Truth? Generative Residual Learning for Generalized AI-Generated Image Detection
artigos.titulo_pt: As Transformações Revelam a Verdade? Aprendizado de Resíduos Generativos para Detecção Generalizada de Imagens Geradas por IA
artigos.resumo_executivo_en: The work proposes GenRes and GenRes++, innovative frameworks for AI-generated image detection through generative residual learning via neural tensor networks. The models employ learnable attention mechanisms to aggregate relational features across transformed samples, improving generalization across multiple generative methods. Experiments on multiple benchmark datasets demonstrate superior performance in detecting images generated by unseen methods.
artigos.resumo_executivo_pt: O trabalho propõe GenRes e GenRes++, frameworks inovadores para detecção de imagens geradas por IA através de aprendizado de resíduos generativos via redes neurais tensoriais. Os modelos utilizam mecanismos de atenção aprendíveis para agregar características relacionais entre amostras transformadas, melhorando a generalização em múltiplos métodos generativos. Experimentos em múltiplos datasets demonstram desempenho superior na detecção de imagens geradas por métodos não vistos anteriormente.
artigos.justificativa: A detecção confiável de imagens geradas por IA é crítica para combater deepfakes, fraude, roubo de identidade digital e manipulação de opinião pública, representando uma necessidade urgente para segurança digital e conformidade regulatória em múltiplos setores.

artigos.titulo: Resample or Reroute? Budget-Aware Test-Time Model Selection for Large Language Models
artigos.resumo: Routing among large language models (LLMs) trades response quality against serving cost, motivated by the reported gap between deployed routers and a per-instance oracle. Recent analysis shows that test-time resampling can recover per-instance selection headroom that no single-commit router captures; however, that guarantee holds only under an idealized oracle equipped with correctness labels and an unconstrained budget, neither of which a deployed system has. To the best of our knowledge, no previous work treats resampling the committed model and rerouting to an alternative model as competing uses of a single per-query cost budget. Therefore, this work formulates budget-aware test-time model selection: given a per-query budget and an imperfect verifier, allocate each unit of budget between resampling and rerouting so that expected correctness is maximized. An online resample-or-reroute (RoR) allocation policy driven by estimated marginal correctness per unit cost is proposed, and its behavior is grounded in the recoverability asymmetry between selection and sampling. Replay experiments on newly regenerated multi-draw correctness tensors from an eleven-model open-weight pool over four benchmarks of differing difficulty show that the proposed RoR policy attains a favorable cost-quality Pareto front relative to single-route, one-commit-router, budget-aware best-of-K, cascade, and random-allocation baselines for the tested pools, with the largest gains on the most heterogeneous benchmark; an ablation further shows the gains are verifier-gated, shrinking as verifier quality degrades, and robustness replays under a provider price vector and a label-free agreement verifier delineate where the conclusions carry over.
artigos.titulo_en: Resample or Reroute? Budget-Aware Test-Time Model Selection for Large Language Models
artigos.titulo_pt: Reamostrar ou Redirecionar? Seleção de Modelo em Tempo de Teste com Orçamento Limitado para Modelos de Linguagem de Grande Escala
artigos.resumo_executivo_en: This work addresses test-time model selection for LLMs under budget constraints, formulating the allocation problem between resampling and rerouting to maximize expected correctness. An online Resample-or-Reroute (RoR) policy is proposed that dynamically allocates per-query budget between resampling the current model and routing to alternatives, driven by estimated marginal correctness gains per unit cost. Experiments across multiple benchmarks demonstrate that the RoR policy achieves a superior cost-quality Pareto front compared to conventional baselines, with larger gains on heterogeneous scenarios.
artigos.resumo_executivo_pt: O trabalho aborda a seleção de modelos em tempo de teste para LLMs sob restrições orçamentárias, formulando o problema de alocação entre reamostragem e redirecionamento para maximizar a qualidade esperada. Propõe uma política online (RoR) que aloca dinamicamente o orçamento por consulta entre reamostragem do modelo atual e roteamento para modelos alternativos, baseada em estimativas de ganho marginal de correção por unidade de custo. Experimentos em múltiplos benchmarks demonstram que a política RoR alcança uma frente de Pareto custo-qualidade superior a baselines convencionais, com ganhos maiores em cenários heterogêneos.
artigos.justificativa: O trabalho endereça um problema crítico em sistemas de produção com LLMs: otimizar a qualidade de resposta sob restrições de custo de inferência. A política RoR oferece um mecanismo prático para melhorar a relação custo-qualidade em ambientes com múltiplos modelos disponíveis, com aplicabilidade direta em sistemas de roteamento inteligente e redução de custos operacionais.

artigos.titulo: WebSwarm: Recursive Multi-Agent Orchestration for Deep-and-Wide Web Search
artigos.resumo: Large language model (LLM)-based web search agents are transforming information seeking from simple factoid question answering into complex, deep-and-wide search and research-oriented tasks. A single ReAct-style agent is constrained by one long trajectory and limited context, making it difficult to handle depth and coverage simultaneously. Existing multi-agent systems improve search coverage through parallel execution and aggregation, but still exhibit clear limitations in recursive depth, collaboration adaptability, and evidence-grounded expansion. We propose WebSwarm, a progressive recursive delegation framework that jointly constructs task decomposition, recursive expansion, and agent collaboration during inference. WebSwarm dynamically instantiates agentic search nodes, each coupling a local objective with a search mode that specifies how the node should organize search and collaboration. Each node can either solve its objective itself or further delegate child nodes; after solving, it returns evidence and results upward, enabling parent nodes to further expand, revise, or aggregate the search process. To guide this process, WebSwarm first probes how task-relevant information is organized on the web to ground subsequent node expansion, and reuses process-level experience across homogeneous sibling nodes. Experiments on BrowseComp-Plus, WideSearch, DeepWideSearch, and GISA show that WebSwarm consistently outperforms single-agent and multi-agent baselines on deep, wide, and interleaved deep-and-wide tasks. Further analyses of ablation, task difficulty, web tool efficiency, and model generalization explain WebSwarm's effectiveness and provide insights for multi-agent search systems.
artigos.titulo_en: WebSwarm: Recursive Multi-Agent Orchestration for Deep-and-Wide Web Search
artigos.titulo_pt: WebSwarm: Orquestração Recursiva Multi-Agente para Busca Web Profunda e Ampla
artigos.resumo_executivo_en: WebSwarm is a progressive recursive delegation framework that orchestrates multiple LLM-based agents for deep-and-wide web search, overcoming single-agent limitations in coverage and depth. The system dynamically instantiates agentic search nodes that can recursively delegate tasks while adaptively expanding and aggregating results. Experiments across multiple benchmarks demonstrate consistent superiority over single-agent and multi-agent baselines on complex research-oriented search tasks.
artigos.resumo_executivo_pt: WebSwarm é um framework de delegação recursiva que orquestra múltiplos agentes baseados em LLM para realizar buscas web profundas e amplas, superando limitações de agentes únicos em cobertura e profundidade. O sistema constrói dinamicamente nós de busca agenticos que podem se delegar recursivamente, expandindo e agregando resultados de forma adaptativa. Experimentos em múltiplos benchmarks demonstram consistente superioridade sobre baselines de agentes únicos e multi-agentes em tarefas complexas de pesquisa.
artigos.justificativa: WebSwarm endereça um problema crítico em sistemas de busca e pesquisa automatizada, com aplicações diretas em assistentes de IA, plataformas de pesquisa empresarial e sistemas de inteligência competitiva que demandam busca profunda e cobertura ampla de informações.

artigos.titulo: EdgeRefine: Privacy-Utility Balance for Graphs via Jaccard Sampling under Edge Differential Privacy
artigos.resumo: Graph Neural Networks (GNNs) have shown considerable success in learning from graph-structured data, but their use in privacy-sensitive areas remains difficult because graph structure can leak sensitive link information. To satisfy edge-level differential privacy, a common approach is to inject noise into all elements of the graph's adjacency matrix, thereby obfuscating the existence of any single edge. However, stronger privacy requires more noise, and excessive noise reduces utility, making the privacy-utility balance a major barrier to practical privacy-preserving graph learning.   To address this issue, we propose EdgeRefine, a local differential privacy framework that improves this trade-off through adaptive edge refinement. EdgeRefine first estimates edge-existence probabilities using Jaccard similarity and ranks edges for noisy edge removal. To ensure the sparsity and reliability of the final graph, it uses the privacy budget $ε$ to determine the ratio of true to false edges, samples them separately based on this probability ranking, and controls the total number of edges with a separate sampling rate $k$. Extensive experiments show that EdgeRefine achieves accuracy comparable to the noise-free baseline and substantially outperforms other privacy-preserving methods across datasets and GNN architectures. Under privacy budget $ε= 2.5$, EdgeRefine improves node classification accuracy over state-of-the-art baselines by 17.8\% on ACM under GAT and 19.7\% on Cora under GCN. In graph classification, it achieves an average accuracy degradation of around 5\% compared to the noise-free baseline. Under graph reconstruction attacks, EdgeRefine maintains relative absolute error levels above 1 across all privacy budgets, averaging 1.962 on Cora and 1.472 on AMAP, indicating strong resilience against privacy leakage.
artigos.titulo_en: EdgeRefine: Privacy-Utility Balance for Graphs via Jaccard Sampling under Edge Differential Privacy
artigos.titulo_pt: EdgeRefine: Equilíbrio Privacidade-Utilidade para Grafos via Amostragem Jaccard sob Privacidade Diferencial de Arestas
artigos.resumo_executivo_en: EdgeRefine is a local differential privacy framework that improves the privacy-utility trade-off in Graph Neural Networks through adaptive edge refinement using Jaccard similarity. The method estimates edge-existence probabilities, ranks edges for noisy removal, and controls the ratio of true to false edges using privacy budget ε. Experiments demonstrate that EdgeRefine achieves accuracy comparable to noise-free baselines, substantially outperforming existing privacy-preserving methods with improvements up to 19.7% in node classification and only 5% average degradation in graph classification.
artigos.resumo_executivo_pt: EdgeRefine é um framework de privacidade diferencial local que melhora o equilíbrio privacidade-utilidade em Redes Neurais de Grafos através de refinamento adaptativo de arestas usando similaridade Jaccard. O método estima probabilidades de existência de arestas, classifica-as para remoção ruidosa e controla a proporção de arestas verdadeiras versus falsas usando o orçamento de privacidade ε. Experimentos demonstram que EdgeRefine alcança acurácia comparável ao baseline sem ruído, superando significativamente métodos de preservação de privacidade existentes, com melhorias de até 19,7% em classificação de nós e degradação média de apenas 5% em classificação de grafos.
artigos.justificativa: EdgeRefine aborda um desafio crítico em aplicações reais de GNNs em domínios sensíveis à privacidade (saúde, finanças, redes sociais), oferecendo uma solução prática que mantém utilidade dos modelos enquanto garante proteção de dados através de privacidade diferencial, com resultados experimentais demonstrando viabilidade em produção.

artigos.titulo: Formal Mechanisms for Market Stability in Self-Interested Agent Societies: A Marketplace Simulation Study
artigos.resumo: Self-interested agents, left unconstrained, tend toward defection in repeated social dilemmas, causing cooperative gains from trade to collapse. This paper investigates what formal mechanisms, layered on top of unrestricted communication, are sufficient for a society of such agents to maintain market stability, and how resilient those mechanisms are to adversarial attack. We instantiate the research question as a multi-agent marketplace simulation where 18 LLM agents (DeepSeek-V3) with complementary production specialties must trade within a constrained social network to obtain utility. We conduct two experimental phases: (1) a mechanism comparison across eight conditions under progressive troll injection over 200 rounds, identifying Mediation as the top-performing mechanism; and (2) adversarial red-teaming of Mediation using iteratively prompt-optimised LLM-driven trolls, finding that the best attack (v6) reduces honest-agent utility by 13.3% but cannot collapse the market. Mediation enables recovery even under sustained adversarial pressure. We define adversarial robustness as a mechanism's ability to sustain positive honest-agent utility under optimised attack, and find that Mediation is robust: it can be bent but not broken.
artigos.titulo_en: Formal Mechanisms for Market Stability in Self-Interested Agent Societies: A Marketplace Simulation Study
artigos.titulo_pt: Mecanismos Formais para Estabilidade de Mercado em Sociedades de Agentes Autointeressados: Um Estudo de Simulação de Marketplace
artigos.resumo_executivo_en: This study investigates formal mechanisms for maintaining market stability in self-interested agent societies using 18 LLM agents (DeepSeek-V3) in a marketplace simulation. Through comparative testing of eight mechanisms under progressive troll injection and optimized adversarial attack, Mediation is identified as the most robust mechanism, limiting utility loss to only 13.3% under optimized attack. The work demonstrates that Mediation can be bent but not broken, sustaining positive utility even under sustained adversarial pressure.
artigos.resumo_executivo_pt: O estudo investiga mecanismos formais capazes de manter a estabilidade de mercado em sociedades de agentes autointeressados utilizando 18 agentes LLM (DeepSeek-V3) em simulação de marketplace. Através de testes comparativos de oito mecanismos sob injeção progressiva de trolls e ataque adversarial otimizado, identifica-se Mediação como o mecanismo mais robusto, reduzindo perdas de utilidade em apenas 13,3% sob ataque otimizado. O trabalho demonstra que Mediação pode ser flexionada mas não quebrada, sustentando utilidade positiva mesmo sob pressão adversarial contínua.
artigos.justificativa: O trabalho aborda estabilidade de mercado e resiliência a ataques adversariais em sistemas multi-agente com LLMs, com aplicações diretas em plataformas de marketplace, sistemas de negociação automatizados e ambientes de comércio digital onde a confiança e a estabilidade são críticas para operações comerciais.

artigos.titulo: Secure Decentralized Federated Learning via Gossip and Virtual Voting
artigos.resumo: Decentralized federated learning (DFL) removes the central server by letting nodes exchange model updates through peer-to-peer gossip, but existing gossip-based methods often lack provenance finality and resilience to Byzantine or lazy participants. Ledger-assisted federated learning (FL) improves auditability, yet blockchains, shards, or settlement committees can reintroduce global coordination costs that conflict with DFL locality. This paper proposes \emph{gspDAG-FL}, a secure DFL framework that derives consensus from the same gossip history used to disseminate models. Nodes exchange model payloads only with neighbors, while full nodes collect event certificates and receiver-endorsed accepted gossip proofs, reconstruct a compact Topology directed acyclic graph (DAG), and run Hashgraph-style virtual voting followed by compact full-node certificates. Finality is over unique model-origin tuples, not identical local parameter states. To improve resilience, gspDAG-FL combines payload validation, accepted-proof validation, and private semantic audit before aggregation. We formalize the adversarial setting, prove safety and conditional liveness of the control plane, and give a convergence guarantee for certified perturbed gossip under time-varying effective mixing. Experiments on MNIST classification and Penn Treebank language modeling, using fair held-out validation/audit data and networks up to \(N=100\), show that gspDAG-FL achieves learning quality close to validation-based ledger FL while reducing coordination bottlenecks, improving throughput, and maintaining high invalid-origin detection under mixed Byzantine and lazy participation.
artigos.titulo_en: Secure Decentralized Federated Learning via Gossip and Virtual Voting
artigos.titulo_pt: Aprendizado Federado Descentralizado Seguro via Gossip e Votação Virtual
artigos.resumo_executivo_en: The paper proposes gspDAG-FL, a secure decentralized federated learning framework that eliminates central servers through peer-to-peer gossip with consensus derived from the model dissemination history. The system combines payload validation, accepted-proof validation, and private semantic audit to improve resilience against Byzantine and lazy participants. Experiments on MNIST classification and language modeling demonstrate learning quality comparable to validation-based ledger FL while reducing coordination bottlenecks and improving throughput.
artigos.resumo_executivo_pt: O artigo propõe gspDAG-FL, um framework de aprendizado federado descentralizado que elimina servidores centrais através de gossip peer-to-peer com consenso derivado do histórico de disseminação de modelos. O sistema combina validação de payload, prova de gossip aceita e auditoria semântica privada para melhorar resiliência contra participantes Bizantinos e preguiçosos. Experimentos em classificação MNIST e modelagem de linguagem demonstram qualidade de aprendizado próxima ao ledger FL validado, reduzindo gargalos de coordenação e melhorando throughput.
artigos.justificativa: O framework aborda desafios críticos em aprendizado federado descentralizado, eliminando gargalos de coordenação central enquanto mantém segurança e auditabilidade, com aplicações diretas em sistemas distribuídos de ML que requerem privacidade, escalabilidade e resiliência a falhas.

artigos.titulo: Multi-Modal, Multi-Environment Machine Teaching for Robust Reward Learning
artigos.resumo: As autonomous agents are increasingly deployed across diverse operational contexts, aligning their behavior with human intent demands reward functions that remain robust to such changes rather than overfitting to any single environment. Inverse reinforcement learning (IRL) provides a principled way to infer such objectives from human feedback. However, existing analyses of optimal teaching approaches for IRL focus on single-environment, demonstration-only settings, leaving underexplored how heterogeneous feedback modalities and environment dynamics jointly constrain reward functions that generalize across multiple environments. Because demonstrations in one MDP entangle reward information with that environments specific structure, the resulting rewards frequently fail to generalize when the agent is deployed in a new setting. We first analyze how different feedback modalities constrain rewards, showing that, in the unlimited-data regime, comparisons impose strictly stronger global constraints than other modalities. Beyond this theoretical analysis, we introduce a hierarchical machine teaching algorithm for reward learning that operates across multiple MDPs. The algorithm first greedily selects informative environments that expose complementary reward constraints, then strategically queries low-cost feedback within those environments. Empirically, our method achieves substantially lower regret and stronger generalization to held-out environments than uniform teaching baselines under identical feedback budgets, demonstrating the importance of multi-environment, multi-modal teaching for learning dynamics-robust reward functions.
artigos.titulo_en: Multi-Modal, Multi-Environment Machine Teaching for Robust Reward Learning
artigos.titulo_pt: Ensino de Máquina Multi-Modal e Multi-Ambiente para Aprendizado Robusto de Funções de Recompensa
artigos.resumo_executivo_en: The paper addresses aligning autonomous agents with human intent through robust reward functions across multiple environments using inverse reinforcement learning. It provides theoretical analysis showing that comparison feedback imposes strictly stronger global constraints than other modalities on reward functions. The authors introduce a hierarchical machine teaching algorithm that selectively queries informative environments and low-cost feedback, achieving lower regret and stronger generalization to held-out environments.
artigos.resumo_executivo_pt: O artigo aborda o desafio de alinhar agentes autônomos com intenções humanas através de funções de recompensa robustas em múltiplos ambientes, utilizando aprendizado por reforço inverso. Propõe uma análise teórica sobre como diferentes modalidades de feedback (comparações, demonstrações) constrangem funções de recompensa, mostrando que comparações impõem restrições globais mais fortes. Introduz um algoritmo hierárquico de ensino de máquina que seleciona ambientes informativos e consulta feedback de baixo custo, alcançando menor arrependimento e melhor generalização em ambientes não vistos.
artigos.justificativa: O trabalho é altamente relevante para empresas que desenvolvem agentes autônomos, pois resolve o problema crítico de generalização de funções de recompensa em múltiplos ambientes operacionais, reduzindo custos de feedback humano e melhorando a robustez de sistemas de IA em produção.

artigos.titulo: UltraX: Refining Pre-Training Data at Scale with Adaptive Programmatic Editing
artigos.resumo: As available training data approaches its physical limit, gains from Scaling Laws have begun to diminish. Consequently, improving Large Language Models (LLMs) now depends less on data expansion and more on higher-quality data utilization. However, in the context of large-scale corpora, existing refinement methodologies face significant limitations in quality, efficiency, and reliability: Rule-based approaches are constrained by fixed heuristics and struggle with instance-level variations; LLM-based approaches improve quality but fail to meet the efficiency and reliability requirements of large-scale data processing. To address these challenges, we propose UltraX, a function-calling refinement framework for large-scale pre-training data that completes the editing function space by introducing insertion in addition to deletion and modification, enabling fine-grained instance-level editing. Specifically, UltraX builds a reliable program-supervision generation pipeline. In this pipeline, dataset-adaptive prompt optimization first guides an expert LLM to produce high-quality end-to-end refined texts, and Line Alignment Mapping and Dynamic Context Replacement then convert original-refined text pairs into structured program supervision. Meanwhile, UltraX improves supervision quality and stabilizes the training distribution with low-confidence example filtering and ratio-controlled sampling by operation combination. During inference and execution, it normalizes and validates model outputs through sliding-window prediction, global operation aggregation, and systematic post-processing, improving the stability and reliability of large-scale execution. Experiments show that UltraX achieves the highest average performance across all corpora and also matches or surpasses baselines with fewer training tokens, demonstrating stronger data efficiency and refinement reliability.
artigos.titulo_en: UltraX: Refining Pre-Training Data at Scale with Adaptive Programmatic Editing
artigos.titulo_pt: UltraX: Refinamento de Dados de Pré-Treinamento em Escala com Edição Programática Adaptativa
artigos.resumo_executivo_en: UltraX is a large-scale pre-training data refinement framework that uses adaptive programmatic editing to improve LLM training data quality. The system combines dataset-adaptive prompt optimization, line alignment mapping, and dynamic context replacement to generate high-quality structured supervision. Experiments demonstrate that UltraX achieves superior performance with greater data efficiency and refinement reliability compared to baselines.
artigos.resumo_executivo_pt: UltraX é um framework de refinamento de dados de pré-treinamento em larga escala que utiliza edição programática adaptativa para melhorar a qualidade dos dados de treinamento de LLMs. O sistema combina otimização de prompts adaptados ao dataset, mapeamento de alinhamento de linhas e substituição dinâmica de contexto para gerar supervisão estruturada de alta qualidade. Experimentos demonstram que UltraX alcança melhor desempenho com maior eficiência de dados e confiabilidade de refinamento comparado aos baselines.
artigos.justificativa: A qualidade dos dados de pré-treinamento é crítica para o desempenho de LLMs em produção, e UltraX oferece uma solução escalável e confiável para refinamento de dados que melhora a eficiência de treinamento e reduz custos computacionais, impactando diretamente a viabilidade econômica de desenvolvimento de modelos de linguagem.

artigos.titulo: BiSCo-LLM: Lookup-Free Binary Spherical Coding for Extreme Low-Bit Large Language Model Compression
artigos.resumo: Large language models (LLMs) are increasingly constrained by memory capacity, weight bandwidth, and checkpoint storage during deployment. Existing low-bit compression methods mainly follow two directions. Scalar or group-wise quantization is simple and compatible with efficient low-precision kernels, but its representation capacity becomes limited when the target budget approaches 2 bits per weight. Vector-quantized weight compression provides a richer block-level representation, but usually introduces explicit codebooks, index lookup, and additional storage accounting. This paper presents BiSCo-LLM, a codebook-free binary spherical coding framework for extreme low-bit LLM weight compression. The core pipeline is built on three components. First, local weight chunks are mapped onto a unit hypersphere and binarized into compact spherical codes, so that the main payload is a bit-packed sign stream rather than explicit VQ centroids. Second, a residual BSQ stage encodes the reconstruction error left by the base spherical codec, providing an explicit rate-distortion path without stored codebooks. Third, category-wise recovery distillation is performed after replacing each Transformer module category, reducing the mismatch between local weight reconstruction and assembled model behavior. A small 8-bit protected-channel path is used as an auxiliary stabilization mechanism for sensitive channels and is counted separately from the BSQ payload. The reported storage budget includes binary codes, neural decoders, protected-channel payloads, LoRA adapters, and metadata.
artigos.titulo_en: BiSCo-LLM: Lookup-Free Binary Spherical Coding for Extreme Low-Bit Large Language Model Compression
artigos.titulo_pt: BiSCo-LLM: Codificação Esférica Binária Sem Busca para Compressão Extrema de Modelos de Linguagem Grande em Baixa Precisão
artigos.resumo_executivo_en: BiSCo-LLM introduces a codebook-free binary spherical coding framework for extreme low-bit weight compression in large language models, addressing memory and bandwidth constraints during deployment. The method maps local weight chunks onto a unit hypersphere, binarizes them into compact spherical codes, and employs residual quantization to encode reconstruction errors without explicit codebooks. The approach incorporates category-wise recovery distillation and an 8-bit protected-channel mechanism for sensitive channels, enabling compression at extreme low-bit precision.
artigos.resumo_executivo_pt: BiSCo-LLM apresenta um framework de codificação esférica binária sem codebook para compressão extrema de pesos em modelos de linguagem grandes, reduzindo a memória e largura de banda durante a implantação. O método mapeia chunks de pesos locais em uma hiperesfera unitária, binariza-os em códigos esféricos compactos e utiliza uma etapa de quantização residual para codificar erros de reconstrução sem armazenar codebooks explícitos. A abordagem inclui destilação de recuperação por categoria e um caminho de canal protegido de 8 bits para estabilização de canais sensíveis, alcançando compressão em extrema baixa precisão.
artigos.justificativa: A compressão extrema de LLMs é crítica para reduzir custos de implantação, armazenamento e inferência em produção, permitindo que modelos de linguagem grandes sejam executados em dispositivos com restrições de memória e largura de banda, o que é essencial para aplicações comerciais em edge computing e ambientes com recursos limitados.

artigos.titulo: DominoTree: Conditional Tree-Structured Drafting with Domino for Speculative Decoding
artigos.resumo: Speculative decoding accelerates LLM inference by drafting several tokens and verifying them in parallel. Block-diffusion drafters such as DFlash produce   a draft block in one pass but model only per-position marginals; best-first tree methods such as DDTree expand candidate trees from those marginals. The   released Domino drafter adds a GRU-based causal correction that makes each draft token's distribution path-dependent, a structure DDTree's factorized   formulation cannot represent. We introduce DominoTree, a training-free best-first draft tree scored by Domino's conditional, non-factorized correction   along each root-to-node path, made practical by restricting the per-node correction to a candidate top-M. On Qwen3-4B across eight benchmarks, DominoTree   reaches up to 6.6x speedup over autoregressive decoding and the highest mean accept length of any evaluated method, up to 10.7 tokens per round, at every   temperature we test. DominoTree constructs its tree with a GPU-native, CUDA-graph builder that is bit-identical to a reference Python implementation, so   acceptance is unchanged, while keeping per-round tree construction cheap. With this builder as default, DominoTree wins throughput over the released   Domino decoder at every temperature, 9-10% overall on Qwen3-4B and up to +22% on Alpaca, and over DDTree/CaDDTree at every temperature we test. On Qwen3-   8B, DominoTree keeps the highest accepted length at every temperature and adds a decisive throughput win at T=0, +24% over DDTree; at higher temperature   that edge over DDTree/CaDDTree narrows to a tie and a small loss, while its Overall aggregate wins over DFlash and Domino persist.
artigos.titulo_en: DominoTree: Conditional Tree-Structured Drafting with Domino for Speculative Decoding
artigos.titulo_pt: DominoTree: Rascunho Estruturado em Árvore Condicional com Domino para Decodificação Especulativa
artigos.resumo_executivo_en: DominoTree is a speculative decoding method that accelerates LLM inference through tree-structured drafting with conditional, non-factorized correction. The method achieves up to 6.6x speedup over autoregressive decoding and mean accept length of up to 10.7 tokens per round. Implemented with a GPU-native CUDA-graph builder, DominoTree outperforms competing methods in throughput across multiple temperatures and models.
artigos.resumo_executivo_pt: DominoTree é um método de decodificação especulativa que acelera a inferência de LLMs através de rascunhos estruturados em árvore com correção condicional não-fatorizada. O método alcança até 6,6x de aceleração sobre decodificação autorregressiva e comprimento médio de aceitação de até 10,7 tokens por rodada. Implementado com construtor CUDA-nativo, DominoTree supera métodos concorrentes em throughput em múltiplas temperaturas e modelos.
artigos.justificativa: A aceleração significativa da inferência de LLMs (até 6,6x) tem impacto direto na redução de latência e custos computacionais em aplicações de produção, tornando modelos maiores viáveis economicamente para serviços em tempo real.

artigos.titulo: Steering Neural Network Training through Interpretable Constraints Based on Partial Dependence
artigos.resumo: Over the last few years, there has been an increased interest in making machine learning models more interpretable. Although a great deal of effort goes into developing techniques for interpreting the interactions learned by a given model, fewer studies focus on assessing the quality of such explanations. Even fewer focus on how to adjust the model to produce explanations faithful to prior knowledge, a process known as explanation-guided learning. Furthermore, most approaches in this area focus on classification problems and usually assume prior knowledge about which input features or regions are most important. In this work, we introduce a new approach to steering neural networks based on partial dependence, such that their average response to certain features aligns with specific functional domain knowledge about the problem. We empirically demonstrate on a range of regression problems, including dynamical systems forecasting, that models whose training has been controlled using our method perform better than unconstrained models and are more data-efficient. Moreover, we highlight that interpretations obtained from the former actually align with the user-provided knowledge, whereas those obtained from the latter do not.
artigos.titulo_en: Steering Neural Network Training through Interpretable Constraints Based on Partial Dependence
artigos.titulo_pt: Direcionamento do Treinamento de Redes Neurais através de Restrições Interpretáveis Baseadas em Dependência Parcial
artigos.resumo_executivo_en: The work introduces a novel approach to steer neural network training through partial dependence-based constraints, aligning model responses with prior domain knowledge. Models trained with this method demonstrate superior performance, improved data efficiency, and interpretations that faithfully align with user-provided knowledge. The technique is validated across regression problems, including dynamical systems forecasting.
artigos.resumo_executivo_pt: O trabalho apresenta uma abordagem inovadora para orientar o treinamento de redes neurais através de restrições baseadas em dependência parcial, alinhando as respostas do modelo com conhecimento de domínio prévio. Os modelos treinados com este método demonstram melhor desempenho, maior eficiência de dados e interpretações que se alinham fielmente com o conhecimento fornecido pelo usuário. A técnica é validada em problemas de regressão, incluindo previsão de sistemas dinâmicos.
artigos.justificativa: A abordagem oferece um mecanismo prático para incorporar conhecimento de domínio no treinamento de modelos, melhorando tanto a interpretabilidade quanto a eficiência de dados, o que é crítico para aplicações em setores regulados e onde a confiabilidade das explicações é essencial.

artigos.titulo: Native Video-Action Pretraining for Generalizable Robot Control
artigos.resumo: The advent of video-action models offers a promising path for robot control. Nevertheless, we argue that repurposing video generative models designed for digital content creation is inherently inadequate for physical environments. To bridge this gap, we present LingBot-VA 2.0, a video-action foundation model built from the ground up for embodiment. Four core design principles showcase its evolution from LingBot-VA. (1) Departing from traditional reconstruction-focused VAEs, we introduce a semantic visual-action tokenizer, which aligns visual representations with both semantics and actions, improving instruction following and action precision in subsequent policy learning. (2) Given the strictly causal nature of temporal dynamics, we adopt a causal pretraining paradigm, training from scratch to circumvent the catastrophic forgetting that frequently occurs when adapting bidirectional architectures. (3) To meet the demands of high-frequency inference, our model employs a sparse MoE backbone, expanding model capacity without compromising efficiency. (4) Real-time closed-loop control is realized through an enhanced asynchronous inference scheme, which predicts future latents in parallel with action execution while re-grounding each rollout on the latest observation via learned forward dynamics. Real-world deployment validates LingBot-VA 2.0 as a robust foundation model, as evidenced by its few-shot generalization across complex manipulation tasks.
artigos.titulo_en: Native Video-Action Pretraining for Generalizable Robot Control
artigos.titulo_pt: Pré-treinamento Nativo de Vídeo-Ação para Controle Robótico Generalizável
artigos.resumo_executivo_en: LingBot-VA 2.0 is a video-action foundation model built specifically for robotics, addressing limitations of video generative models adapted from digital content creation. The model introduces a semantic visual-action tokenizer, adopts causal pretraining to prevent catastrophic forgetting, employs sparse MoE architecture for real-time efficiency, and implements closed-loop control with enhanced asynchronous inference. Real-world deployment demonstrates robust few-shot generalization across complex manipulation tasks.
artigos.resumo_executivo_pt: LingBot-VA 2.0 é um modelo de fundação vídeo-ação desenvolvido especificamente para robótica, superando limitações de modelos generativos de vídeo adaptados de aplicações de criação de conteúdo digital. O modelo introduz um tokenizador visual-ação semântico, adota pré-treinamento causal para evitar esquecimento catastrófico, utiliza arquitetura MoE esparsa para eficiência em tempo real e implementa controle em malha fechada com inferência assíncrona aprimorada. Validação em ambiente real demonstra generalização robusta em tarefas de manipulação complexas com poucos exemplos.
artigos.justificativa: O trabalho apresenta um modelo de fundação pronto para produção que resolve desafios críticos de controle robótico generalizado, com validação em ambiente real e potencial de transformar aplicações de automação industrial e manipulação robótica em larga escala.

artigos.titulo: The complexities of patient-centred conversational artificial intelligence
artigos.resumo: Consumer-facing health chatbots powered by large language models (LLMs) are increasingly used for symptom assessment. However, chatbot development and evaluation often rely on cooperative, articulate, simulated patients. We analysed 2,053 real patient-chatbot conversations and found that communication patterns and expression of emotions vary widely across users. We developed a patient simulator that separately models clinical content, emotional state, conversational strategy, and communication style. In a Turing-inspired evaluation of realism with 15 human graders, simulated conversations were nearly indistinguishable from real ones, with human graders achieving an accuracy of 55%. We used five distinct patient personae, across 1,164 clinician-graded cases, to evaluate the performance of four LLMs in urgency assessment. We found that communication style can significantly alter triage outcomes. Patient-centred conversational artificial intelligence must accommodate communication diversity: systems designed for idealised, rather than realistic, interactions risk underperforming and amplifying health disparities when deployed in the real world.
artigos.titulo_en: The complexities of patient-centred conversational artificial intelligence
artigos.titulo_pt: As complexidades da inteligência artificial conversacional centrada no paciente
artigos.resumo_executivo_en: The paper analyzes 2,053 real patient-chatbot conversations powered by LLMs, revealing that communication patterns and emotional expression vary significantly across users. The authors developed a patient simulator modeling clinical content, emotional state, conversational strategy, and communication style, achieving 55% accuracy in realism evaluation with 15 human graders. The study demonstrates that communication style can significantly alter triage outcomes, highlighting that conversational AI systems must accommodate communication diversity to avoid amplifying health disparities.
artigos.resumo_executivo_pt: O artigo analisa 2.053 conversas reais entre pacientes e chatbots de saúde baseados em LLMs, revelando que padrões de comunicação e expressão emocional variam significativamente entre usuários. Os autores desenvolveram um simulador de pacientes que modela conteúdo clínico, estado emocional, estratégia conversacional e estilo de comunicação, alcançando 55% de acurácia em avaliação de realismo com 15 avaliadores humanos. O estudo demonstra que o estilo de comunicação pode alterar significativamente os resultados de triagem, evidenciando que sistemas de IA conversacional devem acomodar diversidade comunicativa para evitar amplificar disparidades de saúde.
artigos.justificativa: O trabalho aborda desafios críticos na implementação de chatbots de saúde em produção, demonstrando que sistemas atuais podem amplificar disparidades de saúde quando não consideram a diversidade comunicativa real dos pacientes. Essa pesquisa é essencial para empresas de healthtech e provedores de saúde que desenvolvem ou implementam soluções de IA conversacional.

artigos.titulo: When Structured Sparse Autoencoders Learn Consistent Concepts Across Modalities
artigos.resumo: Sparse autoencoders (SAEs) have emerged as a promising technique for mechanistic interpretability by learning a set of sparse latent features in large models, each of which encodes a distinct concept. However, in vision-language models (VLMs), vanilla SAEs struggle to learn modality-consistent concepts, with concepts often exhibiting fragmented coverage (i.e., disjoint regions) in the visual modality. To address this challenge, we propose a Structured Sparse AutoEncoder ($S^2AE$) that enforces concept consistency from both semantic and spatial perspectives in the visual modality. Specifically, we group image patches based on Transformer attention similarity and spatial proximity, and introduce a structured sparsity regularization when training the vanilla SAE. The regularization consists of exclusive sparsity for inter-group concept disentanglement and group sparsity for intra-group concept consistency, which drives the latent neurons by SAEs to specialize in distinct, semantically grounded concepts. Evaluated on the \texttt{Qwen2.5-VL-7B-Instruct} model, the method achieves 6.06% average improvement in semantic alignment (mIoU) and 60.81 in representational efficiency (lower l0 norm) while maintaining near-perfect reconstruction fidelity with an Explained Variance above 99%. Cross-modal analysis further demonstrates that $S^2AE$ enhances neuronal monosemanticity by this visual structural prior, achieving a 3.08% average gain in semantic consistency and a 2.37% average gain in monosemanticity scores for both modalities of multimodal features, thereby fostering more coherent and disentangled representations.
artigos.titulo_en: When Structured Sparse Autoencoders Learn Consistent Concepts Across Modalities
artigos.titulo_pt: Quando Autoencoders Esparsos Estruturados Aprendem Conceitos Consistentes Entre Modalidades
artigos.resumo_executivo_en: The paper proposes Structured Sparse AutoEncoders (S²AE) to improve mechanistic interpretability in vision-language models by learning consistent concepts across modalities through structured sparsity regularization. The method achieves 6.06% improvement in semantic alignment and 60.81 in representational efficiency while maintaining reconstruction fidelity above 99%. Cross-modal analysis demonstrates gains of 3.08% in semantic consistency and 2.37% in monosemanticity scores for both modalities.
artigos.resumo_executivo_pt: O artigo propõe Structured Sparse AutoEncoders (S²AE) para melhorar a interpretabilidade mecânica em modelos visão-linguagem, aprendendo conceitos consistentes entre modalidades através de regularização de esparsidade estruturada. O método alcança 6,06% de melhoria em alinhamento semântico e 60,81 em eficiência representacional, mantendo fidelidade de reconstrução acima de 99%. A análise cross-modal demonstra ganhos de 3,08% em consistência semântica e 2,37% em monosemanticity para ambas as modalidades.
artigos.justificativa: A interpretabilidade mecânica de modelos visão-linguagem é crítica para aplicações empresariais que exigem transparência e confiabilidade. O método S²AE oferece melhorias significativas em consistência conceitual e eficiência representacional, com potencial direto para otimização de modelos multimodais em produção.

artigos.titulo: Towards Precision Therapy in Hepatocellular Carcinoma: A Clinical-Reasoning LLM for Risk Stratification and Treatment Guidance
artigos.resumo: Hepatocellular carcinoma (HCC) is a common malignancy and a leading cause of cancer-related mortality. Current guidelines and staging systems provide coarse categories, but often miss within-stage heterogeneity and the clinical context in electronic medical records (EMRs). We present HCC-STAR (Hepatocellular Carcinoma Staging, Treatment And pRognosis), a clinically aligned large language model that reads routine EMR narratives and jointly outputs risk score-based staging, ranked guideline-consistent treatments with evidence-based rationales, and individualized survival estimates. We curated about 30,000 HCC cases from SEER and expanded them into EMR-style narrative training data using a clinician-validated, prompt-based augmentation workflow. On this corpus, we developed a knowledge-aligned reasoning framework optimized with a step-verifiable composite reward, moving beyond text-level memorization of clinical guidelines. In a multi-center cohort of 6,668 patients from 12 hospitals in China, HCC-STAR achieved state-of-the-art performance in treatment recommendation and risk stratification compared with clinical guidelines and competitive models, including GPT-5 and Gemini-2.5 Pro. Hypothetical overall-survival analysis showed a median survival of 51 months under adherence to HCC-STAR recommendations, compared with 29 and 32 months under BCLC and CNLC. In clinician-centric evaluations, blinded hepatobiliary specialists rated HCC-STAR's reasoning and evidence-based justifications as trustworthy. The model surpassed resident and attending physicians in treatment accuracy and helped physicians make more accurate decisions faster when used as an assistant. These findings support HCC-STAR as a reliable and verifiable decision-support system for risk stratification and precision therapy in HCC.
artigos.titulo_en: Towards Precision Therapy in Hepatocellular Carcinoma: A Clinical-Reasoning LLM for Risk Stratification and Treatment Guidance
artigos.titulo_pt: Rumo à Terapia de Precisão em Carcinoma Hepatocelular: Um LLM com Raciocínio Clínico para Estratificação de Risco e Orientação de Tratamento
artigos.resumo_executivo_en: HCC-STAR is a clinically aligned large language model that reads electronic medical record narratives and provides risk stratification, guideline-consistent treatment recommendations, and individualized survival estimates. Developed with 30,000 hepatocellular carcinoma cases expanded into EMR-style narratives, the model achieved superior performance compared to clinical guidelines and competitive models in a multi-center cohort of 6,668 patients. The system demonstrated trustworthiness in clinical evaluations, surpassing resident and attending physicians in treatment accuracy and assisting clinicians in making faster and more accurate decisions.
artigos.resumo_executivo_pt: HCC-STAR é um modelo de linguagem grande clinicamente alinhado que lê narrativas de registros médicos eletrônicos (EMRs) e fornece estratificação de risco, recomendações de tratamento baseadas em diretrizes e estimativas de sobrevida individualizadas. Desenvolvido com dados de 30.000 casos de carcinoma hepatocelular expandidos em narrativas estilo EMR, o modelo alcançou desempenho superior em comparação com diretrizes clínicas e modelos competitivos em uma coorte multi-centro de 6.668 pacientes. O sistema demonstrou confiabilidade em avaliações clínicas, superando médicos residentes e assistentes em precisão de tratamento e auxiliando profissionais a tomar decisões mais precisas e rápidas.

artigos.titulo: It Takes a MAESTRO To Prune Bad Experts
artigos.resumo: Sparsely-activated Mixture-of-Experts (MoE) language models achieve remarkable inference efficiency by activating only a small fraction of parameters per token, yet their full expert banks reside in memory at all times, creating a prohibitive deployment bottleneck. Existing structured pruning methods, largely designed for dense transformers, assess expert importance using locally derived heuristics that are blind to the interdependent nature of MoE routing. We introduce MAESTRO (Markov-chain Approximated Expert Sparsification via Transition-based ROuting), a structured pruning framework designed for MoE architectures that models autoregressive expert activation trajectories as Ergodic Markov chains whose stationary distributions encode cross-layer dependencies, yielding a globally aware importance heuristic. Evaluated across five diverse domains including Safety, Bias, and Ethics, MAESTRO outperforms state-of-the-art baselines by up to 10.61% in average performance retention under a strict 50% compression regime, while exhibiting substantially lower cross-task variance, indicating that global, routing-congruent pruning produces models that generalize more consistently across heterogeneous tasks.
artigos.titulo_en: It Takes a MAESTRO To Prune Bad Experts
artigos.titulo_pt: É Preciso um MAESTRO para Podar Especialistas Ruins
