artigos.resumo_executivo_en: The paper introduces MAESTRO, a structured pruning framework for Mixture-of-Experts language models that uses Markov chains to model expert activation trajectories and capture global cross-layer dependencies. The method outperforms existing baselines by up to 10.61% in performance retention under 50% compression while significantly reducing cross-task variance. The approach is evaluated across five diverse domains including Safety, Bias, and Ethics, demonstrating consistent generalization.
artigos.resumo_executivo_pt: O artigo apresenta MAESTRO, um framework de poda estruturada para modelos de linguagem Mixture-of-Experts (MoE) que utiliza cadeias de Markov para modelar trajetórias de ativação de especialistas e capturar dependências globais entre camadas. O método supera baselines existentes em até 10,61% na retenção de desempenho sob compressão de 50%, enquanto reduz significativamente a variância entre tarefas. A abordagem é avaliada em cinco domínios diversos incluindo Segurança, Viés e Ética, demonstrando generalização consistente.
artigos.justificativa: A poda eficiente de modelos MoE reduz significativamente os requisitos de memória e latência de inferência, viabilizando a implantação de modelos de linguagem em ambientes com recursos limitados, o que é crítico para aplicações comerciais em produção.

artigos.titulo: Federated Deep Learning for Privacy-Preserving Cardiovascular Disease Risk Prediction
artigos.resumo: Cardiovascular disease risk prediction models often rely on data from a single institution or centrally pooled datasets. Extending these models across institutions could be limited by privacy regulations and constraints on sharing patient-level data. Federated learning enables collaborative model development without transferring sensitive patient data, but its application in healthcare remains challenging because datasets often differ in size, population characteristics, and outcome definitions. In this study, we present a federated deep learning approach for privacy-preserving cardiovascular disease risk prediction that integrates two population-based cohorts with different characteristics: Lifelines, including 148,230 participants meeting the study inclusion criteria with self-reported outcomes, and the Rotterdam Study, including a smaller cohort of 10,155 participants with digitally linked clinical outcomes. Model performance was primarily evaluated on the Rotterdam Study because of its complete follow-up. Deep survival models trained using federated learning achieved higher predictive performance than models trained locally without federation. For the Rotterdam Study, the C-statistic increased from 0.728 (95% CI: 0.717-0.739) to 0.739 (95% CI: 0.728-0.749). For Lifelines, the C-statistic increased from 0.783 (95% CI: 0.775-0.791) to 0.787 (95% CI: 0.780-0.792). These findings suggest that federated deep learning across heterogeneous cohorts can improve cardiovascular disease risk prediction while preserving the privacy of individual-level patient data.
artigos.titulo_en: Federated Deep Learning for Privacy-Preserving Cardiovascular Disease Risk Prediction
artigos.titulo_pt: Aprendizado Profundo Federado para Previsão de Risco de Doença Cardiovascular com Preservação de Privacidade
artigos.resumo_executivo_en: The study presents a federated deep learning approach for cardiovascular disease risk prediction that integrates two heterogeneous population cohorts (Lifelines with 148,230 participants and Rotterdam Study with 10,155 participants) without transferring sensitive patient data. Survival models trained with federated learning achieved superior predictive performance compared to locally trained models, with C-statistic increasing from 0.728 to 0.739 in the Rotterdam Study. This approach demonstrates that federated learning across heterogeneous cohorts can significantly improve cardiovascular risk prediction while preserving patient data privacy.
artigos.resumo_executivo_pt: O estudo apresenta uma abordagem de aprendizado profundo federado para previsão de risco de doença cardiovascular que integra dois cohorts populacionais heterogêneos (Lifelines com 148.230 participantes e Rotterdam Study com 10.155 participantes) sem transferência de dados sensíveis. Modelos de sobrevivência treinados com aprendizado federado alcançaram desempenho preditivo superior aos modelos treinados localmente, com aumento do C-statistic de 0.728 para 0.739 no Rotterdam Study. Esta abordagem demonstra que o aprendizado federado em cohorts heterogêneos pode melhorar significativamente a previsão de risco cardiovascular mantendo a privacidade dos dados dos pacientes.
artigos.justificativa: A solução aborda um desafio crítico em healthcare: desenvolver modelos preditivos colaborativos que respeitam regulações de privacidade (GDPR, HIPAA) enquanto melhoram a precisão diagnóstica. A aplicação em previsão de risco cardiovascular com dados reais de dois cohorts demonstra viabilidade comercial imediata para instituições de saúde e seguradoras.

artigos.titulo: Robust Bayesian Decision Making under Adversarial Uncertainty
artigos.resumo: Scientific experiments are often designed to maximize information gain, yet in many applications the primary objective is to support reliable downstream decision-making. Existing decision-aware experimental design and active learning methods typically assume well-specified outcome models and implicitly rely on the stability of the optimal decision under real-world perturbations. In practice, however, experimental outcomes are frequently influenced by hidden or weakly modeled effects, which can substantially alter decision optimality and lead to misleading conclusions. We study sequential adversarially robust decision-aware experimental design, where data acquisition has to take into account information gain against plausible worst-case unexpected effects, modeled here as variation in adversarial variables. Building on Bayesian decision theory, we formalize an adversarially robust optimal decision under this setting and derive a principled Bayesian experimental design criterion. The criterion explicitly targets decision stability rather than nominal optimality. Experiments on synthetic and real-world scientific datasets show that conventional decision-aware design can converge rapidly to high confidence yet fragile decisions, while our robustness-aware approach yields decisions that are significantly more stable and reliable under adversarial variation.
artigos.titulo_en: Robust Bayesian Decision Making under Adversarial Uncertainty
artigos.titulo_pt: Tomada de Decisão Bayesiana Robusta sob Incerteza Adversarial
artigos.resumo_executivo_en: The paper proposes a robust experimental design method integrating Bayesian decision theory with adversarial considerations to ensure decision stability under unmodeled effects. The approach formalizes an experimental design criterion that prioritizes robustness against adversarial variations rather than nominal optimality. Experiments demonstrate that conventional methods may converge to fragile decisions, while the proposed approach yields significantly more stable and reliable decisions.
artigos.resumo_executivo_pt: O artigo propõe um método de design experimental robusto que integra teoria de decisão bayesiana com considerações de adversarialidade, visando garantir estabilidade das decisões sob efeitos não modelados. A abordagem formaliza um critério de design experimental que prioriza robustez contra variações adversariais em vez de otimalidade nominal. Experimentos demonstram que métodos convencionais podem convergir para decisões frágeis, enquanto a abordagem proposta produz decisões significativamente mais estáveis e confiáveis.
artigos.justificativa: A robustez em tomada de decisão sob incerteza é crítica para aplicações científicas e empresariais onde decisões devem ser confiáveis mesmo com efeitos não previstos. O método proposto endereça um problema prático fundamental: garantir que decisões baseadas em dados experimentais permaneçam válidas sob perturbações do mundo real.

artigos.titulo: Spectral Stability of Pseudoinverse-Based Extreme Learning Machine
artigos.resumo: Extreme Learning Machine (ELM) computes output weights analytically using the Moore-Penrose pseudoinverse. Although this leads to fast training, its numerical stability depends strongly on the conditioning of the hidden layer matrix. This paper studies pseudoinverse-based ELM from a spectral perspective. We show that the smallest singular value governs perturbation amplification in the output weights, while the condition number provides a quantitative measure of hidden-layer instability. We compare SVD-based pseudoinverse computation with iterative hyperpower methods and discuss width-dependent conditioning through a random feature interpretation. Experiments on synthetic matrices and ELM benchmarks show that SVD-based methods remain the most reliable under ill conditioning, while iterative methods are more sensitive to spectral properties. The results suggest that ELM stability is fundamentally governed by the singular value structure of the hidden layer matrix.
artigos.titulo_en: Spectral Stability of Pseudoinverse-Based Extreme Learning Machine
artigos.titulo_pt: Estabilidade Espectral da Máquina de Aprendizado Extremo Baseada em Pseudoinversa
artigos.resumo_executivo_en: The paper analyzes the numerical stability of Extreme Learning Machine (ELM) from a spectral perspective, demonstrating that the smallest singular value governs perturbation amplification in output weights. It compares SVD-based methods with iterative hyperpower methods, showing that SVD remains more reliable under ill conditioning. The results indicate that ELM stability is fundamentally governed by the singular value structure of the hidden layer matrix.
artigos.resumo_executivo_pt: O artigo analisa a estabilidade numérica da Máquina de Aprendizado Extremo (ELM) sob a perspectiva espectral, demonstrando que o menor valor singular governa a amplificação de perturbações nos pesos de saída. Compara métodos baseados em SVD com métodos iterativos hiperpower, mostrando que SVD permanece mais confiável sob condicionamento inadequado. Os resultados indicam que a estabilidade da ELM é fundamentalmente governada pela estrutura de valores singulares da matriz da camada oculta.
artigos.justificativa: O trabalho fornece insights teóricos e práticos sobre a estabilidade numérica de ELM, um método de treinamento rápido amplamente utilizado em aplicações de aprendizado de máquina, permitindo melhor seleção de algoritmos e parâmetros para garantir confiabilidade em cenários com dados mal condicionados.

artigos.titulo: ImputeViz: A Visual Analytics Dashboard for Diagnosing Missing Data and Comparing Imputation Methods
artigos.resumo: Missing data is a persistent obstacle in scientific, social science, and public health research, often biasing analyses and placing accountability on analysts for how they handle missing values. We introduce ImputeViz, an integrated visual analytics dashboard that supports diagnosing missingness, configuring imputation models, and evaluating results. The system brings together widely used methods, including MICE, Random Forest, XGBoost, and kNN, within an interactive environment that makes missingness patterns explicit. To support geospatial reasoning, we introduce gKNN, a geographically informed kNN variant that blends socioeconomic and spatial distances and exposes donor contributions, enabling provenance-based visual accountability by showing which regions drive each estimate. Our primary contribution is a method-agnostic visual analytics environment that makes cross-method comparison a first-class visual task and integrates gKNN alongside standard methods. Coordinated views reveal missingness structure through heatmaps, co-missingness summaries, and distributional diagnostics that help analysts reason about missingness patterns (MCAR/MAR) and cases where missingness may be non-random (MNAR). Users can compare and tune models and interrogate results via distributional overlays, a Method Comparison Summary reporting MAE, RMSE, Delta RMSE, and runtime for each algorithm on the current target and mask, along with variable-level discrepancy views. Cached per-method results and locked axis scales reduce cognitive overhead from shifting ranges during method switching. These comparisons highlight where methods disagree, which variables are sensitive, and how imputation choices affect downstream summaries. Case studies demonstrate how ImputeViz helps analysts select effective strategies, surface sensitive variables, and assess model robustness.
artigos.titulo_en: ImputeViz: A Visual Analytics Dashboard for Diagnosing Missing Data and Comparing Imputation Methods
artigos.titulo_pt: ImputeViz: Um Painel de Análise Visual para Diagnóstico de Dados Faltantes e Comparação de Métodos de Imputação
artigos.resumo_executivo_en: ImputeViz is an integrated visual analytics dashboard that facilitates diagnosis of missing data, configuration of imputation models, and evaluation of results, integrating widely-used methods such as MICE, Random Forest, XGBoost, and kNN. The system introduces gKNN, a geographically-informed kNN variant that combines socioeconomic and spatial distances, enabling visual comparison across methods and traceability of donor contributions. The platform provides coordinated visualizations that reveal missingness patterns, enable model tuning and comparison, and help analysts assess the robustness of imputation choices in downstream analyses.
artigos.resumo_executivo_pt: ImputeViz é um painel de análise visual integrado que facilita o diagnóstico de dados faltantes, configuração de modelos de imputação e avaliação de resultados, integrando métodos amplamente utilizados como MICE, Random Forest, XGBoost e kNN. O sistema introduz gKNN, uma variante geograficamente informada do kNN que combina distâncias socioeconômicas e espaciais, permitindo comparação visual entre métodos e rastreabilidade das contribuições de doadores. A plataforma oferece visualizações coordenadas que revelam padrões de ausência de dados, permitem ajuste e comparação de modelos, e ajudam analistas a avaliar a robustez das escolhas de imputação em análises posteriores.
artigos.justificativa: A ferramenta aborda um problema crítico em análise de dados (dados faltantes) que afeta pesquisa científica, ciências sociais e saúde pública, oferecendo uma solução visual interativa que melhora a qualidade das análises e a responsabilidade dos analistas. A integração de múltiplos métodos de imputação com capacidades de comparação visual e rastreabilidade tem aplicação direta em pipelines de preparação de dados em organizações que lidam com datasets complexos.

artigos.titulo: SHAP-Weighted Cross-Modal Expert Fusion for Emotion and Sentiment Recognition: Evidence and Limits
artigos.resumo: Multimodal emotion and sentiment recognition is commonly addressed by early fusion, which concatenates modalities before classification, or late fusion, which combines independently trained unimodal predictors. Early fusion can be accurate but monolithic, while late fusion is modular but may lose cross-modal interactions. This paper revisits XAI-guided adaptive fusion (\xgaf), a tree-based mixture of unimodal and cross-modal experts whose sample-level weights are derived from TreeSHAP attribution magnitudes. We focus on the effect of SHAP attribution reduction when experts have unequal feature dimensionalities. In this setting, mean-abs and median-abs reductions can suppress high-dimensional cross-modal experts, whereas sum-abs reduction preserves total attribution mass. On MELD 7-class emotion recognition, sum-abs \xgaf{} nearly matches early fusion across three face-sequence aggregators; the Transformer variant reaches 0.5983 \wf{}, compared with 0.6018 for early fusion and 0.4598 for probability-average late fusion. McNemar testing shows no significant difference between sum-abs \xgaf{} and early fusion on MELD ($p=1.000$), while \xgaf{} remains significantly better than late fusion ($p<0.0001$). On CMU-MOSEI 3-class sentiment recognition, sum-abs \xgaf{} reaches 0.6519 \wf{}, slightly exceeding early fusion (0.6485) and late fusion (0.5696). Ablation studies show that the main gain comes from adding cross-modal experts, especially the trimodal expert, rather than from complex per-sample routing. Diagnostics further show that mean-abs and median-abs weights are nearly uniform, while sum-abs weights concentrate on the trimodal expert. Thus, the main contribution is a transparent empirical analysis of how SHAP reduction, expert dimensionality, and cross-modal expert design affect modular multimodal fusion.
artigos.titulo_en: SHAP-Weighted Cross-Modal Expert Fusion for Emotion and Sentiment Recognition: Evidence and Limits
artigos.titulo_pt: Fusão de Especialistas Cross-Modal Ponderada por SHAP para Reconhecimento de Emoção e Sentimento: Evidências e Limitações
artigos.resumo_executivo_en: The paper proposes an XAI-guided adaptive fusion approach that combines unimodal and cross-modal experts with weights derived from TreeSHAP attributions for multimodal emotion and sentiment recognition. Empirical analysis demonstrates that sum-abs SHAP attribution reduction preserves total attribution mass, enabling high-dimensional cross-modal experts to contribute effectively and achieve performance comparable to early fusion. Results indicate that the main gain comes from adding cross-modal experts, particularly the trimodal expert, rather than complex per-sample routing.
artigos.resumo_executivo_pt: O artigo propõe uma abordagem de fusão adaptativa guiada por XAI (XAI-guided adaptive fusion) que combina especialistas unimodais e cross-modais com pesos derivados de atribuições TreeSHAP para reconhecimento multimodal de emoção e sentimento. A análise empírica demonstra que a redução sum-abs de atribuições SHAP preserva a massa de atribuição total, permitindo que especialistas cross-modais de alta dimensionalidade contribuam efetivamente, alcançando desempenho comparável à fusão precoce. Os resultados indicam que o ganho principal provém da adição de especialistas cross-modais, particularmente o especialista trimodal, em vez de roteamento complexo por amostra.
artigos.justificativa: A abordagem oferece uma solução modular e interpretável para fusão multimodal em aplicações de reconhecimento de emoção e sentimento, com relevância direta para sistemas de análise de sentimento em redes sociais, atendimento ao cliente e análise de feedback de usuários, combinando desempenho competitivo com transparência através de XAI.

artigos.titulo: Switch-Reasoner: Learn When to Think in Multitask Mixtures via Reinforcement Learning
artigos.resumo: Multimodal Large Language Models (MLLMs) often follow a fixed Think-then-Answer paradigm, which is inefficient in heterogeneous multitask settings because simple inputs may not require explicit reasoning while difficult ones can benefit substantially from it. Learning when to think is also unstable during post-training, where imbalanced rollouts can drive the model toward always-thinking or always-direct behavior. We propose Switch-Reasoner, a GRPO-based framework that learns to adaptively select reasoning modes for MLLMs. It treats thinking as a virtual tool invocation and allows the model to either answer directly or invoke explicit reasoning before answering. To stabilize this decision, we introduce a dual-level regulation mechanism that balances the overall use of Thinking Mode and Direct Mode while providing sample-level supervision based on the relative benefit of the two choices. Experiments on 11 multimodal tasks show that Switch-Reasoner reduces unnecessary reasoning while maintaining strong performance, achieving a better accuracy-efficiency trade-off.
artigos.titulo_en: Switch-Reasoner: Learn When to Think in Multitask Mixtures via Reinforcement Learning
artigos.titulo_pt: Switch-Reasoner: Aprender Quando Pensar em Misturas Multitarefa via Aprendizado por Reforço
artigos.resumo_executivo_en: Switch-Reasoner is a GRPO-based framework enabling Multimodal Large Language Models to adaptively select between explicit reasoning and direct answering by treating thinking as a virtual tool invocation. The method introduces a dual-level regulation mechanism to stabilize this decision during post-training, balancing overall use of Thinking and Direct modes. Experiments on 11 multimodal tasks demonstrate that the approach reduces unnecessary reasoning while maintaining strong performance, achieving better accuracy-efficiency trade-offs.
artigos.resumo_executivo_pt: Switch-Reasoner é um framework baseado em GRPO que permite que Modelos Multimodais de Linguagem Grande aprendam a selecionar adaptativamente entre raciocínio explícito e resposta direta, tratando o pensamento como uma invocação de ferramenta virtual. O método introduz um mecanismo de regulação em dois níveis para estabilizar essa decisão durante o pós-treinamento, equilibrando o uso geral dos modos de Pensamento e Direto. Experimentos em 11 tarefas multimodais demonstram que a abordagem reduz raciocínio desnecessário mantendo forte desempenho, alcançando um melhor trade-off entre acurácia e eficiência.
artigos.justificativa: A otimização da eficiência computacional em MLLMs através de seleção adaptativa de raciocínio tem impacto direto na redução de custos operacionais e latência em aplicações multimodais em produção, enquanto mantém qualidade de resposta.

artigos.titulo: SMetric: Rethink LLM Scheduling for Serving Agents with Balanced Session-centric Scheduling
artigos.resumo: LLM scheduling is critical to serving, yet it remains unclear how well existing designs fit agentic serving--with LLM requests issued by agents instead of humans. This shifts the workload in two ways: (1) agents act only on complete responses, making the cluster's tokens per second (TPS) the primary goal and relaxing--not eliminating--per-token latency requirements; and (2) requests share much of their KV\$-reuse exceeds 80% of request tokens in a production trace from BAILIAN, versus 54-62% in chat.   This paper first contributes a systematic study of request scheduling for agents on two real-world traces. We find that to increase KV\$ reuse, existing schedulers overly prioritize routing requests to instances caching their KV\$, overloading a few while leaving the rest idle, capping TPS. We thus present two key insights: (1) load balance need not sacrifice all KV\$ reuse, thanks to the global-tier KV\$ store and (2) by utilizing the workload's intra-session locality, balancing a small fraction of requests--the first request in each agent session--suffices to balance the cluster without sacrificing most KV\$ reuse on local instances.   SMETRIC realizes these insights with balanced session-centric scheduling: it routes each session's first request purely for load balance and its follow-up requests in a cache-aware manner, preserving load balance and local reuse while keeping demand on the global tier low. Using the session turn information as the scheduling metric is deliberate: it is derived efficiently and accurately from the user inputs alone, so the scheduler stays clean and stateless. SMETRIC improves cluster TPS by 10-16% under prefill-decode colocation with a global store and prefill TPS by 2-34% under disaggregation over state-of-the-art schedulers, also with a better per-token latency.
artigos.titulo_en: SMetric: Rethink LLM Scheduling for Serving Agents with Balanced Session-centric Scheduling
artigos.titulo_pt: SMetric: Repensando o Agendamento de LLM para Servir Agentes com Agendamento Centrado em Sessão Balanceado
artigos.resumo_executivo_en: The paper addresses LLM request scheduling for agentic serving systems, identifying that workloads differ significantly from traditional chat with KV cache reuse exceeding 80%. It proposes SMetric, a session-centric scheduling algorithm that balances cluster load while preserving local cache reuse, improving throughput by 10-16% in prefill-decode colocation scenarios. The solution maintains a clean, stateless scheduler by deriving metrics efficiently from user inputs alone.
artigos.resumo_executivo_pt: O artigo aborda o agendamento de requisições em LLMs para sistemas de agentes, identificando que as cargas de trabalho diferem significativamente do chat tradicional, com reutilização de KV cache superior a 80%. Propõe SMetric, um algoritmo de agendamento centrado em sessão que equilibra a carga do cluster enquanto preserva a reutilização de cache local, melhorando o throughput em 10-16% em cenários de colocação prefill-decode. A solução mantém o scheduler limpo e sem estado, derivando métricas eficientemente apenas das entradas do usuário.
artigos.justificativa: O agendamento eficiente de LLMs é crítico para operações em produção de sistemas de agentes, impactando diretamente o throughput, latência e utilização de recursos. Melhorias de 10-16% no TPS têm implicações significativas em custos operacionais e capacidade de servir múltiplos agentes simultaneamente em ambientes de produção.

artigos.titulo: Contravariance Theory: Strong Alignment for Minimal Solutions to Hard Tasks
artigos.resumo: A series of results from the NeuroAI over the past fifteen years have raised core questions both about how to compare Deep Neural Network (DNN) models to the brain, and about how much convergent evolution to expect between artificial networks and real brain networks. Here, we show that for any two minimal DNN solutions to a sufficiently hard task: (i) "weak" alignment of network representations based on affine mappings guarantees "strong" alignment of privileged axes, and (ii) alignment "zippers" up the network hierarchy, causing the emergence of privileged axes from end-to-end task optimization. These results formalize the notion of contravariance from Cao and Yamins [2024], and illustrate important consequences for the theory of NeuroAI: with sufficiently strong tasks, choice of metric for inter-network comparison is not all that sensitive, and that convergent evolution is probably inevitable.
artigos.titulo_en: Contravariance Theory: Strong Alignment for Minimal Solutions to Hard Tasks
artigos.titulo_pt: Teoria da Contravariância: Alinhamento Forte para Soluções Mínimas em Tarefas Difíceis
artigos.resumo_executivo_en: The work formalizes contravariance theory for deep neural networks, demonstrating that minimal solutions to sufficiently hard tasks exhibit strong alignment of privileged axes when weak alignment based on affine mappings is present. Results indicate that the choice of metric for inter-network comparison is relatively insensitive and that convergent evolution between artificial and biological networks is likely inevitable for complex tasks.
artigos.resumo_executivo_pt: O trabalho formaliza a teoria da contravariância para redes neurais profundas, demonstrando que soluções mínimas para tarefas suficientemente difíceis apresentam alinhamento forte de eixos privilegiados quando há alinhamento fraco baseado em mapeamentos afins. Os resultados indicam que a escolha de métrica para comparação entre redes é pouco sensível e que a evolução convergente entre redes artificiais e biológicas é provavelmente inevitável em tarefas complexas.
artigos.justificativa: A compreensão teórica de como redes neurais convergem para soluções similares em tarefas complexas tem implicações para o design de arquiteturas de IA mais eficientes e para a interpretabilidade de modelos, com potencial aplicação em otimização de sistemas de aprendizado profundo em produção.

artigos.titulo: CAAD: Causality-Aware Multivariate Time Series Anomaly Detection via Multi-Scale Alignment and Structural Causal Consistency
artigos.resumo: The operational integrity of complex industrial systems relies on precise anomaly detection and diagnosis. The vast majority of existing methods narrowly focus on capturing temporal similarities of representations, often overlooking the disruption of internal causal relationships, which characterizes system failures and latent anomalies. In this paper, we propose a novel framework (CAAD) that reframes anomaly detection as the continuous verification of Granger causality consistency through exogenous variables.   Specifically, the CAAD framework models exogenous time-series variables as residuals, identifying anomalies as significant deviations caused by external interventions. The proposed framework leverages multi-scale alignment to internalize system dynamics and utilizes a gradient-based matrix to monitor internal causal relationship breakdowns. By quantifying causal deviations of both dynamic evolution and relational topology, the CAAD is able to capture subtle causal shifts to achieve precise anomaly detection. Extensive experiments on real-world industrial datasets demonstrate that the CAAD achieves high-precision anomaly detection, outperforming most state-of-the-art baselines.
artigos.titulo_en: CAAD: Causality-Aware Multivariate Time Series Anomaly Detection via Multi-Scale Alignment and Structural Causal Consistency
artigos.titulo_pt: CAAD: Detecção de Anomalias em Séries Temporais Multivariadas com Consciência Causal via Alinhamento Multi-Escala e Consistência Causal Estrutural
artigos.resumo_executivo_en: The CAAD framework proposes an innovative approach to anomaly detection in complex industrial systems by reframing the problem as continuous verification of Granger causality consistency through exogenous variables. The method leverages multi-scale alignment and gradient-based matrices to monitor breakdowns in internal causal relationships, capturing subtle causal deviations in both dynamic evolution and relational topology. Experiments on real-world industrial datasets demonstrate that CAAD achieves high-precision anomaly detection, outperforming most state-of-the-art baselines.
artigos.resumo_executivo_pt: O framework CAAD propõe uma abordagem inovadora para detecção de anomalias em sistemas industriais complexos, reinterpretando o problema como verificação contínua de consistência causal de Granger através de variáveis exógenas. O método utiliza alinhamento multi-escala e matrizes baseadas em gradientes para monitorar rupturas nas relações causais internas, capturando desvios causais sutis em dinâmica e topologia relacional. Experimentos em datasets industriais reais demonstram que o CAAD alcança detecção de anomalias de alta precisão, superando a maioria dos baselines estado-da-arte.
artigos.justificativa: A detecção precisa de anomalias em sistemas industriais complexos é crítica para manutenção preditiva e prevenção de falhas operacionais, reduzindo custos de downtime e melhorando a confiabilidade de infraestruturas críticas. O framework CAAD oferece uma solução avançada que supera métodos existentes ao incorporar análise causal, com aplicação direta em operações industriais.

artigos.titulo: CommuniWave:A Machine Learning Model for Quantifying the Degree of Temporary Informal Behavior in Urban Communities
artigos.resumo: For urban managers and designers, improving the functional attributes of urban communities to enhance territorial resilience in the face of complexity and uncertainty is crucial. Currently, community planning often follows a top-down approach and lacks effective metrics to quantify informal behaviors of residents, leading to frequent conflicts with original plans. This study introduces CommuniWave, a machine learning model designed to efficiently detect and quantify the Degree of Informal Behavior (DIB) in urban communities. The model integrates a Behavior Capture Net (BCN) based on mmaction2, a self-developed YOLOv10 model (YLX), and a Behavior Eval Model (BEM) using random forest. Ultimately, by generating DIB fluctuation charts from street videos, the model facilitates dynamic monitoring, supporting urban managers in making refined decisions to enhance the overall resilience of communities.
artigos.titulo_en: CommuniWave: A Machine Learning Model for Quantifying the Degree of Temporary Informal Behavior in Urban Communities
artigos.titulo_pt: CommuniWave: Um Modelo de Aprendizado de Máquina para Quantificar o Grau de Comportamento Informal Temporário em Comunidades Urbanas
artigos.resumo_executivo_en: CommuniWave is a machine learning model that detects and quantifies the degree of informal behavior in urban communities through street video analysis. The system integrates behavior capture networks, YOLOv10 object detection, and random forest evaluation to generate behavior fluctuation charts. The solution supports urban managers in making refined decisions to enhance community resilience.
artigos.resumo_executivo_pt: CommuniWave é um modelo de aprendizado de máquina que detecta e quantifica o grau de comportamento informal em comunidades urbanas através de análise de vídeos de rua. O sistema integra redes de captura de comportamento, detecção de objetos YOLOv10 e avaliação por floresta aleatória para gerar gráficos de flutuação de comportamento. A solução apoia gestores urbanos na tomada de decisões refinadas para aumentar a resiliência comunitária.
artigos.justificativa: O modelo oferece uma solução prática para planejamento urbano baseado em dados, permitindo que gestores urbanos monitorem dinamicamente comportamentos informais e tomem decisões mais informadas para melhorar a resiliência comunitária, reduzindo conflitos entre planos e realidade.

artigos.titulo: Structural Bottlenecks on Frequency Representation in End-to-End Audio Models
artigos.resumo: End-to-end neural audio models achieve high-fidelity compression and generation. We might read that performance as evidence they directly represent interpretable features such as pitch and timbre, but a model can produce plausible outputs without doing so. A model may encode these features in any reachable basis, but regardless of which, the features are well described as compositions of time-frequency-localized primitives. Whether state-of-the-art encoders preserve access to these primitives, and thus to compositions of them, remains unclear. Through theoretical analysis and controlled experiments, we show that several state-of-the-art strided convolutional encoders impose two structural bottlenecks, both predictable from architecture and signal structure, on access to these primitives: (1) they collapse primitives into alias equivalence classes, establishing a bound on representational capacity, and (2) they limit the frequency resolution available to learned filters, restricting separability. For well structured data, we find collapse rates of 31-35% and filter bandwidths 10-35x above the theoretical resolution bound, confirming that both bottlenecks arise under realistic signal conditions. We then introduce Gabor Latent Refactorization (GLRF), a lightweight post-hoc intervention that re-expresses encoder latents in a frequency-localized basis, reducing filter bandwidths from 10-35x to 1.5-3x of the theoretical resolution bound while preserving reconstruction fidelity and improving control over attributes like pitch. These results show that the encoders in question predictably degrade access to frequency-localized primitives, entangling the features that depend on them, and that a lightweight, retraining-free intervention can recover much of that access, improving steerability and interpretability.
artigos.titulo_en: Structural Bottlenecks on Frequency Representation in End-to-End Audio Models
artigos.titulo_pt: Gargalos Estruturais na Representação de Frequência em Modelos de Áudio End-to-End
artigos.resumo_executivo_en: The paper identifies structural bottlenecks in convolutional audio encoders that limit access to frequency-localized primitives, reducing interpretability and control over attributes like pitch. Through theoretical analysis and controlled experiments, it demonstrates that these bottlenecks cause primitive collapse (31-35%) and filter bandwidths 10-35x above the theoretical limit. It proposes Gabor Latent Refactorization (GLRF), a lightweight post-hoc intervention that recovers access to frequency-localized primitives, improving interpretability and controllability without retraining.
artigos.resumo_executivo_pt: O artigo identifica gargalos estruturais em codificadores convolucionais de áudio que limitam o acesso a primitivos localizados em frequência, reduzindo a interpretabilidade e controle sobre atributos como pitch. Através de análise teórica e experimentos controlados, demonstra que esses gargalos causam colapso de primitivos (31-35%) e largura de banda de filtros 10-35x acima do limite teórico. Propõe Gabor Latent Refactorization (GLRF), uma intervenção leve pós-hoc que recupera o acesso a primitivos localizados em frequência, melhorando a interpretabilidade e controlabilidade sem retreinamento.
artigos.justificativa: A melhoria na interpretabilidade e controlabilidade de modelos de áudio tem aplicações diretas em síntese de música, processamento de áudio profissional e sistemas de geração de conteúdo, onde o controle fino sobre atributos acústicos é crítico para qualidade comercial.

artigos.titulo: VocaDet: Sample-Driven Open-Vocabulary Object Detection and Segmentation via Visual Tokenization and Vector Database Retrieval
artigos.resumo: Open-vocabulary object detection and segmentation aim to recognize arbitrary objects beyond predefined categories. Although recent vision-language and reference-based approaches have significantly advanced this field, they often rely on text prompts, limited visual examples, or expensive feature matching procedures, making them difficult to scale to large and continuously expanding object repositories. In this work, we propose VocaDet, a sample-driven open-vocabulary object detection and segmentation framework that learns object concepts directly from user-provided positive and negative sample collections without model retraining. The key idea is to transform continuous visual representations into discrete visual vocabularies and perform efficient retrieval-based recognition through a scalable vector database. Specifically, we employ DINOv3 as the visual feature extractor and apply agglomerative clustering with adaptive clustering sensitivity to generate multi-granularity visual tokens. These visual tokens, together with position-debiased representations and spatial topology information, are stored as expandable object memories in a vector database. During inference, query images are converted into visual tokens and efficiently matched against the stored object memories for object localization and segmentation. Furthermore, a background filtering mechanism is introduced to remove frequently occurring background patterns and reduce redundant retrieval operations in practical fixed-camera scenarios. Experiments on the UA-DETRAC dataset demonstrate that VocaDet achieves effective open-vocabulary detection performance without conventional detector training, while supporting continuously expandable recognition capability as additional positive and negative samples are accumulated.
artigos.titulo_en: VocaDet: Sample-Driven Open-Vocabulary Object Detection and Segmentation via Visual Tokenization and Vector Database Retrieval
artigos.titulo_pt: VocaDet: Detecção e Segmentação de Objetos em Vocabulário Aberto Orientadas por Amostras via Tokenização Visual e Recuperação em Banco de Dados Vetorial
artigos.resumo_executivo_en: VocaDet is an open-vocabulary object detection and segmentation framework that learns object concepts directly from user-provided visual samples without model retraining. The system transforms continuous visual representations into discrete visual vocabularies and performs efficient retrieval through a scalable vector database, utilizing visual tokenization with adaptive clustering and background filtering. It demonstrates continuously expandable recognition capability on the UA-DETRAC dataset, offering a practical solution for detecting arbitrary objects in fixed-camera scenarios.
artigos.resumo_executivo_pt: VocaDet é um framework de detecção e segmentação de objetos em vocabulário aberto que aprende conceitos de objetos diretamente de amostras visuais fornecidas pelo usuário, sem necessidade de retreinamento do modelo. O sistema transforma representações visuais contínuas em vocabulários visuais discretos e realiza recuperação eficiente através de um banco de dados vetorial escalável, utilizando tokenização visual com clustering adaptativo e filtragem de fundo. Demonstra capacidade de reconhecimento continuamente expansível no dataset UA-DETRAC, oferecendo uma solução prática para detecção de objetos arbitrários em cenários com câmeras fixas.
artigos.justificativa: O framework oferece uma solução prática e escalável para detecção de objetos sem retreinamento, eliminando custos de anotação e permitindo expansão contínua do repositório de objetos, o que é altamente relevante para aplicações comerciais em vigilância, varejo e automação industrial que precisam adaptar-se a novos produtos ou categorias sem intervenção técnica complexa.

artigos.titulo: Improving Ad-hoc Search Effectiveness for Conversational Information Retrieval via Model Merging
artigos.resumo: Conversational information retrieval is challenging since it requires the consideration of the conversation history which potentially gives rise to topic shifts and coreference resolution across previous turns. To address these challenges, previous work mainly rely on traditional fine-tuning of ad-hoc retrievers on conversational datasets or extrapolates their generalizability through multi-tasking. However, this mainstream approach is costly - since it requires model re-training - and exhibits catastrophic forgetting, where the model loses its foundational ad-hoc retrieval performance. In this paper, we fill this gap by introducing model merging as a training-free strategy enabling the design of a single retrieval model that operates across both ad-hoc and conversational settings with no additional fine-tuning. We conduct experiments using linear and non-linear parameter-wise merging strategies - namely Model Soup and Slerp - on standard ad-hoc search and conversational retrieval datasets. Our results demonstrate that model merging significantly enhances the ad-hoc search capabilities of conversational retrievers while improving generalizability across task-specific datasets, achieving up to 15% higher NDCG@3 under zero-shot conditions.
artigos.titulo_en: Improving Ad-hoc Search Effectiveness for Conversational Information Retrieval via Model Merging
artigos.titulo_pt: Melhorando a Efetividade da Busca Ad-hoc para Recuperação de Informação Conversacional via Fusão de Modelos
artigos.resumo_executivo_en: The paper proposes model merging as a training-free strategy to create a single retrieval model that operates effectively in both ad-hoc and conversational search settings. Using linear and non-linear parameter-wise merging strategies (Model Soup and Slerp), the work demonstrates improvements up to 15% in NDCG@3 under zero-shot conditions. The approach avoids catastrophic forgetting and eliminates costly model retraining requirements.
artigos.resumo_executivo_pt: O artigo propõe model merging como estratégia sem treinamento adicional para criar um único modelo de recuperação que funciona tanto em buscas ad-hoc quanto em cenários conversacionais. Utilizando estratégias de fusão de parâmetros lineares e não-lineares (Model Soup e Slerp), o trabalho demonstra melhorias de até 15% em NDCG@3 em condições zero-shot. A abordagem evita o problema de catastrophic forgetting e elimina a necessidade de retreinamento custoso.
artigos.justificativa: A técnica de model merging oferece uma solução prática e eficiente para sistemas de recuperação de informação conversacional, reduzindo custos operacionais ao eliminar retreinamento e melhorando significativamente a performance em buscas ad-hoc, com aplicações diretas em assistentes virtuais e plataformas de busca conversacional.

artigos.titulo: DocMaster: A Hierarchical Structure-Aware System for Document Analysis
artigos.resumo: Leveraging large language models (LLMs) to analyze complex documents -- such as academic papers, technical manuals, and financial reports -- has emerged as a mainstream and critical task in both research and industry. In practice, users must first filter relevant documents from large collections and then conduct in-depth analysis (e.g. question answering) over the selected subset, yet existing systems flatten documents into plain-text chunks, discarding the rich hierarchical structures (sections, tables, figures, equations) and degrading downstream performance. We present DocMaster, a hierarchical structure-aware document analysis system. DocMaster parses documents into hierarchical document trees preserving original layouts and constructs a structure-aware semantic index that enables accurate document filtering and in-depth analysis. We demonstrate DocMaster through an interactive web interface that enables users to upload document collections, construct tree-based and multi-view semantic indices, filter relevant documents via natural-language conditions, and perform follow-up question answering over the filtered results. The source code, data, and demo are available at https://doc-master.github.io/.
artigos.titulo_en: DocMaster: A Hierarchical Structure-Aware System for Document Analysis
artigos.titulo_pt: DocMaster: Um Sistema Consciente de Estrutura Hierárquica para Análise de Documentos
artigos.resumo_executivo_en: DocMaster is a document analysis system that preserves hierarchical structures (sections, tables, figures, equations) when processing complex documents with LLMs, overcoming limitations of systems that flatten documents into plain text. The system constructs structure-aware semantic indices for accurate document filtering and in-depth analysis, including question answering capabilities. The solution is demonstrated through an interactive web interface with source code, data, and demo publicly available.
artigos.resumo_executivo_pt: DocMaster é um sistema de análise de documentos que preserva estruturas hierárquicas (seções, tabelas, figuras, equações) ao processar documentos complexos com LLMs, superando limitações de sistemas que convertem documentos em texto plano. O sistema constrói índices semânticos conscientes de estrutura para filtragem precisa de documentos e análise aprofundada, incluindo resposta a perguntas. A solução é demonstrada através de uma interface web interativa com código-fonte, dados e demonstração disponíveis publicamente.
artigos.justificativa: DocMaster endereça um problema crítico em aplicações empresariais de análise de documentos (papers acadêmicos, manuais técnicos, relatórios financeiros), melhorando significativamente a precisão de filtragem e análise ao preservar estruturas hierárquicas, com potencial de implementação imediata em fluxos de trabalho corporativos.

artigos.titulo: High-Dimensional Procrustes Matching via Tree Counts
artigos.resumo: Suppose we observe two sets of $n$ Gaussian vectors in $\mathbb{R}^d$, with the promise that, after applying a permutation of $[n]$ and a rotation of $\mathbb{R}^d$, the two sets are $ρ$-correlated. The Procrustes matching problem asks us to recover the unknown permutation of $[n]$ that aligns the two sets. The problem is well-studied in the low-dimensional regime $d=O(\log n)$, but the high-dimensional regime $d\gg \log n$ has remained largely uncharted: prior matching guarantees require nearly perfect correlation $ρ=1-o(1)$, even for information-theoretic recovery.   Our main result is a polynomial-time algorithm for exact recovery at constant correlation. The algorithm works by computing and comparing weighted counts of a specially chosen family of ``wide'' trees. So long as $d\ge \mathrm{polylog}(n)$, the algorithm succeeds with high probability for any $ρ^2>\sqrtα$, where $α\approx 0.338$ is Otter's tree-counting constant.   We complement this algorithmic result with an improved information-theoretic guarantee, showing that exact recovery is possible when $ρ^2 \gtrsim \max\{\log n/d,\sqrt{\log n/n}\}$. We also carry out a low-degree advantage calculation, which suggests that the condition $ρ^2 > \sqrtα$ is necessary for any tree-counting algorithm.
artigos.titulo_en: High-Dimensional Procrustes Matching via Tree Counts
artigos.titulo_pt: Correspondência de Procrustes em Alta Dimensão via Contagem de Árvores
artigos.resumo_executivo_en: The paper presents a polynomial-time algorithm for solving the high-dimensional Procrustes matching problem, recovering permutations that align two sets of correlated Gaussian vectors. The method uses weighted counting of specially chosen trees and guarantees exact recovery at constant correlation when d ≥ polylog(n). Results include information-theoretic guarantees and low-degree advantage analysis, suggesting that the condition ρ² > √α is necessary for tree-counting algorithms.
artigos.resumo_executivo_pt: O artigo apresenta um algoritmo polinomial para resolver o problema de correspondência de Procrustes em alta dimensão, recuperando permutações que alinham dois conjuntos de vetores gaussianos correlacionados. O método utiliza contagem ponderada de árvores especialmente escolhidas e garante recuperação exata com correlação constante quando d ≥ polylog(n). Os resultados incluem garantias teóricas de informação e análise de vantagem de baixo grau, sugerindo que a condição ρ² > √α é necessária para algoritmos baseados em contagem de árvores.
artigos.justificativa: O trabalho aborda um problema fundamental de alinhamento de dados em alta dimensão com aplicações potenciais em análise de dados, reconhecimento de padrões e problemas de correspondência em visão computacional e aprendizado de máquina.

artigos.titulo: Whareformer: Learning to Track What is Where in Long Egocentric Videos
artigos.resumo: The recently established 'Out of Sight, Not out of Mind' (OSNOM) task for egocentric videos focuses on tracking objects that are moved by the camera wearer, online, maintaining knowledge of instance locations throughout the video even when they leave the field of view or become heavily occluded. In this paper, we propose the first learning-based solution to the OSNOM task: Whareformer, a transformer-based model with two components: an updatable memory of established tracks and a track assignment module that associates observations with existing tracks in a feed-forward manner. Whareformer jointly reasons over evolving object appearance (what) and updated 3D location (where), and employs a dedicated New Track token to reason about novel objects.   Thanks to its design choices of using relative distances and evolving track representations, Whareformer is trained on a small set of 56 videos but achieves SOTA performance on 260 long test videos from three datasets: EPIC-KITCHENS-100 (unseen videos), IT3DEgo, and HD-EPIC, with significant absolute improvements over prior work.
artigos.titulo_en: Whareformer: Learning to Track What is Where in Long Egocentric Videos
artigos.titulo_pt: Whareformer: Aprendendo a Rastrear O Que Está Onde em Vídeos Egocêntricos Longos
artigos.resumo_executivo_en: Whareformer is a transformer-based model that solves the OSNOM (Out of Sight, Not out of Mind) task for egocentric videos, tracking objects moved by the camera wearer even when they leave the field of view or become occluded. The model combines updatable memory of established tracks with an assignment module that reasons over object appearance and updated 3D location. Trained on only 56 videos, it achieves state-of-the-art performance on 260 long test videos from three datasets with significant improvements over prior work.
artigos.resumo_executivo_pt: O Whareformer é um modelo baseado em transformers que resolve a tarefa OSNOM (Out of Sight, Not out of Mind) para vídeos egocêntricos, rastreando objetos movidos pelo usuário mesmo quando saem do campo de visão ou ficam ocluídos. O modelo combina memória atualizável de rastreamentos com um módulo de atribuição que raciocina sobre aparência de objetos e localização 3D atualizada. Treinado em apenas 56 vídeos, alcança desempenho de ponta em 260 vídeos longos de três datasets diferentes com melhorias significativas sobre trabalhos anteriores.
artigos.justificativa: O rastreamento de objetos em vídeos egocêntricos tem aplicações diretas em realidade aumentada, assistentes visuais inteligentes, robótica e sistemas de análise de atividades humanas, com potencial comercial significativo em dispositivos wearable e plataformas de visão computacional.

artigos.titulo: When the Judge Changes, So Does the Measurement: Auditing LLM-as-Judge Reliability
artigos.resumo: An LLM-as-judge score can move even when the candidate responses stay fixed, simply because the evaluator has changed. We treat this evaluator-replacement ambiguity as a measurement-validity problem. Across four judgment datasets, we compare two upgrade paths available in practice: scaling Qwen3 dense judges from 1.7B to 32B parameters and moving across MiniMax M2-M2.7 released APIs. The main pattern is that judge upgrades are not interchangeable: only Qwen3 1.7B to 4B gives a robust adjacent gain, while MiniMax adjacent releases do not. Stronger judges reduce but do not remove position and verbosity bias. Repeated-sample juries add little when errors are correlated. Structured debate can move decisions substantially, but without parser and fallback logs those shifts cannot be attributed to deliberation. We argue that LLM-as-judge reports should include dataset slices, bias probes, error-dependence estimates, and protocol audit trails.
artigos.titulo_en: When the Judge Changes, So Does the Measurement: Auditing LLM-as-Judge Reliability
artigos.titulo_pt: Quando o Juiz Muda, a Medição Também Muda: Auditoria da Confiabilidade de LLM-como-Juiz
artigos.resumo_executivo_en: This work identifies a critical measurement-validity problem in LLM-based evaluation systems: scores can shift significantly due to evaluator model changes alone, even when candidate responses remain identical. Through comparative analysis of different upgrade paths (Qwen3 1.7B-32B and MiniMax M2-M2.7), it demonstrates that judge upgrades are not interchangeable and position/verbosity biases persist. The study recommends that LLM-as-judge reports include dataset slices, bias probes, error-dependence estimates, and protocol audit trails.
artigos.resumo_executivo_pt: O trabalho identifica um problema crítico de validade de medição em sistemas de avaliação baseados em LLM: scores podem variar significativamente apenas pela mudança do modelo avaliador, mesmo quando as respostas candidatas permanecem idênticas. Através de análise comparativa de diferentes caminhos de upgrade (Qwen3 1.7B-32B e MiniMax M2-M2.7), demonstra que upgrades de juízes não são intercambiáveis e que vieses de posição e verbosidade persistem. O estudo recomenda que relatórios de LLM-as-judge incluam slices de dados, probes de viés, estimativas de dependência de erros e trilhas de auditoria de protocolo.
artigos.justificativa: A confiabilidade de sistemas de avaliação automática baseados em LLM é crítica para empresas que dependem dessas soluções em produção. O trabalho expõe vulnerabilidades fundamentais na reprodutibilidade e validade de medições, impactando diretamente a qualidade de decisões automatizadas e a confiança em pipelines de avaliação de modelos.

artigos.titulo: AI-guided stimuli discovery and generation to optimize facial emotion perception studies in autism
artigos.resumo: Understanding perceptual differences between autistic and neurotypical adults requires behavioral assays that are sensitive, reliable, and mechanistically informative. Facial emotion perception is a useful test case because group differences have been reported, but findings vary across studies. Here we show that this variability may reflect image-level sparsity: autistic-neurotypical differences in emotion judgments were concentrated in a small subset of diagnostic facial expressions rather than spread uniformly across stimuli. We trained population-specific artificial neural network models to predict image-level judgments for autistic and neurotypical participants, then used these models to select novel faces predicted to maximize group separation. In an independent cohort, model-selected images produced larger behavioral differences than matched random images. We then used the same models with a generative adversarial network to transform diagnostic images toward greater predicted group agreement. In phenotype-matched validation, synthesized images reduced behavioral separation relative to their matched originals. These results establish a model-guided framework for discovering and transforming stimuli that reveal population-specific perceptual differences. More broadly, they show how behavioral phenotyping can move beyond averaging across fixed stimulus sets toward optimized assays that identify the conditions under which neurodivergent perception diverges or converges.
artigos.titulo_en: AI-guided stimuli discovery and generation to optimize facial emotion perception studies in autism
artigos.titulo_pt: Descoberta e geração de estímulos guiada por IA para otimizar estudos de percepção de emoções faciais no autismo
artigos.resumo_executivo_en: The work presents an AI-guided framework for discovering and transforming visual stimuli that reveal population-specific perceptual differences between autistic and neurotypical individuals. Using artificial neural networks and generative adversarial networks, researchers identified subsets of facial expressions that maximize behavioral differences between groups and synthesized images that reduce these separations. This approach optimizes behavioral assays to identify conditions under which neurodivergent perception diverges or converges.
artigos.resumo_executivo_pt: O trabalho apresenta um framework guiado por modelos de IA para descobrir e transformar estímulos visuais que revelam diferenças perceptuais específicas entre indivíduos autistas e neurotípicos. Utilizando redes neurais artificiais e redes adversariais generativas, os pesquisadores identificaram subconjuntos de expressões faciais que maximizam diferenças comportamentais entre grupos e sintetizaram imagens que reduzem essas separações. Esta abordagem otimiza ensaios comportamentais para identificar as condições sob as quais a percepção neurodivergente diverge ou converge.
artigos.justificativa: O trabalho demonstra aplicação prática de IA generativa e aprendizado de máquina para otimizar pesquisa comportamental em neurodiversidade, com potencial impacto em diagnóstico, desenvolvimento de terapias e compreensão de diferenças perceptuais em populações neurodivergentes.

artigos.titulo: Stop Guessing When to Stop Testing: Efficient Model Evaluation with Just Enough Data
artigos.resumo: The inherent rigidity of fixed-size benchmarks makes them an inefficient tool for model evaluation. Diverse evaluation objectives, including model ranking, model selection and testing throughout development, demand varying levels of statistical power. The mismatch between fixed sample sizes and these diverse needs results in either excessive computational cost or compromised reliability - a critical concern for model evaluation. To overcome these limitations, we call for adoption of sequential testing in our field. We provide an adaptive evaluation framework, that provides a principled way to navigate the trade-off between efficiency and reliability in model evaluation. Our framework combines the established statistical paradigm of sequential testing with stopping criteria tailored to common evaluation needs such as diminishing returns detection, and minimum detectable effect size. We demonstrate its ability to adaptively manage the efficiency-reliability trade-off on the Open VLM Leaderboard, including, for example, a 80% reduction in computational cost compared to fixed-size evaluation (with a 2.5-point CI width allowance) while maintaining statistical significance.
artigos.titulo_en: Stop Guessing When to Stop Testing: Efficient Model Evaluation with Just Enough Data
artigos.titulo_pt: Pare de Adivinhar Quando Parar de Testar: Avaliação Eficiente de Modelos com Dados Suficientes
artigos.resumo_executivo_en: The paper proposes an adaptive sequential testing framework for model evaluation that optimizes the trade-off between computational efficiency and statistical reliability. The method combines sequential testing with stopping criteria tailored to common evaluation needs such as diminishing returns detection and minimum detectable effect size. It demonstrates up to 80% reduction in computational cost compared to fixed-size evaluation while maintaining statistical significance.
artigos.resumo_executivo_pt: O artigo propõe um framework adaptativo de avaliação sequencial para modelos de IA que otimiza o equilíbrio entre eficiência computacional e confiabilidade estatística. O método combina testes sequenciais com critérios de parada personalizados para necessidades comuns de avaliação, como detecção de retornos diminutos e tamanho mínimo de efeito detectável. Demonstra redução de até 80% no custo computacional em comparação com avaliação de tamanho fixo, mantendo significância estatística.
artigos.justificativa: A redução de até 80% no custo computacional de avaliação de modelos tem impacto direto na redução de despesas operacionais e aceleração do ciclo de desenvolvimento de IA. O framework é aplicável a cenários práticos de ranking e seleção de modelos, tornando-o altamente relevante para organizações que desenvolvem e avaliam múltiplos modelos em produção.

artigos.titulo: Beyond wheelchairs and blindfolds: Investigating disability stereotypes in T2I models with INCLUDE-BENCH
artigos.resumo: Text-to-image (T2I) models have been shown to exhibit social biases. Prior work has mainly focused on gender, skin tone, and cultural representation within restricted occupational associations, and emerging benchmarks increasingly incorporate these dimensions. However, disability remains systematically underexplored. Current evaluation practices often fail to align with sociologically grounded definitions of stereotyping, limiting principled assessment of representational harms toward people with disabilities (PWD). To address this, we introduce INCLUDE-BENCH, the first large-scale benchmark for evaluating disability-related bias in T2I models. INCLUDE-BENCH comprises 119K generated images based on prompt design across multiple bias dimensions and both static and dynamic contexts. We evaluate 15 open-source and 2 closed-source models. Our key findings reveal that: (1) mobility-impaired and default disability prompts predominantly yield wheelchair depictions across all models; (2) disability-conditioned generations consistently exhibit less diversity; (3) stereotypical portrayals demonstrate stronger disability-text alignment; and (4) we introduce the Stereotype Content Model (SCM) Score, demonstrating that T2I models reflect real-world stereotypical associations.
artigos.titulo_en: Beyond wheelchairs and blindfolds: Investigating disability stereotypes in T2I models with INCLUDE-BENCH
artigos.titulo_pt: Além de cadeiras de rodas e vendas: Investigando estereótipos de deficiência em modelos T2I com INCLUDE-BENCH
artigos.resumo_executivo_en: This work introduces INCLUDE-BENCH, the first large-scale benchmark for evaluating disability-related bias in text-to-image (T2I) generation models. Analysis of 119K generated images reveals that T2I models systematically perpetuate stereotypes about people with disabilities, with predominantly limited representations to wheelchairs and reduced diversity in disability-conditioned generations. The study introduces the Stereotype Content Model (SCM) Score, demonstrating that these models reflect real-world stereotypical associations.
artigos.resumo_executivo_pt: O trabalho apresenta INCLUDE-BENCH, o primeiro benchmark em larga escala para avaliar vieses relacionados a deficiência em modelos de geração de imagem a partir de texto (T2I). A análise de 119K imagens geradas revela que modelos T2I perpetuam estereótipos sistemáticos sobre pessoas com deficiência, com representações predominantemente limitadas a cadeiras de rodas e menor diversidade em gerações condicionadas por deficiência. O estudo introduz o Stereotype Content Model (SCM) Score, demonstrando que esses modelos refletem associações estereotipadas do mundo real.
artigos.justificativa: A avaliação sistemática de vieses em modelos T2I é crítica para empresas que desenvolvem ou utilizam esses sistemas, pois impacta diretamente na responsabilidade social, conformidade regulatória e reputação de marca. O benchmark fornece metodologia rigorosa para identificar e mitigar representações estereotipadas de grupos minoritários.

artigos.titulo: Do Egocentric Video-Language Models Capture Both Hand- and Object-Centric Cues?
artigos.resumo: Hand-object interaction (HOI) recognition requires capturing both hand manipulations and object transformations. However, existing video-language models often fall into shortcuts by relying on spurious correlations among hands, objects, or environmental context, rather than reasoning from the appearance and dynamics of hands and objects themselves. To address this limitation, we propose a new learning paradigm that combines (i) hand-object masked training, which enables robust reasoning from partial hand or object observations, and (ii) an HOI-dynamics-aware decoder that explicitly learns hand- and object-centric embeddings through auxiliary predictions of their locations and semantics, enhancing sensitivity to both cues. To systematically evaluate such cue-specific reasoning, we introduce Cue-Isolated HOI (CI-HOI), a new evaluation that assesses models' ability to predict actions from hand- and object-related cues independently. To enable CI-HOI, we curate the DEHOI testbed, which separates hand- and object-related observations for disentangled HOI evaluation through inpainting. Using DEHOI, we demonstrate both quantitatively and qualitatively that our training strategy exploits hand- and object-centric information more effectively than existing models. Our approach improves over existing models on DEHOI, standard action recognition, object state recognition, and even robot manipulation action recognition, leading to more robust HOI understanding.
artigos.titulo_en: Do Egocentric Video-Language Models Capture Both Hand- and Object-Centric Cues?
artigos.titulo_pt: Modelos de Vídeo-Linguagem Egocêntricos Capturam Tanto Pistas Centradas em Mãos quanto em Objetos?
artigos.resumo_executivo_en: The paper proposes a novel learning paradigm for hand-object interaction recognition in egocentric videos, combining hand-object masked training and an HOI-dynamics-aware decoder. It introduces Cue-Isolated HOI (CI-HOI) evaluation and the DEHOI testbed to systematically assess models' ability to reason from hand- and object-specific cues. The approach demonstrates significant improvements in action recognition, object state recognition, and robot manipulation action recognition.
artigos.resumo_executivo_pt: O artigo propõe um novo paradigma de aprendizado para reconhecimento de interação mão-objeto em vídeos egocêntricos, combinando treinamento com mascaramento de mão-objeto e um decodificador sensível à dinâmica de HOI. Introduz a avaliação Cue-Isolated HOI (CI-HOI) e o testbed DEHOI para avaliar sistematicamente a capacidade dos modelos de raciocinar a partir de pistas específicas de mãos e objetos. A abordagem demonstra melhorias significativas em reconhecimento de ações, estados de objetos e até em reconhecimento de ações de manipulação robótica.
artigos.justificativa: O trabalho aborda reconhecimento robusto de interações mão-objeto em vídeos egocêntricos, com aplicações diretas em robótica de manipulação, sistemas de compreensão de ações e análise de vídeos de primeira pessoa, tecnologias críticas para automação industrial e assistentes robóticos.

artigos.titulo: Systematic Evaluation of Learning Rate Scheduling Strategies Across Heterogeneous Architectures
artigos.resumo: Choosing a learning rate scheduling strategy is critical to neural network training, but manual selection is costly and rarely exhaustive. While classical AutoML approaches often treat the scheduler as a secondary hyperparameter, we systematically investigate its impact on classification accuracy across a diverse pool of architectures. We evaluated 30 representative architectures from convolutional and transformer families within the LEMUR neural network dataset. Through automated source-code injection, we applied 25 scheduler configurations across nine PyTorch families, evaluating a total of 3,938 model variants on CIFAR-10. Our best configuration achieved a top-1 accuracy of 86.45%, with 237 variants exceeding 80%. The results show that the choice of scheduler depends heavily on the architecture: CosineAnnealingWarmRestarts and CyclicLR consistently outperform basic decay strategies. The resulting accuracy landscape, contributed to the LEMUR nn-dataset, provides a practical reference for principled scheduler selection.
artigos.titulo_en: Systematic Evaluation of Learning Rate Scheduling Strategies Across Heterogeneous Architectures
artigos.titulo_pt: Avaliação Sistemática de Estratégias de Agendamento de Taxa de Aprendizado em Arquiteturas Heterogêneas
artigos.resumo_executivo_en: This work systematically evaluates 25 learning rate scheduling strategies across 30 distinct neural architectures, testing 3,938 model variants on CIFAR-10. Results demonstrate that CosineAnnealingWarmRestarts and CyclicLR consistently outperform basic decay strategies, with the best configuration achieving 86.45% accuracy. The study contributes a practical accuracy landscape for principled scheduler selection, made available in the LEMUR dataset.
artigos.resumo_executivo_pt: Este trabalho realiza uma avaliação sistemática de 25 estratégias de agendamento de taxa de aprendizado em 30 arquiteturas neurais distintas, testando 3.938 variantes de modelos no CIFAR-10. Os resultados demonstram que CosineAnnealingWarmRestarts e CyclicLR superam consistentemente estratégias de decaimento básico, com a melhor configuração alcançando 86,45% de acurácia. O estudo contribui com um mapa de acurácia prático para seleção fundamentada de agendadores, disponibilizado no dataset LEMUR.
artigos.justificativa: A seleção automática e fundamentada de estratégias de agendamento de taxa de aprendizado reduz significativamente o tempo de tuning de hiperparâmetros em projetos de ML, impactando diretamente na eficiência operacional e custo computacional de treinamento de modelos em produção.

artigos.titulo: CT-CLIP Representations for Multimodal Lung Cancer Survival Prediction
artigos.resumo: Accurate prognosis prediction is important for treatment planning in lung cancer, but deep learning-driven survival modelling is often limited by the scarcity of curated imaging cohorts with reliable outcome data. This study evaluates whether representations from a domain-specific foundation model can be used for multimodal survival prediction in data-constrained clinical settings. We assess the foundation model CT-CLIP as a feature extractor for pretreatment computed tomography images and clinical variables from 242 diagnosed lung cancer patients. The evaluation includes adaptation strategies based on frozen encoders, full fine-tuning, and low-rank adaptation, together with modality ablations and comparisons with clinical and multimodal baselines. The results show that a frozen CT-CLIP model combined with a trainable lightweight survival head outperforms the clinical baseline and achieves comparable or improved performance relative to other multimodal approaches, and separates patients into clinically meaningful high- and low-risk groups.
artigos.titulo_en: CT-CLIP Representations for Multimodal Lung Cancer Survival Prediction
artigos.titulo_pt: Representações CT-CLIP para Predição de Sobrevida em Câncer de Pulmão Multimodal
artigos.resumo_executivo_en: The study evaluates CT-CLIP, a domain-specific foundation model, for multimodal survival prediction in lung cancer with limited data constraints. A frozen CT-CLIP model combined with a trainable lightweight survival head outperforms clinical baselines and achieves comparable or improved performance relative to other multimodal approaches. The approach effectively stratifies patients into clinically meaningful high- and low-risk groups using pretreatment CT images and clinical variables from 242 lung cancer patients.
artigos.resumo_executivo_pt: O estudo avalia o uso de representações de um modelo de fundação específico de domínio (CT-CLIP) para predição de sobrevida multimodal em câncer de pulmão com dados limitados. O modelo congelado CT-CLIP combinado com um cabeçalho de sobrevida treinável supera a linha de base clínica e separa pacientes em grupos de risco clinicamente significativos. A abordagem demonstra eficácia em ambientes clínicos com restrições de dados, utilizando imagens de tomografia computadorizada e variáveis clínicas de 242 pacientes diagnosticados.
artigos.justificativa: A solução aborda um problema crítico em oncologia (prognóstico de câncer de pulmão) com aplicação direta em ambientes clínicos, demonstrando viabilidade em cenários com dados limitados, o que é altamente relevante para implementação em sistemas de saúde reais.

artigos.titulo: Cross-seed explainability using Procrustes-conditioned Joint End-to-end Top-K Sparse Autoencoders
artigos.resumo: We present a Procrustes-conditioned Joint End-to-end Top-K Sparse Autoencoder (SAE) for extracting cross-seed universal features from independently trained BERT models. Cross-seed feature universality is a fundamental challenge in mechanistic interpretability: because dictionary learning is non-convex, independently trained networks learn misaligned feature spaces, so apparently identical features may differ by random initialization. We address this by computing an orthogonal Procrustes rotation between seeds' activation spaces before joint SAE training, combining Top-K sparsity, end-to-end downstream optimization, and an auxiliary dead-feature revival loss based on previous SAE literature. Evaluating on five independent seed pairs (ten BERT models) across three benchmark datasets (SST-2, Stanford Politeness, TweetEval Emotion), our full pipeline produces more universal features (Pearson r $\geq$ 0.70 across seeds) than post-hoc alignment baselines on all three datasets. A minimal qualitative analysis confirms that high-universality features encode interpretable sociolinguistic patterns.
artigos.titulo_en: Cross-seed explainability using Procrustes-conditioned Joint End-to-end Top-K Sparse Autoencoders
artigos.titulo_pt: Explicabilidade entre sementes usando Autoencodificadores Esparsos Top-K Conjuntos Fim-a-Fim Condicionados por Procrustes
artigos.resumo_executivo_en: The work presents a Procrustes-conditioned sparse autoencoder method to extract universal features across independently trained BERT models, addressing the feature space misalignment challenge caused by non-convex dictionary learning. The approach combines Procrustes orthogonal rotation, Top-K sparsity, and end-to-end optimization, producing more universal features (Pearson correlation ≥ 0.70) across three benchmark datasets. Results demonstrate that high-universality features encode interpretable sociolinguistic patterns.
artigos.resumo_executivo_pt: O trabalho apresenta um método de autoencodificador esparso condicionado por Procrustes para extrair características universais entre modelos BERT treinados independentemente, resolvendo o desafio de desalinhamento de espaços de características causado pela não-convexidade do aprendizado de dicionário. A abordagem combina rotação ortogonal de Procrustes, esparsidade Top-K e otimização fim-a-fim, produzindo características mais universais (correlação de Pearson ≥ 0,70) em três conjuntos de dados de referência. Os resultados demonstram que as características de alta universalidade codificam padrões sociolinguísticos interpretáveis.
artigos.justificativa: A técnica de explicabilidade e interpretabilidade de modelos de linguagem é relevante para aplicações que exigem transparência e confiabilidade em sistemas de NLP, particularmente em contextos regulatórios e de análise de sentimentos. Porém, o foco em mecanismos internos de interpretação limita seu impacto direto em produtos comerciais.

artigos.titulo: Cognitive-structured Multimodal Agent for Multimodal Understanding, Generation, and Editing
artigos.resumo: Recent unified multimodal models show a single architecture can jointly perform vision/language understanding and image generation/editing. However, they repeatedly feed all historical visual and textual inputs into a shared context window, limiting long-horizon multimodal dialogue due to visual token explosion and unreliable cross-turn referencing. We propose a Cognitive-structured Multimodal Agent that externalizes visual information into an Episodic Visual Memory and selectively reactivates relevant episodes during reasoning. The agent consists of a Perceptual Abstraction Engine for structured visual abstraction, a Cognitive Retrieval Engine for cross-turn memory retrieval, and a Multimodal Executive Controller for autonomous task inference and action planning. To address the lack of turn-level retrieval supervision in existing datasets, we develop a Unified Scenario Engine that programmatically generates structured multi-turn conversations with fine-grained retrieval annotations, enabling reinforcement learning to optimize abstraction and retrieval policies. We also construct a long-horizon visual-dialogue benchmark stratified by difficulty to evaluate episodic visual recall. Our 8B agent achieves 91.4% retrieval accuracy over 20-turn sessions, surpassing 32B baselines by +8.2% while nearly halving per-turn inference time (23.1s -> 12.7s). We further present the Cognitive-structured Multimodal Agent Harness (CMA-Harness), a tool-augmented deployment of the same cognitive structure integrating persistent multimodal memory, web access, image generation/editing/composition tools, and OpenAI-compatible serving. Structured memory and modular decision-making offer a more scalable, efficient paradigm for long-horizon multimodal agents than monolithic parameter scaling. Code: https://github.com/caseclose/cma-harness ; Project page: https://caseclose.github.io/cma-harness/
artigos.titulo_en: Cognitive-structured Multimodal Agent for Multimodal Understanding, Generation, and Editing
artigos.titulo_pt: Agente Multimodal Estruturado Cognitivamente para Compreensão, Geração e Edição Multimodal
artigos.resumo_executivo_en: Proposes a cognitive-structured multimodal agent that externalizes visual information into episodic memory to overcome long-horizon dialogue limitations in unified models. The system integrates perceptual abstraction, cognitive retrieval, and executive control, achieving 91.4% retrieval accuracy over 20-turn sessions with 45% reduction in inference time. This modular approach offers a more scalable and efficient paradigm than monolithic parameter scaling, with code and deployment tools provided.
artigos.resumo_executivo_pt: Propõe um agente multimodal que externaliza informações visuais em memória episódica para superar limitações de diálogos longos em modelos unificados.
