O sistema integra abstração perceptual, recuperação cognitiva e controle executivo, alcançando 91,4% de precisão em recuperação visual em sessões de 20 turnos com redução de 45% no tempo de inferência. A abordagem oferece um paradigma mais escalável e eficiente que o aumento paramétrico monolítico, com código e ferramenta de implantação disponibilizados.
artigos.justificativa: O trabalho endereça desafios críticos em agentes multimodais de longa duração com aplicações diretas em assistentes conversacionais, sistemas de edição de conteúdo e plataformas de geração de mídia, demonstrando ganhos significativos em eficiência computacional e precisão que viabilizam implantação em produção.

artigos.titulo: The Context Access Divide: Interaction-Level Architecture as a Complementary Dimension of Agentic Inequality
artigos.resumo: Sharp et al. (2025) introduce "agentic inequality" as a framework for analyzing disparities in access to AI agents across three dimensions: availability, quality, and quantity. These person- and organization-level dimensions characterize who can access agents and at what capability, but do not address a structurally important divide operating at a finer level: the individual interaction. Two users with nominally equivalent agent access may experience qualitatively different AI utility depending on whether the system can autonomously retrieve context from the user's knowledge corpus (Dynamic Context Retrieval) or requires the user to manually identify and attach relevant documents at each query (Manual Attachment). We term this the Context Access Divide (CAD). For knowledge-intensive workers whose intellectual capital spans tens of thousands of files, the CAD constitutes a qualitative threshold in AI usefulness: below it, the cognitive burden of context curation falls on the human, reproducing the inefficiencies AI is meant to eliminate. We propose contextuality -- the degree to which an AI system autonomously accesses a user's accumulated knowledge capital -- as a dimension of AI-mediated inequality that complements, but is not reducible to, the Sharp et al. framework. We formalize the CAD with a probabilistic model grounded in the fan effect literature in cognitive psychology, demonstrating that manual context attachment leads to a combinatorial collapse in task-success probability as corpus size and task conjunctivity grow, while dynamic retrieval architectures are structurally insulated from this collapse. We analyze the technical basis of this divide in the Model Context Protocol (MCP) and retrieval-augmented generation (RAG) architectures, and examine its implications for knowledge-work stratification and AI platform governance.
artigos.titulo_en: The Context Access Divide: Interaction-Level Architecture as a Complementary Dimension of Agentic Inequality
artigos.titulo_pt: A Divisão de Acesso ao Contexto: Arquitetura em Nível de Interação como Dimensão Complementar da Desigualdade Agêntica
artigos.resumo_executivo_en: The paper introduces the "Context Access Divide" (CAD) as a complementary dimension of agentic inequality, focusing on how AI systems with dynamic context retrieval versus manual document attachment create qualitative disparities in utility for knowledge workers. Through probabilistic modeling grounded in cognitive psychology, it demonstrates that manual context attachment leads to combinatorial collapse in task-success probability as corpus size grows, while dynamic retrieval architectures are structurally insulated from this collapse. The work analyzes technical implications in protocols like MCP and RAG, with impacts for knowledge-work stratification and AI platform governance.
artigos.resumo_executivo_pt: O artigo introduz o conceito de "Divisão de Acesso ao Contexto" (CAD) como uma dimensão complementar da desigualdade agêntica, focando em como sistemas de IA com recuperação dinâmica de contexto versus anexação manual de documentos criam disparidades qualitativas na utilidade para trabalhadores do conhecimento. Através de modelagem probabilística baseada em psicologia cognitiva, demonstra-se que a anexação manual de contexto leva a um colapso combinatorial na probabilidade de sucesso conforme o tamanho do corpus cresce, enquanto arquiteturas de recuperação dinâmica são estruturalmente protegidas desse colapso. O trabalho analisa as implicações técnicas em protocolos como MCP e RAG, com impactos para estratificação do trabalho do conhecimento e governança de plataformas de IA.
artigos.justificativa: O trabalho aborda uma desigualdade estrutural crítica em sistemas de IA empresariais que afeta diretamente a produtividade de trabalhadores do conhecimento e a adoção de plataformas de IA, com implicações diretas para design de produtos, estratégia de governança e equidade no acesso a tecnologia.

artigos.titulo: Ensemble Diversity Optimization for Subjective Supervision
artigos.resumo: Subjective NLP tasks often exhibit systematic annotator disagreement, requiring models that represent uncertainty rather than collapse it. We introduce Ensemble Diversity Optimization (EDO), a prediction-space framework that jointly optimizes ensemble weights, effective cardinality, and calibration through a unified differentiable objective. EDO learns ensemble composition and size end-to-end via Gumbel-Softmax relaxation and incorporates a signed diversity regularizer, tuned on validation data, to steer optimization toward either preserving or suppressing disagreement. This regularization prevents ensemble collapse and enables controlled navigation of the utility-calibration trade-off. The framework integrates a soft F1 surrogate, class-weighted cross-entropy to address imbalance, and reliability-weighted diversity to regulate intra-ensemble variability. Experiments on four subjective text-classification benchmarks (ArMIS, ConvAbuse, HS-Brexit, MD-Agreement) show that EDO substantially improves probabilistic calibration, reducing cross-entropy (40-78% depending on baseline) and lowering Brier scores relative to Soft-CE, Soft-MD, Top-5 Voting, and WEL, while maintaining competitive F1 and better alignment with annotator distributions. These results demonstrate that jointly optimizing ensemble structure with a signed diversity regularizer provides an efficient, model-agnostic approach for modeling human subjectivity in supervised learning.
artigos.titulo_en: Ensemble Diversity Optimization for Subjective Supervision
artigos.titulo_pt: Otimização da Diversidade de Ensemble para Supervisão Subjetiva
artigos.resumo_executivo_en: The paper introduces Ensemble Diversity Optimization (EDO), a framework that jointly optimizes ensemble weights, effective cardinality, and calibration for subjective NLP tasks with systematic annotator disagreement. EDO employs Gumbel-Softmax relaxation and signed diversity regularization to control the utility-calibration trade-off while preventing ensemble collapse. Experiments on four subjective text-classification benchmarks demonstrate substantial improvements in probabilistic calibration, reducing cross-entropy by 40-78% and Brier scores while maintaining competitive F1 and better alignment with annotator distributions.
artigos.resumo_executivo_pt: O artigo apresenta Ensemble Diversity Optimization (EDO), um framework que otimiza conjuntamente pesos de ensemble, cardinalidade efetiva e calibração para tarefas NLP subjetivas com desacordo sistemático entre anotadores. EDO utiliza relaxação Gumbel-Softmax e regularização de diversidade assinada para controlar a navegação entre utilidade e calibração, prevenindo colapso de ensemble. Experimentos em quatro benchmarks de classificação de texto subjetiva demonstram melhorias substanciais em calibração probabilística, reduzindo cross-entropy em 40-78% e scores de Brier, mantendo F1 competitivo e melhor alinhamento com distribuições de anotadores.
artigos.justificativa: O framework EDO aborda um problema crítico em aplicações NLP reais: modelar incerteza e desacordo humano em tarefas subjetivas. A melhoria de 40-78% em calibração probabilística e a capacidade de alinhar modelos com distribuições de anotadores têm aplicação direta em sistemas de classificação de texto, análise de sentimento e moderação de conteúdo em produção.

artigos.titulo: Drift-Aware Temporal Graph Rewiring (DATGR) for Adaptive Semantic Modeling in Biomedical Text
artigos.resumo: Biomedical language evolves rapidly as new discoveries emerge, causing traditional text models to lose semantic fidelity over time. Static embeddings and co-occurrence graphs cannot capture such evolution, leading to performance degradation in retrieval and knowledge discovery tasks. This paper introduces a Drift-Aware Temporal Graph Rewiring (DATGR) framework that models concept evolution by dynamically updating co-occurrence edges based on estimated semantic drift. Instead of retraining embeddings for each time slice, DATGR performs lightweight, feedback-driven rewiring using a logistic update rule applied to edge weights. Evaluated on the Biomedical Multi-Relation Corpus (BIOMRC), the method achieved a mean Area Under the Receiver Operating Characteristic (AUROC) improvement of approximately 0.066 absolute difference (0.699 vs. 0.633) over a static baseline. Area Under the Precision-Recall Curve (AUPRC) remained comparable (0.738 vs. 0.744), showing that drift-aware adaptation enhances link-prediction recall without a loss in precision. These results demonstrate that edge-level adaptation effectively captures temporal semantic change in evolving biomedical text while remaining computationally efficient and interpretable.
artigos.titulo_en: Drift-Aware Temporal Graph Rewiring (DATGR) for Adaptive Semantic Modeling in Biomedical Text
artigos.titulo_pt: Rewiring de Grafo Temporal Consciente de Drift (DATGR) para Modelagem Semântica Adaptativa em Texto Biomédico
artigos.resumo_executivo_en: The paper introduces DATGR, a framework that models concept evolution in biomedical texts through dynamic updating of co-occurrence graphs based on estimated semantic drift. The method achieves a 0.066 AUROC improvement (0.699 vs. 0.633) over static baselines while maintaining computational efficiency and interpretability. Edge-level adaptation effectively captures temporal semantic changes in evolving biomedical text without requiring full embedding retraining.
artigos.resumo_executivo_pt: O artigo apresenta DATGR, um framework que modela a evolução de conceitos em textos biomédicos através de atualização dinâmica de grafos de co-ocorrência baseada em drift semântico estimado. O método alcança melhoria de 0.066 em AUROC (0.699 vs. 0.633) comparado a baseline estático, mantendo eficiência computacional e interpretabilidade. A abordagem demonstra que adaptação em nível de aresta captura efetivamente mudanças semânticas temporais em textos biomédicos em evolução.
artigos.justificativa: A solução aborda um problema crítico em aplicações biomédicas reais: a degradação de modelos de linguagem conforme novos conhecimentos emergem. A melhoria significativa em AUROC (6.6 pontos percentuais) e a eficiência computacional tornam a abordagem viável para sistemas de recuperação de informação e descoberta de conhecimento em domínios que evoluem rapidamente.

artigos.titulo: VEGAS: Human-Aligned Video Caption Evaluation via Gaze
artigos.resumo: Vision-language models excel at video captioning, yet typically generate descriptions that fail to capture individual viewers' attention. We propose VEGAS (Video caption Evaluation via GAze Score), a training-free metric that leverages test-time gaze to sample personalized, attention-aligned text. It is a cross-modal, information-theoretic metric that quantifies how well a candidate caption matches a viewer's focus. To evaluate VEGAS, we curate a dataset of egocentric activities and instructional slides paired with synchronized gaze and reference annotations. We then select captions based on VEGAS via rejection sampling without model retraining. Experiments show that VEGAS-selected captions align significantly better with human focus and improve downstream caption-to-video retrieval, demonstrating the practical utility of incorporating viewer attention during inference.
artigos.titulo_en: VEGAS: Human-Aligned Video Caption Evaluation via Gaze
artigos.titulo_pt: VEGAS: Avaliação de Legendas de Vídeo Alinhadas ao Humano via Rastreamento de Olhar
artigos.resumo_executivo_en: VEGAS is a training-free metric for video caption evaluation that leverages gaze data to generate personalized descriptions aligned with viewer attention. The cross-modal, information-theoretic approach selects captions via rejection sampling without model retraining. Experiments demonstrate that VEGAS-selected captions significantly improve alignment with human focus and downstream caption-to-video retrieval performance.
artigos.resumo_executivo_pt: VEGAS é uma métrica de avaliação de legendas de vídeo que incorpora dados de rastreamento de olhar para gerar descrições personalizadas alinhadas à atenção do espectador. A abordagem é baseada em teoria da informação e não requer retreinamento de modelos, utilizando amostragem por rejeição durante a inferência. Experimentos demonstram que legendas selecionadas por VEGAS melhoram significativamente o alinhamento com o foco humano e a recuperação de vídeos a partir de legendas.
artigos.justificativa: A métrica VEGAS oferece uma solução prática para melhorar a qualidade de legendas de vídeo em aplicações comerciais de acessibilidade, educação e recuperação de conteúdo, incorporando dados de atenção do usuário sem necessidade de retreinamento de modelos.

artigos.titulo: Frequency-Domain Multi-Modality Transportation Modeling
artigos.resumo: Multi-modality transportation refers to urban systems composed of multiple transportation modes, such as traffic flow and public transit, whose dynamics are coupled by shared temporal patterns. Accurate multi-modality transportation forecasting remains challenging because (1) different modalities exhibit distinct spectral characteristics and (2) interact unevenly across frequencies, whereas most existing methods operate primarily in the time domain or rely on coarse feature fusion. To address these limitations, we propose a lightweight yet effective Frequency-Domain Multi-Modality modeling (FreMo) that explicitly exploits the frequency domain to enable adaptive and selective cross-modality synergy. FreMo disentangles modality-wise spectral refinement from cross-modality synergy and supports plug-and-play integration with general time series backbones. Specifically, FreMo introduces a Modality-Wise Frequency Filter (MFF) to adaptively refine spectral components within each modality, emphasizing informative frequencies while suppressing noise. FreMo further incorporates a Frequency-Guided Synergy Integrator (FSI) that selectively aggregates information across modalities based on their relative contribution at each frequency, facilitating effective cross-modality knowledge sharing while mitigating negative transfer. Extensive experiments on real-world datasets show that FreMo consistently outperforms state-of-the-art baselines, with superior performance and generalization across diverse forecasting scenarios. The code is available at https://github.com/beginner-sketch/FreMo.
artigos.titulo_en: Frequency-Domain Multi-Modality Transportation Modeling
artigos.titulo_pt: Modelagem de Transportes Multi-Modalidade no Domínio da Frequência
artigos.resumo_executivo_en: The paper proposes FreMo, a lightweight and effective method for multi-modality transportation modeling that exploits the frequency domain to capture distinct spectral characteristics and interactions between different transportation modes. The method introduces a Modality-Wise Frequency Filter (MFF) for adaptive spectral refinement and a Frequency-Guided Synergy Integrator (FSI) for selective cross-modality information aggregation. Experiments on real-world datasets demonstrate superior performance and better generalization across diverse forecasting scenarios.
artigos.resumo_executivo_pt: O artigo propõe FreMo, um método leve e eficaz para modelagem de transportes multi-modalidade que explora o domínio da frequência para capturar características espectrais distintas e interações entre diferentes modos de transporte. O método introduz um Filtro de Frequência Modality-Wise (MFF) para refinar adaptativamente componentes espectrais e um Integrador de Sinergia Guiado por Frequência (FSI) para agregar seletivamente informações entre modalidades. Experimentos em conjuntos de dados reais demonstram desempenho superior e melhor generalização em diversos cenários de previsão.
artigos.justificativa: A previsão precisa de transportes multi-modalidade é crítica para planejamento urbano e otimização de sistemas de mobilidade, com aplicações diretas em cidades inteligentes e gestão de tráfego. O método proposto oferece melhor generalização e desempenho, potencialmente reduzindo custos operacionais e melhorando a eficiência dos sistemas de transporte público.

artigos.titulo: MatBind: A Shared Embedding Space for Multimodal Materials Characterization
artigos.resumo: Fully characterizing a crystalline material requires integrating heterogeneous data sources -- atomic structures, diffraction patterns, electronic density of states, and natural language -- each of which captures a different facet of the same physical object. In practice, however, these modalities are stored and analyzed in isolation, making it difficult to relate or query materials across representational boundaries. We present MatBind, a contrastive learning framework that aligns four materials modalities -- crystal structure, powder X-ray diffraction (pXRD) simulated from structures, density of states (DOS), and text -- into a unified embedding space using crystal structure as the central physical anchor. The framework induces alignment between modalities never explicitly paired during training, enabling emergent zero-shot cross-modal retrieval as a direct consequence of the shared representation. The learned embedding space organizes materials according to physically meaningful properties without explicit supervision, and retrieval performance improves systematically when modalities are combined at query time. These results demonstrate that treating heterogeneous materials data as complementary projections of a single physical reality, rather than as isolated data sources, is not a practical choice but is consistent with the underlying physics.
artigos.titulo_en: MatBind: A Shared Embedding Space for Multimodal Materials Characterization
artigos.titulo_pt: MatBind: Um Espaço de Embedding Compartilhado para Caracterização Multimodal de Materiais
artigos.resumo_executivo_en: MatBind is a contrastive learning framework that aligns four materials data modalities -- crystal structure, powder X-ray diffraction, density of states, and text -- into a unified embedding space. The system enables zero-shot cross-modal retrieval between modalities never explicitly paired during training, organizing materials by physically meaningful properties without explicit supervision. The approach demonstrates that heterogeneous materials data should be treated as complementary projections of a single physical reality, with systematic performance improvements when modalities are combined.
artigos.resumo_executivo_pt: MatBind é um framework de aprendizado contrastivo que alinha quatro modalidades de dados de materiais -- estrutura cristalina, difração de raios X em pó, densidade de estados e texto -- em um espaço de embedding unificado. O sistema permite recuperação zero-shot entre modalidades nunca explicitamente pareadas durante o treinamento, organizando materiais segundo propriedades fisicamente significativas sem supervisão explícita. A abordagem demonstra que dados heterogêneos de materiais devem ser tratados como projeções complementares de uma única realidade física, melhorando sistematicamente o desempenho quando modalidades são combinadas.
artigos.justificativa: MatBind tem alta relevância para negócios em ciência de materiais e indústrias de manufatura avançada, pois permite integração eficiente de dados heterogêneos para descoberta e caracterização de novos materiais. A capacidade de recuperação zero-shot entre modalidades reduz custos de anotação e acelera ciclos de pesquisa e desenvolvimento em aplicações críticas como semicondutores, baterias e materiais funcionais.

artigos.titulo: Applying JEPA-Style Predictive Learning to JA4-Derived Network Fingerprints
artigos.resumo: I-JEPA and V-JEPA learn by matching latent predictions to target encoder outputs rather than regenerating the original input, and this has worked well for images and video. We explore whether the same objective works for compact network fingerprints. We built JA4-JEPA, a Transformer-based model trained on JA4, JA4H, JA4S, and JA4X subfields drawn from JA4DB and CIC-IDS- 2017. The training data combines roughly 397K samples from both sources, though no single sample contains all four view families. We evaluated the learned representations with a frozen kNN probe on protocol-family classification across TLS, DNS, and SSH. On 39,416 heldout samples the model achieved a cosine similarity of 0.9899 and a kNN accuracy of 0.9220. These results indicate that JEPA-style predictive learning can produce useful embeddings from JA4-derived fingerprints, even with incomplete view overlap across sources.   Keywords: JA4, network fingerprinting, JEPA, predictive representation learning, self-supervised learning
artigos.titulo_en: Applying JEPA-Style Predictive Learning to JA4-Derived Network Fingerprints
artigos.titulo_pt: Aplicando Aprendizado Preditivo no Estilo JEPA a Impressões Digitais de Rede Derivadas de JA4
artigos.resumo_executivo_en: The work explores applying JEPA-style predictive learning to JA4 network fingerprints, developing JA4-JEPA, a Transformer-based model trained on 397K samples from multiple sources. The model achieved cosine similarity of 0.9899 and kNN accuracy of 0.9220 on protocol-family classification across TLS, DNS, and SSH. Results demonstrate that self-supervised predictive learning can generate useful embeddings for network fingerprinting even with incomplete view overlap across data sources.
artigos.resumo_executivo_pt: O trabalho explora a aplicação de aprendizado preditivo no estilo JEPA (Joint-Embedding Predictive Architecture) para impressões digitais de rede JA4, desenvolvendo o modelo JA4-JEPA baseado em Transformer. Treinado com 397K amostras de múltiplas fontes, o modelo alcançou similaridade de cosseno de 0.9899 e acurácia kNN de 0.9220 em classificação de famílias de protocolo. Os resultados demonstram que aprendizado preditivo auto-supervisionado pode gerar embeddings úteis para fingerprinting de rede, mesmo com sobreposição incompleta entre diferentes visualizações de dados.
artigos.justificativa: A técnica apresenta aplicação direta em segurança de rede e detecção de anomalias, com métricas de desempenho robustas (0.9220 de acurácia) em classificação de protocolos de rede, sendo relevante para soluções de monitoramento e análise de tráfego em ambientes corporativos.

artigos.titulo: Two Axes of LLM Abstention: Answer Correctness and Question Answerability
artigos.resumo: A model should refuse two different things: answers it would get wrong, and questions it should not answer at all, such as unanswerable ones or ones resting on a false premise. The usual recipe thresholds a single confidence score, which cannot tell these apart. Across five instruction-tuned models from three families (2B to 14B), we find they are separate axes. Ordinary answer-confidence tracks whether an answer is right but is nearly blind to whether the question is answerable; a linear probe on hidden states does the reverse. The blind spot does not shrink with scale. It is worst on naturally occurring false-premise questions (CREPE). There, answer-confidence, P(IK), P(True), and even asking the model outright whether a premise is false all stay near chance, while a hidden-state probe reaches 0.69 to 0.77 AUROC: the model represents a problem it will not report. This turns out to be fixable. Instructing a model to check premises backfires, because it then disputes sound and false premises alike (57% false challenges), unable to tell them apart; routing the same instruction with the probe roughly triples challenge precision. We turn the two axes into a calibrated policy that answers only when an answerability score and a correctness score each clear a separately certifies behave differently: the unanswerable-answer rate is controllable at every scale, while the wrong-answer rate is capped by model accuracy, so the guarantee tightens as threshold policy certifies both budgets at 0.75 coverage of correct answers, against 0.31 for a single threshold; at 14B it is the only policy that certifies at all.
artigos.titulo_en: Two Axes of LLM Abstention: Answer Correctness and Question Answerability
artigos.titulo_pt: Dois Eixos de Abstenção em LLMs: Correção de Respostas e Respondibilidade de Perguntas
artigos.resumo_executivo_en: The work demonstrates that language models should refuse two distinct types of requests: incorrect answers and unanswerable questions or those based on false premises, which cannot be differentiated by a single confidence score. Through analysis across five instruction-tuned models, the authors identify that answer confidence and question answerability are independent axes, with confidence being blind to answerability. The proposed solution uses a calibrated policy with two separate scores (answerability and correctness), achieving 0.75 coverage of correct answers versus 0.31 with single threshold, and being the only policy that certifies guarantees in the 14B model.
artigos.resumo_executivo_pt: O trabalho demonstra que modelos de linguagem devem recusar dois tipos distintos de solicitações: respostas incorretas e perguntas inanswerable ou baseadas em premissas falsas, que não podem ser diferenciadas por um único score de confiança. Através de análise em cinco modelos instruction-tuned, os autores identificam que a confiança de resposta e a respondibilidade da pergunta são eixos independentes, com a confiança sendo cega à respondibilidade. A solução proposta utiliza uma política calibrada com dois scores separados (answerability e correctness), alcançando 0.75 de cobertura de respostas corretas contra 0.31 com threshold único, e sendo a única política que certifica garantias no modelo de 14B.
artigos.justificativa: A capacidade de modelos de linguagem recusarem respostas incorretas e perguntas inanswerable é crítica para aplicações de produção, especialmente em domínios sensíveis como suporte ao cliente, assistência jurídica e saúde, onde respostas incorretas ou inapropriadas podem causar danos significativos.

artigos.titulo: Spatio-Temporal Scheduling Prediction Under Backhaul Delay for Resilient Coordinated Beamforming
artigos.resumo: Coordinated beamforming in distributed 5G networks relies on the timely exchange of inter-cell scheduling information, but backhaul latency makes this information stale. Even a single transmission time interval (TTI) of delay can reduce CBF-SLNR performance below the uncoordinated baseline, because the precoder suppresses interference toward users that are no longer active. Coordination on stale information is therefore worse than no coordination at all. To address this, we propose a two-stage predictive framework in which a Spectral Temporal Graph Neural Network (StemGNN) predicts future user equipment (UE) scheduling states from delayed historical observations, and the predictions replace stale inputs to the CBF-SLNR precoder. Evaluated on a three-cell massive MIMO downlink with 60 UEs and 64 antennas per base station under Quadriga Urban Micro (UMi) channels and a proportional fair scheduler, StemGNN achieves a mean scheduling prediction accuracy of 87.57%, outperforming LSTM, GRU, Simple RNN, and Markov chain baselines at all evaluated horizons, with gains of up to 7.71% over LSTM at longer horizons where inter-UE structural dependencies dominate over temporal autocorrelation. When integrated into coordinated beamforming, the predictions recover 57-73% of the sum rate loss caused by one TTI of backhaul delay, improving sum rate by 9.58-14.35% over the no-prediction baseline and recovering up to 83% of the Lag-1 fairness loss for cell-edge users, with fairness gains persisting at higher lag values where throughput gains diminish. These results show that treating backhaul latency as a spatio-temporal forecasting problem is an effective approach for robust inter-cell coordination in delay-constrained networks.
artigos.titulo_en: Spatio-Temporal Scheduling Prediction Under Backhaul Delay for Resilient Coordinated Beamforming
artigos.titulo_pt: Previsão de Agendamento Espaço-Temporal sob Atraso de Backhaul para Beamforming Coordenado Resiliente
artigos.resumo_executivo_en: The paper proposes a predictive framework using Spectral Temporal Graph Neural Networks (StemGNN) to forecast user equipment scheduling states in distributed 5G networks, mitigating backhaul latency effects on coordinated beamforming performance. The model achieves 87.57% scheduling prediction accuracy, outperforming LSTM and GRU baselines, and recovers 57-73% of sum rate loss caused by transmission delays. The approach demonstrates that treating backhaul latency as a spatio-temporal forecasting problem enables robust inter-cell coordination in delay-constrained networks.
artigos.resumo_executivo_pt: O artigo propõe um framework preditivo baseado em Redes Neurais Gráficas Espaço-Temporais (StemGNN) para prever estados de agendamento de usuários em redes 5G distribuídas, compensando atrasos de backhaul que degradam o desempenho do beamforming coordenado. O modelo alcança 87,57% de precisão na previsão de agendamento, superando baselines como LSTM e GRU, e recupera 57-73% da perda de taxa de soma causada por atrasos de latência. A abordagem demonstra que tratar latência de backhaul como um problema de previsão espaço-temporal é eficaz para coordenação inter-célula robusta em redes com restrições de atraso.
artigos.justificativa: A solução aborda um desafio crítico em redes 5G distribuídas (latência de backhaul) que impacta diretamente a qualidade de serviço e eficiência espectral, com demonstração prática em cenários realistas de massive MIMO, oferecendo ganhos mensuráveis de 9.58-14.35% em taxa de soma.

artigos.titulo: Predicting Viticulture Potential through an Ensemble of U-Net and a Geospatial Foundation Model
artigos.resumo: Determining agricultural potential is fundamental to sustainable land management and agricultural planning. Remote sensing data is increasingly valuable as an avenue for agricultural potential due to the cost of traditional methods (surveys, in-situ measurements, soil testing, etc). ImageCLEF AI4Agri 2026: Subtask 1 is concerned with the prediction of viticulture potential in Southern France. The DS@GT ARC's submission for Subtask 1 introduces an ensemble of U-Net and a Geospatial Foundation Model (Prithvi-2.0). Our best model achieved a $\pm$1 accuracy of 68.32 on the leaderboard, ranking 2nd among 7 teams. The implementation for this work is publicly available at https://github.com/dsgt-arc/imageclef-ai4agri-2026 .
artigos.titulo_en: Predicting Viticulture Potential through an Ensemble of U-Net and a Geospatial Foundation Model
artigos.titulo_pt: Predição do Potencial de Viticultura através de um Ensemble de U-Net e um Modelo de Fundação Geoespacial
artigos.resumo_executivo_en: The work presents an ensemble solution combining U-Net and the Prithvi-2.0 geospatial foundation model to predict viticulture potential in Southern France using remote sensing data. The model achieved 68.32% accuracy (±1), ranking 2nd among 7 teams in the ImageCLEF AI4Agri 2026 challenge. The approach demonstrates the feasibility of using geospatial foundation models to optimize sustainable agricultural planning.
artigos.resumo_executivo_pt: O trabalho apresenta uma solução de ensemble combinando U-Net e o modelo de fundação geoespacial Prithvi-2.0 para prever o potencial de viticultura na França meridional utilizando dados de sensoriamento remoto. O modelo alcançou 68,32% de acurácia (±1), classificando-se em 2º lugar entre 7 equipes no desafio ImageCLEF AI4Agri 2026. A abordagem demonstra a viabilidade de usar modelos de fundação geoespaciais para otimizar o planejamento agrícola sustentável.
artigos.justificativa: A solução oferece uma alternativa de baixo custo aos métodos tradicionais de avaliação agrícola (levantamentos, medições in-situ, testes de solo), com potencial de aplicação em planejamento agrícola sustentável e gestão de terras em larga escala.

artigos.titulo: Statistical Efficiency and Inference of Quantile Distributional Reinforcement Learning
artigos.resumo: In this paper, we study quantile-based distributional reinforcement learning from the perspective of statistical efficiency. We focus on distributional policy evaluation, whose goal is to characterize the return distribution, namely the distribution of discounted cumulative rewards under a given policy. To obtain a finite-dimensional representation of the return distribution, we consider the quantile fixed point $η_m$ induced by the quantile-projected distributional Bellman equation. Assuming access to a generative model, we construct an estimator $η_m^{(n)}$ based on an empirical Markov decision process. For a fixed number of quantiles $m$, we establish a non-asymptotic error bound for $η_m^{(n)}$ and $η_m$ under the supremum $W_\infty$ metric, showing that the estimation error scales as $\widetilde{O}(\sqrt{m/n})$ with respect to $m$ and $n$. This implies that the quantile-based distributional policy evaluation problem can be solved with sample efficiency, achieving the optimal parametric $\sqrt{n}$ convergence rate. We derive the asymptotic distribution of the quantile parameters $\sqrt{n}(θ_m^{(n)}-θ_m)$ and characterize the semiparametric efficiency bound, which is attained by our estimator. Beyond the fixed-dimensional setting, we investigate the asymptotic regime in which the number of quantiles diverges. We characterize the limit covariance structure and show that it matches the semiparametric efficiency bound of the nonparametric model for distributional policy evaluation, showing that quantile-based estimators remain asymptotically efficient in the infinite-dimensional limit. Finally, we establish a Berry--Esseen theorem for smooth functionals $\sqrt{n}(η_m^{(n)}(s)-η_m(s))f$, thereby providing a foundation for statistically valid inference on functionals of the quantile-projected return distribution.
artigos.titulo_en: Statistical Efficiency and Inference of Quantile Distributional Reinforcement Learning
artigos.titulo_pt: Eficiência Estatística e Inferência em Aprendizado por Reforço Distribucional Baseado em Quantis
artigos.resumo_executivo_en: This paper investigates the statistical efficiency of quantile-based distributional reinforcement learning, focusing on distributional policy evaluation that characterizes the return distribution. The authors establish non-asymptotic error bounds and demonstrate optimal parametric √n convergence rates, achieving semiparametric efficiency bounds. The work provides theoretical foundations for statistically valid inference on functionals of the quantile-projected return distribution.
artigos.resumo_executivo_pt: Este artigo estuda a eficiência estatística do aprendizado por reforço distribucional baseado em quantis, focando na avaliação de políticas que caracterizam a distribuição de retornos acumulados. Os autores estabelecem limites de erro não-assintóticos e demonstram que o problema pode ser resolvido com taxa de convergência ótima de √n, atingindo eficiência semiparamétrica. O trabalho fornece fundações teóricas para inferência estatisticamente válida sobre funcionais da distribuição de retornos quantilizados.
artigos.justificativa: O trabalho contribui para a compreensão teórica de algoritmos de aprendizado por reforço distribucional, com potencial aplicação em sistemas de decisão que requerem caracterização completa de distribuições de retorno, como em finanças e otimização de risco.

artigos.titulo: ADORN: Adaptive Drift handling for Open RAN using Reinforcement Learning
artigos.resumo: Dynamic traffic variations in Open Radio Access Networks (O-RAN) lead to drift, which degrades the performance of Artificial Intelligence/Machine Learning (AI/ML) models. Traditional retraining approaches maintain forecasting accuracy but incur high computational cost and may lead to violations of Service Level Agreements (SLAs). This work proposes a Q-learning-based adaptive retraining approach that formulates the retraining decision as a Markov Decision Process (MDP), where a Reinforcement Learning (RL) agent learns a policy that balances forecasting accuracy and retraining cost. The proposed approach incorporates a multi-expert Long Short-Term Memory (LSTM) ensemble to mitigate catastrophic forgetting and improve robustness across diverse traffic conditions. Experimental results show that the proposed approach effectively reduces retraining overhead compared to greedy and random baselines, while maintaining system performance within predefined limits.
artigos.titulo_en: ADORN: Adaptive Drift handling for Open RAN using Reinforcement Learning
artigos.titulo_pt: ADORN: Tratamento Adaptativo de Desvio em Open RAN usando Aprendizado por Reforço
artigos.resumo_executivo_en: The work proposes a Q-learning-based adaptive approach to manage model drift in Open RAN networks by formulating retraining decisions as a Markov Decision Process. A reinforcement learning agent learns a policy balancing forecasting accuracy and computational retraining costs. The solution employs a multi-expert LSTM ensemble to mitigate catastrophic forgetting and demonstrates significant reduction in retraining overhead while maintaining performance within SLA limits.
artigos.resumo_executivo_pt: O trabalho propõe uma abordagem adaptativa baseada em Q-learning para gerenciar o desvio de modelos de IA/ML em redes Open RAN, formulando a decisão de retreinamento como um Processo de Decisão de Markov. Um agente de aprendizado por reforço aprende uma política que equilibra precisão de previsão e custo computacional de retreinamento. A solução utiliza um ensemble de LSTM multi-especialista para mitigar esquecimento catastrófico e demonstra redução significativa de overhead de retreinamento mantendo o desempenho dentro dos limites de SLA.
artigos.justificativa: A solução aborda um problema crítico em redes 5G/6G (Open RAN) ao reduzir custos computacionais de retreinamento de modelos enquanto mantém conformidade com SLAs, impactando diretamente na eficiência operacional e redução de custos de infraestrutura de telecomunicações.

artigos.titulo: DeltaV: Thinking with Visual State Updates in Unified Large Multimodal Models
artigos.resumo: Current Unified Large Multimodal Models (ULMMs) support interleaved multimodal reasoning through textual reasoning and intermediate visual states, but typically generate each visual state as a full image. This full-image generation paradigm introduces substantial visual-token redundancy and dilutes supervision on sparse yet reasoning-critical state transitions. We propose DeltaV, a ULMM that replaces full-image generation with visual updates. Conditioned on historical visual states, DeltaV incrementally predicts compact update tokens that capture the visual changes across reasoning steps, avoiding repeated modeling of unchanged content. To align the token budget of each update with the magnitude of visual change, DeltaV introduces a temporal similarity (TSIM) Router, which stops allocating tokens once the marginal reconstruction gain falls below a threshold. To support more diverse and generalizable reasoning, we further construct StructCoT, a large-scale interleaved multimodal reasoning dataset with 1.05M samples spanning 44 task domains. Experiments show that the visual-update paradigm reduces newly generated visual tokens by 55.6\% on average without compromising reconstruction fidelity, and improves multimodal reasoning by 3.3\% over full-image generation. Trained with StructCoT and large-scale multimodal data, DeltaV-2B further outperforms substantially larger open-source models by 8.4\% on in-domain multimodal reasoning evaluations and surpasses the comparable-scale Qwen3-VL-2B by 5.9\% on external multimodal reasoning and understanding benchmarks. Code, models, and StructCoT will be released at https://github.com/Pengjie-W/DeltaV.
artigos.titulo_en: DeltaV: Thinking with Visual State Updates in Unified Large Multimodal Models
artigos.titulo_pt: DeltaV: Pensamento com Atualizações de Estado Visual em Modelos Multimodais Grandes Unificados
artigos.resumo_executivo_en: DeltaV is a unified multimodal model that replaces full-image generation with incremental visual updates, reducing visual token redundancy by 55.6% without compromising fidelity. The model introduces a temporal similarity (TSIM) router that dynamically allocates tokens based on visual change magnitude, and is trained with StructCoT, a dataset of 1.05M interleaved multimodal reasoning samples. DeltaV-2B substantially outperforms larger open-source models by 8.4% on multimodal reasoning evaluations and improves 3.3% over full-image generation.
artigos.resumo_executivo_pt: DeltaV é um modelo multimodal unificado que substitui a geração de imagens completas por atualizações visuais incrementais, reduzindo redundância de tokens visuais em 55,6% sem comprometer a fidelidade. O modelo introduz um roteador de similaridade temporal (TSIM) que aloca tokens dinamicamente conforme a magnitude da mudança visual, e é treinado com StructCoT, um dataset de 1,05M amostras de raciocínio multimodal interleado. DeltaV-2B supera modelos de código aberto significativamente maiores em 8,4% em avaliações de raciocínio multimodal e melhora 3,3% em relação à geração de imagens completas.
artigos.justificativa: DeltaV oferece eficiência computacional significativa (55,6% redução de tokens) e desempenho superior em raciocínio multimodal, com potencial direto para aplicações comerciais em processamento visual inteligente, análise de imagens e sistemas de IA generativa mais eficientes.

artigos.titulo: Predicting Male Fertility Using Machine Learning: A Semen Parameters Based Analysis with the VISEM Dataset
artigos.resumo: Male infertility is a significant yet often underdiagnosed aspect of reproductive health, with semen analysis serving as the cornerstone of clinical evaluation. To address this problem, this study investigates the use of machine learning algorithms to classify male fertility status based on key semen parameters, i.e., sperm concentration, motility, and morphology, using the VISEM dataset. This dataset includes semen samples from 85 participants, classified into three categories, i.e., Fertile, Sub-Fertile, and Infertile, according to the World Health Organization's criteria. After pre-processing and feature engineering, the dataset was used to train and assess multiple classification models using the LazyPredict framework. Among the more than 40 algorithms tested, the Nearest Centroid classifier achieved an accuracy of 94.2%, outperforming other models such as Support Vector Machines and Quadratic Discriminant Analysis. The model's robustness was validated using 5-fold cross-validation and multiclass ROC-AUC analysis. This study illustrates that machine learning models can provide fast, accurate, and objective assessments of semen quality, potentially supporting clinical decision-making in andrology and assisted reproductive technologies. These findings emphasize the growing potential of machine learning to enhance fertility diagnostics and inform patient-specific treatment strategies.
artigos.titulo_en: Predicting Male Fertility Using Machine Learning: A Semen Parameters Based Analysis with the VISEM Dataset
artigos.titulo_pt: Predição de Fertilidade Masculina Usando Machine Learning: Uma Análise Baseada em Parâmetros de Sêmen com o Dataset VISEM
artigos.resumo_executivo_en: This study applies machine learning algorithms to classify male fertility status based on semen parameters (concentration, motility, and morphology) using the VISEM dataset with 85 participants. The Nearest Centroid classifier achieved 94.2% accuracy, outperforming models such as SVM and QDA, with robust validation through 5-fold cross-validation and multiclass ROC-AUC analysis. Results demonstrate that ML models can provide fast, accurate, and objective assessments of semen quality, potentially supporting clinical decision-making in andrology and assisted reproductive technologies.
artigos.resumo_executivo_pt: Este estudo aplica algoritmos de machine learning para classificar o status de fertilidade masculina baseado em parâmetros de sêmen (concentração, motilidade e morfologia) utilizando o dataset VISEM com 85 participantes. O classificador Nearest Centroid alcançou 94,2% de acurácia, superando modelos como SVM e QDA, com validação robusta através de validação cruzada 5-fold e análise ROC-AUC multiclasse. Os resultados demonstram que modelos de ML podem fornecer avaliações rápidas, precisas e objetivas da qualidade do sêmen, potencialmente apoiando decisões clínicas em andrologia e tecnologias reprodutivas assistidas.
artigos.justificativa: A aplicação de ML para diagnóstico de fertilidade masculina oferece potencial comercial significativo no setor de saúde reprodutiva e tecnologias reprodutivas assistidas, com modelos de alta acurácia (94,2%) que podem ser integrados em sistemas clínicos para melhorar a objetividade e velocidade das avaliações diagnósticas.

artigos.titulo: FPGN: Redefining Ultra-Fast Programmable Gate-based Neural Acceleration with Differentiable LUTs
artigos.resumo: Achieving nanosecond-scale inference latency for deep neural networks (DNNs) has become a primary architectural concern for latency-critical applications. While Field-Programmable Gate Arrays (FPGAs) offer a promising substrate for low-latency inference, conventional FPGA accelerators remain arithmetic-centric, using LUTs primarily as building blocks for numerical operators and peripheral logic. In contrast, recent LUT-native neural networks treat LUTs as learnable neurons, revealing promising theoretical potential to exploit their intrinsic logic expressivity. However, existing methods are largely confined to algorithmic optimizations, failing to translate this theoretical potential into high-performance FPGA accelerators. Specifically, their differentiable formulations do not faithfully match FPGA LUT primitives, their physically-unaware topologies compromise routability and timing closure, and their lack of automated optimization flow hinders systematic design space exploration (DSE) and efficient hardware implementation.   In this paper, we propose FPGN, an end-to-end physically-aware framework that closes the gap between LUT-native learning and latency-optimized FPGA implementation. FPGN addresses these challenges through (i) a hardware-aligned differentiable formulation for training FPGA-native LUT neurons, (ii) a structured LUT-native topology with a streaming hardware architecture to improve routing locality and timing closure, and (iii) a latency-driven compiler that leverages high-fidelity analytical Quality of Results models to automate DSE and hardware generation. Experiments show that FPGN achieves up to 205$\times$ latency reduction compared to representative FPGA-based BNN accelerators and up to 30$\times$ higher LUT efficiency than prior differentiable LUT-native networks, while maintaining competitive inference accuracy.
artigos.titulo_en: FPGN: Redefining Ultra-Fast Programmable Gate-based Neural Acceleration with Differentiable LUTs
artigos.titulo_pt: FPGN: Redefinindo Aceleração Neural Baseada em Portas Programáveis Ultra-Rápida com LUTs Diferenciáveis
artigos.resumo_executivo_en: FPGN is an end-to-end framework that optimizes neural network inference on FPGAs through hardware-aligned differentiable LUT-native neurons. The system combines hardware-aligned formulations, structured topologies with streaming architecture, and a latency-driven compiler for automated design space exploration. Results demonstrate up to 205× latency reduction compared to FPGA-based BNN accelerators and 30× higher LUT efficiency while maintaining competitive inference accuracy.
artigos.resumo_executivo_pt: FPGN é um framework end-to-end que otimiza a inferência de redes neurais em FPGAs através de neurônios LUT diferenciáveis e nativos de hardware. O sistema combina formulações alinhadas ao hardware, topologias estruturadas com arquitetura de streaming e um compilador orientado por latência para automação do design space exploration. Os resultados demonstram redução de latência até 205× comparado a aceleradores BNN em FPGA e eficiência de LUT 30× superior, mantendo acurácia competitiva.
artigos.justificativa: O trabalho endereça aplicações críticas de latência ultra-baixa em edge computing e sistemas embarcados, oferecendo aceleração de hardware significativa (205× redução de latência) com eficiência de recursos 30× superior, viabilizando deployment de DNNs em ambientes com restrições severas de latência e consumo de energia.

artigos.titulo: OmniFood-Bench: Evaluating VLMs for Nutrient Reasoning and Personalized Health Advice
artigos.resumo: The rapid integration of Large Vision-Language Models (VLMs) into critical infrastructure promises to revolutionize   personalized healthcare and dietary management. However, in the domain of food systems, autonomous agents face a   unique and persistent challenge: the "Systemic Information Asymmetry" between visual appearance and intrinsic   nutritional composition. Existing benchmarks primarily focus on coarse-grained classification tasks, such as food   category recognition, which fail to evaluate the intricate reasoning chain required for real-world dietary management   -- specifically, the ability to traverse from identifying hidden ingredients to estimating physical mass, and finally   synthesizing safety-critical medical advice. In this paper, we introduce OmniFood-Bench, a comprehensive benchmark   constructed from the MM-Food-100K dataset. Unlike previous works, OmniFood-Bench evaluates VLMs across three   progressive capabilities: Basic Perception (Ingredients & Cooking Methods), Quantitative Reasoning (Portion Size &   Nutritional Profiling), and Safety-Critical Advisory (Disease-Specific Recommendations). We evaluate six   state-of-the-art VLMs, including gpt-5.1, gemini-3-flash, and qwen3-vl-8B. Our extensive experiments reveal a   startling "Semantic-Physical Gap": while models achieve near-human accuracy in naming dishes, they exhibit   catastrophic failure in mass estimation and frequently hallucinate benign advice for high-risk diabetic profiles. This   work establishes a rigorous standard for trustworthiness in autonomous agents deployed for public health. The code   and datasets are available in: https://anonymous.4open.science/r/OmniFood-Bench-7D0B
artigos.titulo_en: OmniFood-Bench: Evaluating VLMs for Nutrient Reasoning and Personalized Health Advice
artigos.titulo_pt: OmniFood-Bench: Avaliação de Modelos de Visão-Linguagem para Raciocínio Nutricional e Aconselhamento de Saúde Personalizado
artigos.resumo_executivo_en: OmniFood-Bench is a comprehensive benchmark evaluating vision-language models on critical health tasks, from ingredient identification to mass estimation and personalized medical recommendations. The study reveals a significant gap between model accuracy in naming dishes and catastrophic failure in mass estimation and high-risk patient advice, such as diabetic profiles. This work establishes rigorous standards for trustworthiness of autonomous agents in public health applications.
artigos.resumo_executivo_pt: OmniFood-Bench é um benchmark abrangente que avalia modelos de visão-linguagem em tarefas críticas de saúde, desde identificação de ingredientes até estimativa de massa e recomendações médicas personalizadas. O estudo revela uma lacuna significativa entre a precisão dos modelos em nomear pratos e seu desempenho catastrófico em estimativa de massa e aconselhamento para perfis de alto risco, como diabéticos. Este trabalho estabelece padrões rigorosos para confiabilidade de agentes autônomos em aplicações de saúde pública.
artigos.justificativa: A avaliação de modelos de visão-linguagem para aconselhamento nutricional e médico é crítica para aplicações de saúde, pois identifica falhas de segurança em sistemas autônomos que podem impactar diretamente a saúde pública e exigem conformidade regulatória rigorosa.

artigos.titulo: Detecting Ladder Logic Bombs in IEC 61131-3 PLC Programs using ESBMC-PLC+: A Formal Verification Approach with Trigger Synthesis
artigos.resumo: A Ladder Logic Bomb (LLB) is malicious control logic in a Programmable Logic Controller (PLC) program that lies dormant until a trigger activates a payload to manipulate actuators, forge sensor readings, or deny operator control. We observe that real malicious logic hides inside function-block bodies, which existing ladder-diagram verifiers drop from their intermediate representation (IR), making bombs invisible to provers. We present ESBMC-LLB, which uses ESBMC-PLC+ as its verification engine and adds a modeling layer that exposes function-block logic and recasts bomb detection as a formal verification problem: a scan-watchdog exposes non-termination payloads, and output wiring exposes actuator-forgery payloads as safety violations. k-induction gives an unbounded proof of bomb-absence across all scans, and the bounded model checker returns a counterexample that is the trigger - guarantees that signature, anomaly, and CFG-triage detectors lack. On the public Iacobelli 2024 dataset, ESBMC-LLB detects all 30 bombs and recovers every trigger; it also detects adaptive triggers (computed, opaque-arithmetic, multi-scan) that evade CFG-triage. We also report the first semantic model-checker evaluation on PLC-Defuser's SWaT corpus: our analog extension makes the full corpus parseable; on v1.0.0, it detects 149/150 bombs (99%) with zero false positives, recovering each trigger; on a later version with nonlinear non-termination bombs, detection drops to 49% as the SMT solver times out. We conclude that semantic model checking and CFG-triage are complementary - the former gives unbounded proofs, adaptive-trigger robustness, and handles Boolean/integer and linear analog logic; the latter leads to nonlinear analog non-termination, and we delineate where each wins.
artigos.titulo_en: Detecting Ladder Logic Bombs in IEC 61131-3 PLC Programs using ESBMC-PLC+: A Formal Verification Approach with Trigger Synthesis
artigos.titulo_pt: Detecção de Bombas de Lógica em Escada em Programas PLC IEC 61131-3 usando ESBMC-PLC+: Uma Abordagem de Verificação Formal com Síntese de Gatilhos
artigos.resumo_executivo_en: The work presents ESBMC-LLB, a formal verification tool that detects malicious logic bombs in Programmable Logic Controller (PLC) programs through trigger synthesis. The approach uses k-induction and bounded model checking to provide unbounded proofs of bomb absence and recover adaptive trigger signatures that evade conventional detection methods. On public datasets, the tool achieves 99% detection with zero false positives, demonstrating complementarity between semantic verification and control-flow analysis.
artigos.resumo_executivo_pt: O trabalho apresenta ESBMC-LLB, uma ferramenta de verificação formal que detecta bombas de lógica maliciosa em programas de Controladores Lógicos Programáveis (PLCs) através de síntese de gatilhos. A abordagem utiliza k-induction e model checking limitado para fornecer provas não-limitadas de ausência de bombas e recuperar assinaturas de gatilhos adaptativos que evitam detecção por métodos convencionais. Nos datasets públicos testados, a ferramenta alcança 99% de detecção com zero falsos positivos, demonstrando complementaridade entre verificação semântica e análise de fluxo de controle.
artigos.justificativa: A detecção de bombas lógicas em PLCs é crítica para segurança de infraestruturas críticas e sistemas industriais, onde lógica maliciosa dorminhoca pode causar danos físicos, manipulação de sensores e negação de controle operacional. A capacidade de recuperar gatilhos adaptativos e fornecer provas formais de ausência de bombas oferece garantias de segurança que métodos convencionais não conseguem.

artigos.titulo: When Synthetic Speech Is All You Have: Better Call GRPO
artigos.resumo: LLM-based ASR adapted to regulated domains such as banking is bottlenecked by privacy: real speech is costly and legally constrained to collect, making synthetic text-to-speech (TTS) an attractive substitute. Yet synthetic speech stays acoustically mismatched with real recordings, and work on this gap has stayed within supervised fine-tuning (SFT). We instead turn to reinforcement learning, and show that Group Relative Policy Optimization (GRPO) extracts far more from the same synthetic speech than SFT. Synthetic-only adaptation of the model with GRPO, a critic-free method rewarding low-WER hypotheses, reduces WER by 40\% relative to SFT (36.71\%$\to$22.09\%), and an SFT-then-GRPO combination pushes this further to 45\%. We trace the gain to behavior rather than representation: GRPO reduces insertion errors by improving stopping calibration and speech-to-text alignment by better anchoring attention to audio, leaving early-layer representations intact. When synthetic speech is the main resource, reinforcement learning should be preferred over supervised fine-tuning.
artigos.titulo_en: When Synthetic Speech Is All You Have: Better Call GRPO
artigos.titulo_pt: Quando Você Só Tem Fala Sintética: Melhor Chamar GRPO
artigos.resumo_executivo_en: The paper addresses adaptation of LLM-based automatic speech recognition (ASR) models for regulated domains like banking using synthetic speech from TTS when real data is costly and legally constrained. It proposes reinforcement learning, specifically Group Relative Policy Optimization (GRPO), which reduces word error rate (WER) by 40% compared to traditional supervised fine-tuning. The method improves model behavior through better stopping calibration and speech-to-text alignment, demonstrating that reinforcement learning outperforms supervised fine-tuning when only synthetic speech is available.
artigos.resumo_executivo_pt: O artigo aborda a adaptação de modelos de reconhecimento de fala (ASR) baseados em LLM para domínios regulados, como banking, utilizando fala sintética gerada por TTS quando dados reais são custosos e legalmente restritos. Propõe o uso de aprendizado por reforço, especificamente Group Relative Policy Optimization (GRPO), que reduz a taxa de erro de palavra (WER) em 40% em relação ao fine-tuning supervisionado tradicional. O método melhora o comportamento do modelo através de melhor calibração de parada e alinhamento entre fala e texto, demonstrando que aprendizado por reforço é superior ao fine-tuning supervisionado quando apenas fala sintética está disponível.
artigos.justificativa: A solução aborda um problema crítico em domínios regulados (banking) onde dados reais são custosos e legalmente restritos, oferecendo uma abordagem prática que reduz significativamente erros de reconhecimento de fala (40% de melhoria) utilizando apenas dados sintéticos, com aplicabilidade imediata em sistemas de produção.

artigos.titulo: Track2Map: Online Deformable SLAM with Motion-Aware Pose Optimization in Robotic Surgery
artigos.resumo: Gaussian splatting is the current state-of-the-art for dense, deformable 3D anatomy reconstruction in robot-assisted minimally invasive surgery (RAMIS); however, most pipelines are offline and depend on accurate camera trajectory priors (often from robotic kinematics), limiting applicability when priors are missing or noisy. To address these limitations, we propose Track2Map, an online 3D Gaussian Splatting pipeline that jointly optimizes camera trajectory and 3D deformable scene representation directly from surgical video. Track2Map is therefore capable of robust 3D reconstructions when camera trajectory priors are either absent or noisy, and due to its online nature it effectively works as a Simultaneous Localisation and Mapping (SLAM) method. To stabilize optimization in the presence of tissue motion and ambiguous visual cues, we introduce a track-anchored deformation initialization using dense 2D point tracks. Track statistics are further utilized to disentangle camera motion from scene deformation by detecting static camera periods and reducing drift during incremental mapping. Experiments on StereoMIS show improved reconstruction quality and camera trajectory against competing SLAM methods, as well as compared to non-SLAM methods that utilize camera trajectory priors. The code is available at https://track2map.github.io/.
artigos.titulo_en: Track2Map: Online Deformable SLAM with Motion-Aware Pose Optimization in Robotic Surgery
artigos.titulo_pt: Track2Map: SLAM Deformável Online com Otimização de Pose Consciente de Movimento em Cirurgia Robótica
artigos.resumo_executivo_en: Track2Map is an online 3D Gaussian Splatting pipeline that jointly optimizes camera trajectory and deformable scene representation directly from surgical videos, functioning as a robust SLAM method even without trajectory priors. The system leverages dense 2D point tracking for deformation initialization and track statistics to disambiguate camera motion from scene deformation. Experiments demonstrate superior reconstruction quality and camera trajectory compared to competing SLAM methods and approaches utilizing trajectory priors.
artigos.resumo_executivo_pt: Track2Map é um pipeline online de Gaussian Splatting 3D que otimiza conjuntamente a trajetória da câmera e a representação de cena deformável diretamente de vídeos cirúrgicos, funcionando como um método SLAM robusto mesmo sem priors de trajetória. O sistema utiliza rastreamento de pontos 2D densos para inicializar deformações e estatísticas de rastreamento para desambiguar movimento de câmera de deformação de cena. Experimentos demonstram qualidade de reconstrução superior e trajetória de câmera melhorada em comparação com métodos SLAM concorrentes e métodos que utilizam priors de trajetória.
artigos.justificativa: O trabalho aborda um desafio crítico em cirurgia robótica minimamente invasiva ao eliminar a dependência de priors de trajetória de câmera, viabilizando reconstrução 3D de anatomia deformável em tempo real sem informações de cinemática robótica, com aplicação direta em sistemas cirúrgicos assistidos por robô.

artigos.titulo: Beyond Backpropagation: Monte Carlo Method Can Train Deep Neural Networks
artigos.resumo: Backpropagation (BP) dominates deep learning training, but its reliance on gradients brings inherent troubles -- vanishing and exploding gradients. The pursuit of gradient-free methods has long been a goal in the field of artificial intelligence. This paper shows that indeed the simplest Monte Carlo algorithm implemented on a single GPU -- randomly mutate a parameter, keep it if the loss decreases, otherwise retry -- can practically train deep networks. This gradient-free method does not even need common techniques such as batch normalization or residual connections to directly train sufficiently deep networks. More remarkably, its flexibility extends to several nontrivial scenarios: it enables pure pruning training, supports discrete weights, accommodates unconventional transfer functions such as Gaussian, and reveals the substantial redundancy of deep networks. We have demonstrated its feasibility on deep networks with more than 20 layers, single-hidden-layer wide networks with up to 16,384 hidden neurons, and even a simple Transformer architecture trained on both image classification (MNIST) and character-level language modeling (Tiny Shakespeare). This simple gradient-free method may offer a complementary perspective for understanding the self-organization and learning mechanisms of neural networks, and also provides an alternative route for building physically inspired deep learning systems.
artigos.titulo_en: Beyond Backpropagation: Monte Carlo Method Can Train Deep Neural Networks
artigos.titulo_pt: Além da Retropropagação: Método de Monte Carlo Pode Treinar Redes Neurais Profundas
artigos.resumo_executivo_en: The paper demonstrates that a simple gradient-free Monte Carlo algorithm can practically train deep neural networks, avoiding vanishing and exploding gradient problems inherent to backpropagation. The method is flexible enough to support pure pruning, discrete weights, unconventional activation functions, and reveals substantial redundancy in deep networks. The approach was validated on networks with over 20 layers, wide networks with up to 16,384 hidden neurons, and even a simple Transformer architecture on image classification and language modeling tasks.
artigos.resumo_executivo_pt: O artigo demonstra que um algoritmo de Monte Carlo simples e sem gradientes pode treinar redes neurais profundas de forma prática, evitando problemas de gradientes que desaparecem ou explodem. O método é flexível o suficiente para suportar poda pura, pesos discretos, funções de transferência não convencionais e revela redundância substancial em redes profundas. A abordagem foi validada em redes com mais de 20 camadas, redes largas com até 16.384 neurônios ocultos e até uma arquitetura Transformer simples em tarefas de classificação de imagens e modelagem de linguagem.
artigos.justificativa: O método oferece uma alternativa viável ao treinamento tradicional com retropropagação, com potencial para simplificar a implementação de redes neurais e reduzir requisitos computacionais em cenários específicos, embora ainda necessite validação em aplicações de produção em larga escala.

artigos.titulo: DrugGen 2: A disease-aware language model for enhancing drug discovery
artigos.resumo: Current computational approaches for drug design typically focus on generating molecules conditioned on specific targets or general molecular properties, often neglecting the influence of disease context on target behavior and therapeutic outcomes. To address this gap, we introduce DrugGen-2, a novel generative model that designs small molecules conditioned on both disease ontology and target protein sequences. DrugGen-2 was developed by fine-tuning a pre-trained GPT-2 model on a curated dataset of approved drugs linked to their diseases and targets, using a two-step strategy of supervised fine-tuning followed by reinforcement learning via group relative policy optimization (GRPO). This process was guided by reward functions optimizing for chemical validity, novelty, diversity, and high predicted binding affinity. When evaluated on five protein targets relevant to diabetic nephropathy, DrugGen-2 significantly outperformed baseline models (DrugGPT and DrugGen). It demonstrated a superior capacity to generate unique molecules, exhibited greater structural similarity to approved drugs, and achieved improved predicted binding affinities across all targets. Molecular docking analyses further supported these findings, identifying candidate ligands with strong binding potential, including compounds with predicted affinities (-9.917, -9.485, and -9.367) exceeding those of reference drugs such as enalapril for angiotensin-converting enzyme (-8.283). By integrating disease-specific context into molecular generation, DrugGen-2 advances AI-assisted drug discovery, offering a powerful tool for de novo design and drug repurposing that accounts for the complex interplay between diseases and molecular targets.
artigos.titulo_en: DrugGen 2: A disease-aware language model for enhancing drug discovery
artigos.titulo_pt: DrugGen 2: Um modelo de linguagem consciente de doenças para aprimorar a descoberta de fármacos
artigos.resumo_executivo_en: DrugGen-2 is a generative model based on GPT-2 that designs small molecules conditioned on both disease ontology and target protein sequences, using supervised fine-tuning followed by reinforcement learning via GRPO. Evaluated on five protein targets relevant to diabetic nephropathy, the model significantly outperformed baseline models in generating unique molecules, achieving greater structural similarity to approved drugs and improved predicted binding affinities. By integrating disease-specific context into molecular generation, DrugGen-2 advances AI-assisted drug discovery as a powerful tool for de novo design and drug repurposing.
artigos.resumo_executivo_pt: DrugGen-2 é um modelo generativo baseado em GPT-2 que projeta moléculas pequenas condicionadas simultaneamente à ontologia de doenças e sequências de proteínas alvo, utilizando fine-tuning supervisionado seguido de aprendizado por reforço via GRPO. O modelo foi avaliado em cinco alvos proteicos relevantes para nefropatia diabética, superando significativamente modelos baseline em geração de moléculas únicas, similaridade estrutural com fármacos aprovados e afinidade de ligação predita. A integração de contexto específico de doenças no processo de geração molecular avança a descoberta de fármacos assistida por IA, oferecendo uma ferramenta poderosa para design de novo e reposicionamento de fármacos.
artigos.justificativa: A descoberta de fármacos é um processo extremamente custoso e demorado; DrugGen-2 oferece uma solução de IA generativa que pode acelerar significativamente o desenvolvimento de novos medicamentos e reposicionamento de fármacos, com potencial impacto comercial e terapêutico direto na indústria farmacêutica.

artigos.titulo: Game Theory Driven Multi-Agent Framework Mitigates Language Model Hallucination
artigos.resumo: The application of lightweight Large Language Models in rule-based scientific domains remains severely limited by their tendency to mimic linguistic patterns rather than reproduce axiomatic reasoning, causing frequent hallucinations. Here, we show that G-Frame, an adaptive multi-agent framework integrating Bayesian and team game principles, establishes an automated closed-loop for high-quality data synthesis and model training. By forcing the internalization of domain constraints through structured reasoning, we synthesized a specialized corpus of 363,045 chains-of-thought and 199,589 question-answer pairs. The resulting 7B model OmniChem achieves performance parity with GPT 4o mini on custom benchmarks and ChemBench while exhibiting a 79.46% reduction in hallucinations relative to its base architecture. We further demonstrate the advanced capabilities of OmniChem in molecular design and synthesis planning. This work establishes a scalable paradigm utilizing adaptive multi-agents to overcome inherent reasoning deficiencies, offering a feasible pathway for accelerating knowledge discovery in specialized scientific fields.
artigos.titulo_en: Game Theory Driven Multi-Agent Framework Mitigates Language Model Hallucination
artigos.titulo_pt: Framework Multi-Agente Orientado por Teoria dos Jogos Mitiga Alucinações em Modelos de Linguagem
artigos.resumo_executivo_en: The work presents G-Frame, an adaptive multi-agent framework integrating Bayesian and game theory principles to mitigate hallucinations in lightweight language models applied to rule-based scientific domains. Through automated high-quality data synthesis, OmniChem, a 7B parameter model, achieves performance parity with GPT-4o mini while reducing hallucinations by 79.46%. The paradigm establishes a scalable pathway for accelerating knowledge discovery in specialized scientific fields, with demonstrated applications in molecular design and synthesis planning.
artigos.resumo_executivo_pt: O trabalho apresenta G-Frame, um framework multi-agente adaptativo que integra princípios bayesianos e teoria dos jogos para mitigar alucinações em modelos de linguagem leves aplicados a domínios científicos baseados em regras. Através de síntese automatizada de dados de alta qualidade, foi desenvolvido OmniChem, um modelo de 7B parâmetros que alcança paridade de desempenho com GPT-4o mini enquanto reduz alucinações em 79,46%. O paradigma estabelece um caminho escalável para acelerar descoberta de conhecimento em campos científicos especializados, com aplicações demonstradas em design molecular e planejamento de síntese.
artigos.justificativa: A redução significativa de alucinações em modelos de linguagem leves (79,46%) com desempenho comparável a GPT-4o mini tem aplicação imediata em domínios científicos críticos como química e descoberta de fármacos, onde precisão é essencial. O framework oferece uma solução escalável e eficiente em custo para empresas que necessitam de LLMs especializados sem dependência de modelos proprietários de grande escala.

artigos.titulo: Swapping Faces, Saving Features: A Dual-Purpose Pipeline for Pedestrian Privacy in ITS
artigos.resumo: Large-scale and diverse datasets are needed to train AI models to take real-time decisions for autonomous vehicles (AVs), an intelligent transportation system (ITS) application. Pedestrian intention and trajectory prediction are critical models used in AVs, requiring datasets involving diverse pedestrian images. Unrestricted access to these datasets imposes serious security risks, like identity theft and pedestrian tracking. The challenge is to apply privacy preservation procedures while maintaining the image attributes needed to train the models. Existing privacy methods may preserve the pedestrian's privacy, but degrade the image usability, which hinders the models' effectiveness. This work's focus is to implement a five-stage pipeline to protect pedestrians' privacy through face swapping while keeping the essential facial attributes intact. It should be tailored to satisfy the privacy needs of the Egy-DRiVeS dataset. Moreover, Roop and Ghost-v2 face-swapping models are evaluated. Provenly, Roop outperforms Ghost-v2 in various aspects, as will be discussed. Consequently, Roop is the face-swapping model to be used in the pipeline to strike the balance between pedestrian privacy via identity concealment and data usability via facial attribute preservation.
artigos.titulo_en: Swapping Faces, Saving Features: A Dual-Purpose Pipeline for Pedestrian Privacy in ITS
artigos.titulo_pt: Trocando Rostos, Preservando Características: Um Pipeline Dual para Privacidade de Pedestres em ITS
artigos.resumo_executivo_en: This work presents a five-stage pipeline to protect pedestrian privacy in intelligent transportation systems through face swapping while preserving essential facial attributes for AI model training. The study evaluates face-swapping models (Roop and Ghost-v2), demonstrating that Roop achieves superior performance in balancing identity concealment with data usability preservation. The solution is specifically tailored for the Egy-DRiVeS dataset, addressing the critical privacy challenge in autonomous vehicle datasets.
artigos.resumo_executivo_pt: O trabalho apresenta um pipeline de cinco estágios para proteger a privacidade de pedestres em sistemas de transporte inteligente através de troca de rostos, mantendo os atributos faciais essenciais para treinamento de modelos de IA. O estudo avalia modelos de face-swapping (Roop e Ghost-v2), demonstrando que Roop oferece melhor desempenho ao equilibrar ocultação de identidade com preservação de usabilidade dos dados. A solução é especificamente adaptada para o dataset Egy-DRiVeS, abordando o desafio crítico de privacidade em datasets de veículos autônomos.
artigos.justificativa: A solução aborda um desafio crítico para a indústria de veículos autônomos: permitir o uso de datasets de pedestres em larga escala mantendo conformidade com regulamentações de privacidade (GDPR, LGPD), essencial para comercialização de sistemas ITS. O pipeline oferece um equilíbrio prático entre proteção de privacidade e qualidade de dados para treinamento de modelos de predição de intenção e trajetória de pedestres.

artigos.titulo: TRACE: A Two-Channel Robust Attribution Watermark via Complementary Embeddings for LLM-Agent Trajectories
artigos.resumo: LLM agents reach users through resellers, who may rebrand a developer's agent or substitute a cheaper model. When provenance is disputed, attribution rests on the trajectory log (the record of tool calls, observations, and executed actions, not the model's reasoning), which the reseller stores and processes to meter usage. A watermark must therefore survive an adversary with full read/write access to the very evidence it is detected from; existing agent watermarks do not, as their attribution is read straight off that log. We present TRACE, to our knowledge the first agent watermark that is distortion-free in its action choices, self-synchronizing under deletion, and unconditionally invariant under rewriting. Deletion desynchronizes a position-derived key and rewriting alters content, so a deletion-robust key must come from content and a rewrite-robust key from position, and no single key serves both. A trajectory, however, has room for two watermarks. TRACE superposes a selection channel that sets which action is chosen, keyed on local content with a distortion-free sampler, so the agent's distribution is provably unchanged and detection resynchronizes after deletions, and a tally channel that sets how many records each decision group holds, keyed on the log's skeleton alone, which no rewriting can touch. We prove this behavioral watermark's signal is bought with decision entropy, each decision paying at least half its entropy and deterministic decisions nothing, and that erasing both channels forces the reseller to corrupt the trajectories it resells. On ToolBench and ALFWorld, TRACE matches the unwatermarked agent's success rate while its selection channel reaches detection scores near z = 100 on long-horizon trajectories, stays detectable under 70% step deletion, and keeps a tally channel exactly unchanged under LLM rewriting of any strength.
artigos.titulo_en: TRACE: A Two-Channel Robust Attribution Watermark via Complementary Embeddings for LLM-Agent Trajectories
artigos.titulo_pt: TRACE: Uma Marca d'Água de Atribuição Robusta de Dois Canais via Embeddings Complementares para Trajetórias de Agentes LLM
artigos.resumo_executivo_en: TRACE is the first watermark for LLM agents that resists adversaries with full read/write access to trajectory logs, using two complementary channels: a selection channel keyed on local content and a tally channel keyed on log structure. The method maintains the agent's action distribution unchanged, remains detectable after 70% step deletion, and is invariant under rewriting of any strength. The approach proves that detecting both channels forces resellers to corrupt the trajectories they resell.
artigos.resumo_executivo_pt: TRACE é a primeira marca d'água para agentes LLM que resiste a adversários com acesso total de leitura/escrita aos logs de trajetória, utilizando dois canais complementares: um de seleção baseado em conteúdo local e outro de contagem baseado na estrutura do log. O método mantém a distribuição de ações do agente inalterada, permanece detectável mesmo após 70% de exclusão de passos e é invariante sob reescrita de qualquer força. A abordagem prova que a detecção de ambos os canais força o revendedor a corromper as trajetórias que revende.
artigos.justificativa: A proteção de propriedade intelectual de agentes LLM contra rebranding e substituição por modelos mais baratos é crítica para empresas que desenvolvem e distribuem agentes através de revendedores. TRACE oferece a primeira solução robusta para atribuição de proveniência em ambientes adversariais, protegendo receita e reputação da marca.

artigos.titulo: Prompt Compression via Activation Aggregation
artigos.resumo: Large language models process prompts by propagating activations through dozens of layers before generating a response. We ask whether the task-relevant information contained in an instruction prompt can be compressed into a single activation vector and re-injected into the model, replacing the original token sequence? We show this is achievable using a learned weighted sum of activations extracted at an intermediate layer and injected at an early layer of the target LLM. The compressed vector preserves task-relevant information, incurring an accuracy drop of under $2\%$ relative to full prompt processing. Beyond its practical implications, including reducing per-query computation for fixed instruction prompts without reprocessing the original token sequence, our analysis reveals structure in the activation space of LLMs: (i) mid-layer representations transfer meaningfully to early layers, suggesting a degree of cross-layer compatibility in how information is encoded; (ii) a single activation vector encodes a quantifiable and recoverable amount of semantic information; (iii) a weighted sum of activations is a robust representation compressor.
artigos.titulo_en: Prompt Compression via Activation Aggregation
artigos.titulo_pt: Compressão de Prompts via Agregação de Ativações
artigos.resumo_executivo_en: The work proposes a method to compress instruction prompts into a single activation vector, reducing processing requirements in large language models without significant accuracy loss. The technique uses a weighted sum of activations extracted from intermediate layers and re-injected into early layers, preserving task-relevant information with accuracy drop below 2%. The method reveals important structures in the activation space of LLMs, including cross-layer compatibility and the ability of a single activation vector to encode recoverable semantic information.
artigos.resumo_executivo_pt: O trabalho propõe um método para comprimir prompts de instruções em um único vetor de ativação, reduzindo o processamento necessário em modelos de linguagem grandes sem perda significativa de precisão. A técnica utiliza uma soma ponderada de ativações extraídas de camadas intermediárias e reinjetadas em camadas iniciais, preservando informações relevantes para a tarefa com queda de acurácia inferior a 2%. O método revela estruturas importantes no espaço de ativações de LLMs, incluindo compatibilidade entre camadas e a capacidade de um único vetor de ativação codificar informações semânticas recuperáveis.
artigos.justificativa: A compressão de prompts reduz significativamente o custo computacional por query para instruções fixas, permitindo otimização de latência e eficiência energética em aplicações de LLMs em produção, com impacto direto na redução de custos operacionais e escalabilidade de serviços.
